prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament lessons for this feast are proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

(Tone 8): Descending to the human race, our Savior,* eight days old according to His Mother and without beginning according to the Father,* allowed Himself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes* and did not refuse to accept bodily circumcision.* To Him, O faithful, let us sing: You are our God, have mercy on us.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Descending to the human race, our Savior,* eight days old according to His Mother and without beginning according to the Father,* allowed Himself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes* and did not refuse to accept bodily circumcision.* To Him, O faithful, let us sing: You are our God, have mercy on us.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities,* who could stand, O Lord? But with You there is forgiveness.

The all-merciful God was not ashamed to accept the sign of lawful adoption,* but gave Himself as a pattern and example to all for salvation.* For behold, the Lawgiver fulfills the law and the prophecies concerning Him.* O Lord, who hold all things in Your palm* yet allow Yourself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes — glory to You!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The all-merciful God was not ashamed to accept the sign of lawful adoption,* but gave Himself as a pattern and example to all for salvation.* For behold, the Lawgiver fulfills the law and the prophecies concerning Him.* O Lord, who hold all things in Your palm* yet allow Yourself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes — glory to You!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You were called): Your name, O Father Basil, is royal,* and your priesthood is royal,* for you shepherded Christ's holy people with piety and knowledge.* Therefore the King of kings and Lord of all crowned you with a royal crown,* He who is consubstantial with the Father,* the co-eternal and co-beginningless Son.* Entreat Him to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Your name, O Father Basil, is royal,* and your priesthood is royal,* for you shepherded Christ's holy people with piety and knowledge.* Therefore the King of kings and Lord of all crowned you with a royal crown,* He who is consubstantial with the Father,* the co-eternal and co-beginningless Son.* Entreat Him to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Clothed in the hierarchal vestment, O Basil,* you joyfully preached the Gospel of the Kingdom,* transmitting orthodox teaching to the Church.* Today we too, enlightened by it, confess and glorify one Godhead,* undivided in three Persons:* the Father Almighty, the Only-begotten Word of God, and the Divine Spirit.* Entreat Him to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O Father Basil!* Living among men on earth as one without a body,* you dwelt and lived together with the heavenly choirs,* striving to imitate them in the flesh* by the most pure radiance of your life.* Therefore entreat Christ our God* to save from dangers and the darkness of ignorance those* who delight in your divinely inspired teachings,* and to enlighten our souls.

Glory (Tone 8): You were a lover of wisdom, O Saint,* and wisely above all things you valued union with God in your life,* constantly remembering death and conquering bodily passions by temperance.* By the teaching of God's law you preserved the unblemished dignity of the soul,* and by the wealth of virtues you subjected to the spirit all carnal reasoning.* Therefore you despised the body, the world, and its ruler.* Standing before Christ, intercede for our souls and obtain great mercy.

Now and ever: Descending to the human race, our Savior,* eight days old according to His Mother and without beginning according to the Father,* allowed Himself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes* and did not refuse to accept bodily circumcision.* To Him, O faithful, let us sing: You are our God, have mercy on us.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 1): O divine and sacred bee of the Church of Christ, all-blessed Basil!* Armed with the sting of divine zeal, you overcame the vile blasphemy of heresies* and gave to the souls of the faithful the sweet treasure of piety.* Therefore now, dwelling in the incorruptible mansions of divine paradise, remember us also* as you stand before the consubstantial Trinity.
Verse: My mouth shall speak wisdom,* and the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.

You gathered the virtues of all the saints, O Father Basil:* the meekness of Moses, the zeal of Elijah,* the confession of Peter, the theology of John;* and like Paul, you unceasingly declared:* Who is weak, and I am not weak?* Who is led into temptation, and I do not burn?* Therefore, dwelling with them,* pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom,* and his tongue speaks what is right.

(Tone 2): O divinely manifested Basil!* Having studied the nature of things and convinced yourself of their transience,* you found the one unchanging — the supreme Creator of the universe,* and inclined rather to Him, rejecting the allure of the passing.* Therefore pray that we too may be filled with the love of God.

Glory (Tone 6): Having received from heaven the grace of wonder-working* and having put to shame by your teachings the deception of the devil,* you, all-blessed Basil, are the glory and confirmation of hierarchs* and the model of teaching for all the venerable.* Having boldness before God,* entreat Him that our souls may be saved.

Now and ever (Tone 8): The all-merciful God was not ashamed to accept the sign of lawful adoption,* but gave Himself as a pattern and example to all for salvation.* For behold, the Lawgiver fulfills the law and the prophecies concerning Him.* O Lord, who hold all things in Your palm* yet allow Yourself to be wrapped in swaddling clothes — glory to You!

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 1): On the eighth day You were circumcised, O Lord, and the heavenly ranks of angels trembled in amazement. You gave the law to Moses on Sinai, and You Who are the Lord of all willed to bear the law: You accepted circumcision, O our Savior, for the salvation of our souls.

Glory: (Tone 1): You arose from the dead on the third day, O Christ, and raised up with You the fallen race of Adam and our mother Eve. Death, your pride is shattered! Hades, you are conquered! For He Who assumed flesh and granted resurrection to the world is Christ our God.

Now and ever: (Tone 1): What shall we call you, O Full of Grace? Heaven, for you caused the Sun of righteousness to shine forth. Paradise, for you brought forth the flower of incorruption. Virgin, for you remained pure. Pure Mother, for you held in your holy embraces the Son, the God of all. Intercede with Him that our souls may be saved.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:

May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother; of our holy Father Basil the Great, Archbishop of Caesarea; and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Verse: I lift up my eyes to the hills; from where does my help come?

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): Let us piously sing* pre-festal songs* of the radiant baptism of our God!* For He, as a man,* desires to come in the flesh to His Forerunner* and ask for saving baptism,* for the edification of all* who are sacredly enlightened by faith* and are partakers of the Holy Spirit.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Christ appears,* God is manifestly revealed,* as David once wrote,* and goes to His servant to ask for baptism.* O Jordan River, be filled with gladness!* Earth and sea,* mountains and hills,* and human hearts, rejoice now,* receiving the spiritual light.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

How can the river's waters receive You,* O almighty Lord,* who are, as it is written, a river of peace* and a torrent of joy?* You who come forth naked,* yet cover the heavens with clouds;* You who reveal* every hostile evil* and clothe mortals in incorruption?
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 6, Podoben: Having placed all hope): Wholly sanctified,* you showed yourself a God-bearer,* holy anointed one of God,* and were filled with the Holy Spirit.* Illumined by the divine lights,* as a true and most glorious hierarch,* you always entered with radiance into the Holy of Holies* and partook of the grace of the holy Mysteries.* Pray with boldness for our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Your life shone with the rays of virtue,* and you enlightened the faithful,* dispelling the darkness of deception.* You appeared as a truly luminous sun, O most blessed Sylvester,* who now dwell where the never-setting sun shines,* and by the grace of the Holy Spirit became a son of the day.* Therefore, honorably celebrating your holy and radiant memory,* we glorify you with love, O ever-memorable one.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Your mind, O wise in God, was directed toward God,* adorned by faith and brightly deified.* In your mortal and corruptible body,* you, O wise one, attained incorruption,* acquired the radiance of the bodiless ones,* and rising above the passions,* adorned yourself with purity, O Sylvester, Father and wise hierarch,* glorious luminary and intercessor for those* who with love honor your memory.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): Christ, the Truth, comes to the Jordan to be baptized by John,* and John says to Him:* I need to be baptized by You, and You come to me?* I dare not, like hay, touch fire!* Sanctify me, O Master, by Your divine manifestation.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 1): Radiant was Your past feast, O Savior,* but more radiant is the one that approaches.* The former was announced by an angel, the latter prepared by the Forerunner;* at the previous one, blood was shed, for childless Bethlehem wept,* but at this one the divine waters appear* — a font for many children.* Then a star proclaimed You to the wise men,* but now the Father has revealed You to the world.* O Lord, who became incarnate and come again openly — glory to You!
Verse: Therefore I remember You in the land of Jordan* and of Hermon.

(Tone 2): Behold, Jesus is purified in the Jordan,* or rather, He washes away our sins.* For He truly comes to be baptized,* desiring to wash away the condemnation of Adam,* and says to John: Come, O Baptizer,* serve the institution of this wondrous mystery!* Come, quickly stretch out your hand and touch the head of Him* who crushed the head of the serpent* and opened paradise, closed by sin,* when the serpent cunningly persuaded us to taste of the forbidden tree.
Verse: The waters saw You, O God;* the waters saw You and were afraid.

(Tone 3): Radiant was the past feast,* but more glorious is this present day.* Then the wise men worshipped the Savior,* but now a servant baptizes the Master.* There the shepherds, seeing Him, played and marveled,* but here the voice of the Father proclaimed the Only-begotten Son.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 3): Let all the earth rejoice today with the prophets, and let the mountains be glad!* Turn back — as it is written — O Jordan, your course,* and be afraid before the face of the Lord;* for this is God, who became man,* and came to cleanse all people from the sin of Adam.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 7)
O God, You are my defender, and Your mercy shall come to meet me.
Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8): Seeing how the words of your God-inspired tongue are fulfilled,* I glorify you now, O Prophet, marveling at their truth:* for with a resounding voice you encompassed the world,* proclaiming the salvific coming of the Lord.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

To the angels, through your angel-like life on earth, you were called,* and with the name "Angel" you were most harmoniously enriched,* having united the beauty of soul with the beauty of body,* O all-blessed Malachi.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, I bring to You Your prophet as an intercessor and defender.* By his prayers, O Merciful One, and by his intercessions,* loose the sins of my soul, bound by despair,* as the Compassionate One who possesses essential and natural goodness.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8, Podoben: O most glorious wonder): Seeing the fog of deception reaching its peak,* you fled and withdrew,* O passion-bearer Gordius,* waiting upon God;* and having tested yourself with many labors* and tempered yourself with fasting,* you, as an athlete who feels his strength,* of your own accord* went forth to the adversary.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

With firm confidence* you came to the multitude of enemies,* O all-glorious Gordius,* counting as nothing the spectacle of the lawless.* Standing in their midst* as a self-willed preacher,* you proclaimed with a great voice Christ the Creator and Lord,* co-beginningless with the Father and the Holy Spirit,* equal in honor and co-eternal.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Your head was cut off by the sword,* yet you openly passed from life to life,* from death to immortality,* and from corruption to incorruption,* not waiting for natural death* but voluntarily being led to die.* O your struggles, O passion-bearer Gordius!* Through them you magnified Christ, O martyr,* the only Compassionate One.

Glory, now and ever, of the Pre-feast (Tone 4): O most wondrous miracle!* He who baptizes with the Holy Spirit and with fire* comes to the Jordan to be baptized by John:* not God naked, nor a simple man,* but in two natures one and the same Only-begotten Son,* who as man seeks baptism from a mortal* and as God takes away the sin of the world* and grants to all great mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): Behold, the Enlightenment of the faithful,* behold, our Purification* desires to enter the river's streams* to wash away the filth of human evil* and to restore us who are broken.
Verse: Therefore I remember You* from the land of Jordan and of Hermon.
When Your Forerunner saw You, O Lord,* coming and asking to be baptized by him,* he cried out in fear:* My God, my Creator,* how shall I baptize You, the undefiled?
Verse: The waters saw You, O God;* the waters saw You and were afraid.

Let us come together, O faithful, and gaze in contemplation* at the streams of the Jordan,* that we may clearly behold a great wonder:* for the Creator of all has appeared visible* and now comes to be baptized.
Glory, now and ever (Tone 5): To the Jordan River Christ our God* comes for baptism,* desiring to purify us from our iniquities by His manifestation,* as the only Good One and Lover of mankind.

Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.

More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 5): By Your precious Cross, O Christ,* You put the devil to shame,* and by Your Resurrection You quenched the sting of sin* and saved us from the gates of death.* We glorify You, O Only-begotten.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

He who gives resurrection to the human race* was led like a lamb to the slaughter.* The princes of Hades trembled before Him, and the gates of sorrow were opened,* for the King of glory — Christ — entered, saying to the prisoners: Come forth!* — and to those in darkness: Be revealed!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

Great is the wonder! The Creator of the invisible* suffered visibly because of His love for mankind* and rose as the Immortal One.* Come then, O tribes of the nations, and worship Him,* for by His mercy we have been delivered from error* and have learned to glorify the one God in three Persons.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

We offer our evening worship to You, the never-setting Light,* who at the end of the ages, through the body as in a mirror,* visibly shone upon the world and descended even to Hades;* there You dispelled the stagnant darkness,* and the radiance of the Resurrection shone upon the nations.* O Light-giving Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): Let us piously sing* pre-festal songs* of the radiant baptism of our God!* For He, as a man,* desires to come in the flesh to His Forerunner* and ask for saving baptism,* for the edification of all* who are sacredly enlightened by faith* and are partakers of the Holy Spirit.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Christ appears,* God is manifestly revealed,* as David once wrote,* and goes to His servant to ask for baptism.* O Jordan River, be filled with gladness!* Earth and sea,* mountains and hills,* and human hearts, rejoice now,* receiving the spiritual light.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

How can the river's waters receive You,* O almighty Lord,* who are — as it is written — a river of peace* and a torrent of joy?* You who come forth naked,* yet cover the heavens with clouds;* You who reveal* every hostile evil* and clothe mortals in incorruption?
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): As eyewitnesses and servants* of the incarnate Word,* you are glorified, O wise Disciples.* Like bright lightning* you appeared to the world,* and as spiritual mountains you pour forth delight;* as inexhaustible rivers of paradise you have spread abroad* and with divine waters you nourish* the assemblies of the nations.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

As sparkling rays* of spiritual radiance,* having become servants of Christ's mysteries,* you were sent into all the world* to generously distribute wonder-working.* You are ministers of Christ's mysteries,* divinely inscribed tablets of God's grace* and of the God-given law,* most rich administrators of the holy sacraments.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

The fishing rod,* by God-wise teachings* and outlines of dogmas,* crossed out the arrogance* and eloquence of the philosophers,* clearly setting forth the saving mysteries of the Gospels,* communion in eternal nourishment,* angelic delight,* and everlasting glory.

Glory (Tone 6): Christ, the Truth, comes to the Jordan to be baptized by John,* and John says to Him:* I need to be baptized by You, and You come to me?* I dare not, like hay, touch fire!* Sanctify me, O Master, by Your divine manifestation.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 5): Once, in the Red Sea,* a figure of the immaculate Bride was inscribed.* There Moses was the divider of the water,* and here Gabriel is the minister of the miracle.* Then Israel passed through the deep as on dry land,* but now the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.* The sea, after the passage of Israel, remained impassable,* and the Immaculate One, after bearing Emmanuel, remained incorrupt.* O eternal and pre-eternal God,* who appeared as man — have mercy on us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5): We magnify You, O Savior Christ, who accepted the flesh and were not separated from heaven,* with voices and songs.* For You accepted the Cross and death for our race as the Lord who loves mankind,* and having overthrown the gates of Hades, You rose on the third day,* saving our souls.
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

From Your pierced side, O Giver of life,* You poured forth streams of life and salvation.* Having accepted bodily death, You granted us immortality;* having dwelt in the tomb, as God You delivered us,* raising us up together with Yourself.* Therefore we cry out to You:* O Lord who loves mankind — glory to You!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Wondrous is Your crucifixion, O Lover of mankind, and Your descent into Hades!* For You captured it and raised up with Yourself the former prisoners;* as God, having opened paradise, You made them worthy to attain it.* Therefore grant also to us who glorify Your Resurrection on the third day* the cleansing of sins, and deign to make us inhabitants of paradise,* as the only Merciful One.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
O You, Lover of mankind, who for our sake accepted bodily suffering* and rose on the third day from the dead:* heal our bodily passions,* raise us from our grave offenses, and save us!

Glory, now and ever (Tone 3): Let all the earth rejoice today with the prophets, and let the mountains be glad!* Turn back — as it is written — O Jordan, your course,* and be afraid before the face of the Lord;* for this is God, who became man,* and came to cleanse all people from the sin of Adam.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 5): Co-eternal Word of the Father and the Spirit, born of a virgin for our salvation: let us the faithful praise and worship You, for You willed to go to the Cross in the flesh and to endure death, and to raise the dead by Your glorious resurrection.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.

Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord.
Verse: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): Let us praise Theonas and Theopemptus,* the wise ones!* For obeying the dogmas of Christ,* they radiantly put an end* to every impious worship* and served holily, reverently, and piously,* fearlessly confessing before the faces of torturers* the one Lord and God,* and were crowned from on high.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Having abandoned the lower allurements of life* and the pleasures of indulgence* and the blooming glory as transient, O all-praised ones,* you clung to Christ,* inflamed by the charm of His beauty,* and offered yourselves like sweet-scented roses,* and with the crown of the imperishable Kingdom* you were divinely crowned!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

You who hated the world* appeared as ones above the world,* gathered in the Church of the firstborn,* and with the angels sing* an imperishable hymn,* standing before the face of God,* having cast off the foul deception of idols* — you who martyr-like* put the frenzy of torturers to shame.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): Truly desiring good glory of the Fathers,* you desired the good and imperishable glory;* therefore, withdrawing from pleasures,* you gave your body to every labor.* And now for your labors you receive the reward,* reigning with Christ.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

He who permitted the cunning Tempter to torment Job,* desiring to test you also as gold,* left your body* to endure the violence of the Evil One.* Therefore, having shamed the tempter by your endurance of afflictions,* you were crowned with the crown of victory.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having desired the blooming beauty of the Bridegroom — Christ,* and longing to become His bride,* through the labors of asceticism* you adorned yourself with every kind of virtue;* therefore now you reign with Him* in His bridal chamber.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): Prepare yourself, O Jordan River:* for behold, Christ God comes to be baptized by John,* to crush the invisible heads of the serpents by His Divinity in your waters.* Rejoice, O wilderness of the Jordan!* Mountains, dance with gladness:* for eternal Life comes to call Adam back again.* O voice of one crying in the wilderness,* cry out, O John the Forerunner:* Prepare the way of the Lord* and make His paths straight!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): O earth and all that is above the earth,* dance and rejoice:* the Torrent of delight plunges into the river,* drying up the outpouring of evil* and pouring forth divine forgiveness.
Verse: Therefore I remember You* from the land of Jordan and of Hermon.
Jesus, the radiant Giver of light,* not needing baptism Himself,* comes in the flesh to the streams of the Jordan,* desiring to enlighten those in darkness.* Let us meet Him eagerly with faith.
Verse: The waters saw You, O God;* the waters saw You and were afraid.

Bearing the form of a servant,* You come forth, O Christ, to be baptized by a servant* in the streams of the Jordan,* delivering us from the slavery of ancient sin* and sanctifying and enlightening us.

Glory, now and ever: Let the wilderness of the Jordan rejoice* and blossom like a lily,* for the voice of one crying has been heard in it:* Prepare the way of the Lord!* For He who set the mountains upright* and measured the valleys,* who fills all things as God,* is baptized by a servant.* He begins to impoverish Himself, the Bestower of rich gifts.* Eve heard: In sorrow shall you bear children.* But now the Virgin has heard: Rejoice, O Full of Grace,* the Lord is with you,* who has great mercy!

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

(Tone 2): Seeing our Illuminator,* who enlightens every person,* coming to be baptized,* the Forerunner rejoices in soul, and with trembling hand points to Him and says to the people:* Behold the One who saves Israel and delivers us from corruption.* O sinless Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Seeing our Illuminator,* who enlightens every person,* coming to be baptized,* the Forerunner rejoices in soul, and with trembling hand points to Him and says to the people:* Behold the One who saves Israel and delivers us from corruption.* O sinless Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities,* who could stand, O Lord? But with You there is forgiveness.

The angelic powers were astonished,* seeing how our Savior accepts baptism from a servant,* while the Holy Spirit descends to bear Him witness,* and the voice of the Father is heard from heaven:* The One whom the Forerunner baptizes with his hand* is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.* O Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The angelic powers were astonished,* seeing how our Savior accepts baptism from a servant,* while the Holy Spirit descends to bear Him witness,* and the voice of the Father is heard from heaven:* The One whom the Forerunner baptizes with his hand* is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.* O Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The stream of the Jordan received You, the Source;* the Comforter descended in the form of a dove.* He bows His head, who bowed the heavens;* and the clay cries out to the Creator:* Why do You command me to do what is above me?* I need Your baptism.* O sinless Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having resolved to save the human race that had gone astray,* You did not refuse to take on the form of a servant.* For it was fitting for You, the Lord and God,* to accept for our sake what is ours.* Having accepted bodily baptism, O Savior,* You made us worthy of forgiveness.* Therefore we cry out to You: O Christ our God, Benefactor — glory to You!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Today Christ comes to the Jordan to be baptized.* Today John touches the head of the Master.* The heavenly powers were astonished, beholding the most glorious mystery:* the sea saw it and fled, and the Jordan, seeing it, turned back.* And we, having been enlightened, proclaim:* Glory to God who has appeared and become visible on earth* and has illumined the world.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You, O Lord, resolved to accomplish all that You ordained from eternity.* From all creation You received service for Your Mystery:* from the angels — Gabriel; from men — the Virgin;* from the heavens — the star; and from the Jordan — the water,* in which You destroyed the iniquities of mankind.* O Lord — glory to You!

Glory, now and ever (Tone 2): You bowed Your head before the Forerunner and crushed the heads of the serpents.* Entering the water, You enlightened all* who glorify You, O Savior, Illuminator of our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 1): When You were baptized in the Jordan, O Lord, the worship of the Trinity was revealed; for the voice of the Father bore witness to You, calling You His beloved Son, and the Spirit in the form of a dove confirmed the certainty of His word. O Christ our God, who appeared and enlightened the world, glory to You.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.

More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 1)
Your mercy, O Lord, shall be with me all the days of my life.
Verse: The Lord is my Shepherd; I shall not want.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): Seeing our Illuminator,* who enlightens every person,* coming to be baptized,* the Forerunner, beholding Him, rejoices in soul and trembles with his hand,* points to Him and says to the people:* Behold the Redeemer of Israel,* who delivers us from corruption!* O sinless Christ our God, glory to You!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Our Redeemer,* who is baptized by a servant* and witnessed to by the coming of the Spirit,* the angelic hosts, beholding, were astonished.* And a voice came from heaven from the Father:* He upon whom the Forerunner lays his hand* is My beloved Son, in whom is My good pleasure!* O Christ our God, glory to You!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The Jordan's streams* received You, the Fountain,* and the Comforter descended in the form of a dove.* He bows His head who bowed the heavens.* The clay cries out and calls to the Potter:* Why do You command me what is above me?* I need baptism from You!* O sinless Christ our God, glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): The Forerunner, seeing* that You, O Christ, come to him* and ask for baptism,* cried out with trembling:* Why do You command me to accomplish what is beyond my strength,* O Almighty Lord? How shall I touch You with my hand,* who hold all things in Your hand?* Rather, baptize me,* Your servant!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

I have appeared as a complete Man now,* and have become accessible to you,* though by nature I am inaccessible.* Of My own will I became poor, though I am rich,* in order to enrich the impoverished* with incorruption and deliverance.* Approach, baptize* the One who is subject not to corruption* and who delivers the world from corruption!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

I am constrained on every side,* and where to flee I am at a loss,* says the Forerunner to the Creator.* You are a life-giving stream!* How shall the river's currents receive You, O Merciful One,* as You enter them, pouring out salvation* for those who honor* Your holy,* O Word, manifestation?

Glory (Tone 7): O lantern in the flesh, O Forerunner of the Savior, shoot of the barren one, friend of Him who is from the Virgin, before whom you bowed in the womb, and whom you baptized in the streams of the Jordan — entreat Him, we beg you, O Prophet, that we may escape the future storms and tempests.

Now and ever, of the Feast: God the Word appeared in the flesh to the human race,* standing in the Jordan to be baptized.* And the Forerunner said to Him:* How shall I stretch out my hand* and touch the crown of Him who holds all things?* Even if You are a Child of Mary,* I know You as the pre-eternal God.* You walk upon the earth, yet the Seraphim sing to You,* and I, a servant, have not learned to baptize the Master!* O incomprehensible Lord, glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4, Podoben: You gave as a sign): When John the Forerunner saw* that You, O Master, were coming to him,* he was struck with fear and cried out* as a prudent servant:* What is this humiliation, O Savior?* What is this poverty in which You are clothed?* You, O Merciful One, exalted the humbled race by the wealth of Your goodness,* as one clothed in our nature!
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.

Come to Me,* today I accomplish a mystery,* the Savior of all replied to the Forerunner.* Serve with fear and do not be alarmed.* For, renewing Adam, ruined by sin,* I, who am undefiled by nature,* am baptized as a man* in the Jordan's waters,* into which, as you see, I have entered.
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?

Who among those born on earth* has ever seen the Sun being purified?* said John in reply.* And that He who clothes the heavens with clouds should strip Himself bare,* and He who creates springs and rivers* should enter the waters?* I marvel at Your ineffable Providence, O Master!* Do not burden Your servant* with fearful commands!

Glory: As a zealot of the Spirit* and a swallow of God-speaking grace,* you clearly illumined the Providence of the King,* who for the rebirth of mankind* shone light from the Pure One upon the race of mortals,* O Forerunner.* You put an end to the custom of putting on mournful faces,* and directed the hearts of those baptized in repentance* to receive life everlasting,* O blessed and divinely inspired one.

Now and ever: Come, let us imitate the wise virgins;* come, let us meet the appearance of the Master,* for He has come, like a Bridegroom, to John.* The Jordan, seeing You, was afraid and stood still.* John cried out:* I dare not touch the head of the Immortal One!* The Spirit descended in the form of a dove to sanctify the waters,* and a voice resounded from heaven:* This is My Son, who has come into the world to save the human race!* O Lord, glory to You!

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 5)
O God, by Your name save me, and by Your power defend me.
Verse: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8, Podoben: O most glorious wonder): O venerable God-wise Fathers!* You give light to the blind,* healing to the sick,* health to the lame,* mercifully inclining toward those* who come with faith to your temple* and ask for your help.* Glorifying you, we have all found in you* faithful defenders and guides.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O venerable and all-honorable Fathers!* By your virtues you mounted the chariot* and ascended to God,* receiving the honor of victors.* To us, O all-honorable Fathers, you left as a mantle your bodies,* placed in tombs,* to give healings* and drive away evil spirits;* therefore we glorify you, O most glorious ones.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

You have been numbered among the choirs of the fasters, O Blessed Ones,* for you adorned yourselves with the life of fasting,* and now you live, rejoicing,* in the heavenly dwellings* where the angels celebrate.* Truly sanctified by the divine light,* remember those* who with love glorify you on earth* and celebrate your holy feast.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): O sacredly manifested and divinely inspired Emilian!* In you we recognize the foundation of righteousness,* the confirmation of faith, a distinguished teacher,* an aid in piety and a dwelling of purity,* a chosen haven,* a fragrance of the Spirit,* a great treasury of teachings,* and a pillar of the Church of Christ.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

We extol you* as one enthroned with the Apostles,* equal in rank to the passion-bearers,* a fellow-zealot of the fasters, the seal of teachers,* a divine image and seer of Christ's mysteries,* O all-praised God-bearer,* and a God-flowing river of knowledge* that drowns the lawless and the blasphemers,* O wise Emilian.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You increased the talent of wisdom* and were counted worthy of the joy* of your Lord, O all-praised one.* Adorned with the grace* of divine radiance,* shining as a wise spiritual star,* you now stand at the life-giving right hand* and from there receive your illumination,* O glorious Emilian.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 2): Today the Creator of heaven and earth comes in the flesh to the Jordan,* and the Sinless One asks for baptism* to cleanse the world from the deception of the enemy.* The Lord of all is baptized by a servant,* and by the water grants purification to the human race.* To Him let us cry out: O God who has appeared — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): O Lord, though You, the Sinless One, deigned to be baptized in the flesh,* desiring to deliver the world from the bondage of sin,* we know You as God and glorify You.* For by being baptized, You freed our souls from the bondage of the devil.
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.

O incarnate Lord!* When You came to the waters of the Jordan* to sanctify the water by Your glorious baptism,* You deigned to bow Your head before John* and be baptized by a servant, to save our souls.
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?

O Lord, to deliver from deception the world that You created,* You deigned to take flesh from the immaculate Virgin,* and as the Lover of mankind, You delivered Adam from corruption;* and being baptized, You granted illumination to our souls.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 4): Come, let us imitate the wise virgins!* Come, let us meet the Master who has appeared,* for He has come like a Bridegroom to John!* The Jordan, seeing You, was afraid;* and John cried out: I dare not touch the head of the Immortal One!* The Spirit descended in the form of a dove to sanctify the waters,* and a voice from heaven resounded: This is My Son* who has come into the world to save the human race!* O Lord — glory to You!

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8): O Lord, though as a man You stood before John in the Jordan,* yet sitting with the Father, You did not leave the throne.* Being baptized for our sake, You delivered the world from the bondage of sin,* as the Merciful and Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, though as a man You descended into the Jordan's waters,* the Spirit, descending from above, bore witness,* and the Father's voice called You Son.* Therefore, appear and grant incorruption to our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, unchanging before the ages, You were born of the Father,* and in the latter times You came, assuming the form of a servant;* and as Creator, You renewed Your image,* for being baptized, You granted incorruption to our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): As in the Psalm of David, from the pit of suffering* and from the miry clay,* Christ led you out of the deception of idols, O martyr;* for He set your feet upon the rock* of His knowledge mystically.* Entreat Him that we may be saved.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Neither your wife's entreaties,* nor the love of children, nor your rank,* nor the abundance of possessions and estates* shook the firmness of your soul* from the true faith in Christ,* O all-blessed Polyeuctus.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Even before the grace of your suffering, O blessed one,* you truly adorned yourself with works of righteousness;* therefore afterward you were counted worthy* to become a faithful witness of Christ,* having been baptized in your own blood* in the likeness of His death.

Glory (Tone 1): The angelic hosts today rejoice* at the memory of the martyr Polyeuctus,* and the human race faithfully celebrates* and joyfully cries out:* Rejoice, O all-praised one, who overcame the cunning snares of Belial* and was crowned with the crown of victory by Christ!* Rejoice, O soldier of the great King and our Savior,* who destroyed the altars of idols!* Rejoice, O adornment of martyrs!* Pray that all who with faith celebrate* your eternally venerable memory* may be delivered from every misfortune.

Now and ever (Tone 2): Today the Creator of heaven and earth comes in the flesh to the Jordan,* and the Sinless One asks for baptism* to cleanse the world from the deception of the enemy.* The Lord of all is baptized by a servant,* and by the water grants purification to the human race.* To Him let us cry out: O God who has appeared — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): You stood upon the waters,* O incomprehensible One;* You bowed Your head before the Forerunner;* having sanctified the world, You redeemed it from slavery* by Your baptism, O Sinless One.
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.
From the heights on high,* the Father and the Spirit bore witness* that You are the beloved Son, O Christ;* by this the mystery of the Trinity was made known* when You were baptized in the Jordan.
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
You sanctified, O Savior,* all the streams of the Jordan* and the nature of the waters, as God.* Therefore the human race glorifies* and hymns Your manifestation.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 4): He who wraps Himself in light as in a garment* deigned for our sake to become like us.* He is clothed today in the streams of the Jordan,* not Himself needing them for purification,* but preparing in Himself our rebirth.* O wonder!* Without fire He refines,* and rebuilds without destruction,* and saves those who are enlightened in Him,* Christ God and Savior of our souls.

Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 5): O venerable Father, holy Gregory, breath filled with the Comforter and most radiant voice of piety, most luminous lantern of the Divine Radiance, preacher of truth, foundation of theology, wellspring of lofty dogmas, stream of honey-flowing instructions, divinely speaking flute that delights the minds of the faithful with God-written songs! Entreat Christ, pray to Christ, O all-wise one, that He who renewed the world in the streams of the Jordan may save our souls.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O venerable Father, all-holy Gregory, axe that hews the strivings of heretics, two-edged sword of the Comforter, knife that cuts away unlawfully planted disputes, fire that burns the brushwood of heresies, and truly agricultural shovel by which the heavy and light in teachings are separated, and scale that directs all strictness to the path of salvation! Entreat Christ, pray to Christ always, that He who renewed the world in the streams of the Jordan may save our race.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O venerable Father, all-blessed Gregory, lips that pour forth blessed discourses, drawing them from the unfathomable and divine springs of salvation, and explaining to all the words of prayer; rule that directs toward virtue; enlightened by divine contemplations, illumined by the radiance of the Uncreated Trinity, of which you were an invincible champion! Entreat Christ, pray to Christ, O venerable one, that He who renewed the world in the streams of the Jordan may save our race.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): He who once threatened* to annihilate the whole earth* and boasted of drying up the sea* — today proved to be* mocked by the faithful, like a sparrow* and more ridiculous than a mosquito;* he who struck fear with his appearance like some monster* was trampled underfoot* by Christ's servant.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Dometian calls us, O faithful,* today* to a God-pleasing banquet.* Let us come together to him* with joyful thought* and celebrate with him* his righteous deeds,* entreating the Savior of our souls* to grant peace to the world.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You multiplied the talent of Christ* given to you* with a double portion of grace,* as a faithful servant.* Therefore you hear, O all-honorable one:* Well done, good servant,* as a truly good pleaser!* Enter into the joy of your Lord,* receiving the reward for your labors!

Glory (Tone 4): Having illumined the word of your wisdom* with unblemished virtue,* you were beautiful in both, O Gregory of Nyssa,* with your divinely eloquent voice* adorning and consoling the people,* wisely and most sagely expounding the unity of the Godhead of the Trinity.* Therefore, having overcome foreign heresies with orthodox dogmas,* you raised the banner of the faith to the ends of the earth.* Standing before Christ with the immaterial Liturgies,* intercede for our souls for peace* and great mercy.

Now and ever: The hand of the Baptist trembled* when it touched Your most pure crown;* the River Jordan turned back,* not daring to serve You;* for if it feared Joshua, the son of Nun,* how could it not be terrified of its Creator?* But You fulfilled the whole plan of salvation, O our Savior,* to save the world by Your manifestation, O only Lover of mankind.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2
(Tone 2, Podoben: O House of Ephratha): A new song, as is fitting,* let all creation sing* to Christ, who was born of the Virgin* and baptized* in the Jordan today!
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.
Let the prophetic trumpet — the Forerunner —* cry aloud:* Bring to the Lord,* who is baptized in the streams,* the fruits of your finest deeds!
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
The kindred Divine Spirit* shone from on high* in the form of a dove,* ineffably visible,* and now has hastened to Christ.

Glory (Tone 4): Having corrected your life in God,* you illumined your actions with contemplation,* O divinely manifested Gregory!* For having loved wisdom with divine desire,* you were enriched by the grace of the Spirit's utterance.* And as from a honeycomb with honey, dripping with the sweetness of your words,* you unceasingly gladden with divine thoughts the Church of God.* Therefore, dwelling in the heavenly courts in hierarchal fashion,* pray unceasingly for us* who celebrate your memory.

Now and ever: Seeing You, who purify the sin of the world by the Spirit and fire,* the Baptist, beholding You coming to him,* cried out in awe and trembling:* I dare not touch Your most pure crown!* Sanctify me, O Master, by Your divine manifestation,* O only Lover of mankind!

The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 6): Having victory over Hades,* You ascended the Cross, O Christ,* to raise up with Yourself those who dwelt in the darkness of death.* Free from death, O almighty Savior,* who pour forth life from Your own light, have mercy on us!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Now Christ has conquered death* and, as He foretold, has risen and granted joy to the world,* so that we all, crying out, may sing this song:* O Fountain of life, O unapproachable Light,* O almighty Savior, have mercy on us!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

From You, O Lord, who are present in all creation,* where shall we sinners flee?* In the heavens You Yourself dwell; in Hades You conquered death.* Into the depths of the sea? Even there is Your hand, O Master!* Therefore to You we run, and falling down we pray:* O You who rose from the dead, have mercy on us!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

(Tone 8, Podoben: O Lord, though before the tribunal): O Lord, though as a man You stood before John in the Jordan,* yet sitting with the Father, You did not leave the throne.* Being baptized for our sake, You delivered the world from the bondage of sin,* as the Merciful and Lover of mankind.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

O Lord, though as a man You descended into the Jordan's waters,* the Spirit, descending from above, bore witness,* and the Father's voice called You Son.* Therefore, appear and grant incorruption to our souls.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, unchanging before the ages, You were born of the Father,* and in the latter times You came, assuming the form of a servant;* and as Creator, You renewed Your image,* for being baptized, You granted incorruption to our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 5, Idiomelon): O venerable Father, God-bearing Theodosius,* you greatly struggled in this temporal life* in hymns, fasting, and vigils, becoming a model for your disciples.* Now in one choir with the Bodiless Powers, you unceasingly glorify Christ,* God the Word and Redeemer from God,* who bowed His head before the Forerunner and sanctified the nature of the waters.* Entreat Him, beseech Him, O venerable one,* to grant the Church unity, peace, and great mercy.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O venerable Father, God-bearing Theodosius,* you greatly struggled in this temporal life* in hymns, fasting, and vigils, becoming a model for your disciples.* Now in one choir with the Bodiless Powers, you unceasingly glorify Christ,* God the Word and Redeemer from God,* who bowed His head before the Forerunner and sanctified the nature of the waters.* Entreat Him, beseech Him, O venerable one,* to grant the Church unity, peace, and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable Father, God-bearing Theodosius,* as you desired, the grace of the All-Holy Spirit dwelt in your pure soul* as a most pure light: adorned radiantly by its action,* you unceasingly glorify Christ,* the one Son in two natures,* baptized by the hand of the Forerunner and witnessed to by the voice of the Father.* Entreat Him, beseech Him, O venerable one,* to grant the universe unity, peace, and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O venerable Father, God-bearing Theodosius,* you were worthily counted worthy of the blessed life,* having attained it by purity and asceticism.* For even in this life you passed over to the life on high,* commanding all to rejoice, and with the Bodiless Powers unceasingly glorifying Christ,* who ineffably took flesh from the Virgin* and drowned our sins through baptism in the streams of the Jordan.* Entreat Him, beseech Him, O venerable one,* to grant the universe unity, peace, and great mercy.

Glory (Tone 1): A multitude of monastics honors you, our Father Theodosius,* as their instructor;* for by your path we have truly learned to walk in righteousness.* Blessed are you, who labored for Christ* and put the enemy's power to shame,* companion of angels, co-heir of the venerable and the righteous!* Pray with them to the Lord* that He may have mercy on our souls.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 6): Who would not call you blessed, O most holy Virgin?* Or who would not hymn your most pure childbirth?* For the Only-begotten Son, who shone forth timelessly from the Father,* came forth from you, O Pure One, having been ineffably made flesh.* Being God by nature, He became true man for our sake,* not divided into two persons* but known in two unconfused natures.* Entreat Him, O pure and all-blessed one,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): Your Resurrection, O Christ our Savior,* the angels hymn in heaven;* make us also on earth worthy* to glorify You with a pure heart.
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

As the Almighty, You shattered the gates of brass* and broke the bars of Hades,* and raised up the fallen human race.* Therefore we cry out with one accord:* O You who rose from the dead, O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Christ, desiring to raise us from the ancient corruption,* gave Himself to be nailed to the Cross and laid in the tomb.* The myrrh-bearing women sought Him with tears,* and weeping said: Woe to us, O Savior of all!* How could You consent to be laid in a tomb?* You willingly were laid there — how then were You stolen?* How were You moved? What place hid Your life-giving body?* But as You promised us, O Master,* appear and still our bitter weeping.* And while they wept, the angel spoke to them:* Cease your weeping and announce to the Apostles that the Lord is risen,* granting the world purification and great mercy.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
Voluntarily crucified, O Christ,* by Your burial You captured death,* and on the third day You rose as God in glory,* granting the world unending life and great mercy.

Glory (Tone 2): You, O venerable one, renounced the world and the things of the world.* Having received the Gospel, you followed it in an evangelical manner,* and living righteously as in paradise,* you slew the serpent, the man-slayer, by the utmost austerity of the body.* Therefore, having settled in heaven, O venerable Theodosius,* intercede for us and obtain great mercy.

Now and ever (Tone 4): Come, let us imitate the wise virgins!* Come, let us meet the Master who has appeared,* for He has come like a Bridegroom to John!* The Jordan, seeing You, was afraid;* and John cried out: I dare not touch the head of the Immortal One!* The Spirit descended in the form of a dove to sanctify the waters,* and a voice from heaven resounded: This is My Son* who has come into the world to save the human race!* O Lord — glory to You!

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 6): The angelic powers were at Your tomb and those who guarded it became as dead; and Mary stood at the grave, seeking Your most pure body. You despoiled Hades and were not tempted thereby. You met the Virgin and granted life. O Lord, risen from the dead, glory to You!
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord.
Verse: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8): O Lord, though You endured baptism in the flesh, the Sinless One, desiring to deliver the world from sin, yet knowing You as God, we glorify You: for being baptized, You freed our souls from bondage.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, when You, incarnate, came to the streams of the Jordan to sanctify the water by Your glorious baptism, You deigned to bow Your head before John, and are baptized by a servant, to save our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, desiring to save the world You created from deception, You deigned to become incarnate from the Virgin who knew no wedlock; and You delivered Adam from corruption as the Lover of mankind; and being baptized, You granted illumination to our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 2, Podoben: When from the tree): When you, illumined richly by the light of the Spirit,* despised riches,* having loved only the heavenly,* then you were clearly clothed in martyric firmness* and went to endure immeasurable sufferings.* Therefore, having overthrown the enemy,* you wove for yourself crowns of victory,* O God-blessed Tatiana.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

When you, O pure one, extinguished the stench of the flesh and the flame of sin* with the dew of the Divine Spirit,* which worked together with you,* then you tamed the beasts in the midst of the arena,* bravely giving your body over to wounds.* Therefore, having overthrown the enemy,* you wove for yourself crowns of victory,* O God-blessed Tatiana.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Neither sword, nor fire, nor wounds,* nor oppression, nor hunger,* nor any kind of torture* dulled your love for the Lord:* for with an inflamed heart seeking Him,* you despised all visible things in a single moment, O martyr,* and settled within the divine bridal chamber,* becoming the bride of the King of all.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): Beholding You, the naked Creator,* asking for baptism in the waters,* every nature of mortals was changed with fear and trembled;* the Forerunner, seized with trembling,* dared not approach You;* the sea fled, the Jordan turned its streams back;* the mountains danced, beholding You,* and the angelic powers marveled, saying:* O wonder! The Savior strips Himself bare,* desiring to clothe mankind in salvation and rebirth!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): You stood upon the waters,* O incomprehensible One;* You bowed Your head before the Forerunner;* having sanctified the world, You redeemed it from slavery* by Your baptism, O Sinless One.
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.
From the heights on high,* the Father and the Spirit bore witness* that You are the beloved Son, O Christ;* by this the mystery of the Trinity was made known* when You were baptized in the Jordan.
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
You sanctified, O Savior,* all the streams of the Jordan* and the nature of the waters, as God.* Therefore the human race glorifies* and hymns Your manifestation.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 4): He who wraps Himself in light as in a garment* deigned for our sake to become like us.* He is clothed today in the streams of the Jordan,* not Himself needing them for purification,* but preparing in Himself our rebirth.* O wonder!* Without fire He refines,* and rebuilds without destruction,* and saves those who are enlightened in Him,* Christ God and Savior of our souls.

Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You gave as a sign): Glorious Hermylus* and God-wise Stratonicus,* a wondrous pair,* confessing the uncreated Trinity,* overcame the deception of polytheism* after numerous wounds and tortures,* and crowned with the diadems of victory,* pray that peace and mercy* be granted to the ends of the earth.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O martyr Hermylus,* having been a deacon of the Word* by the election of the Divine Spirit,* you, by the shedding of your blood,* dyed the garment of the priesthood,* making it truly more sacred and radiant.* And now you have passed to the invisible royal chambers,* becoming a fervent intercessor* for those who honor you.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Having endured scraping* at the command of the torturer,* you were both sewn into a net,* and accepting a blessed end by drowning in the waters,* you wisely amazed the enemy, inventive in tortures, with all this.* Now you dwell most joyfully in heaven,* praying for all,* O all-valiant passion-bearers.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8, Podoben: What shall we call you): You greatly struggled, O saints,* valiantly enduring the barbarian onslaught,* zealously surrendering your souls to the sword.* Crowned with martyric crowns,* you worthily became fellow-dwellers with the angels.* Great is your endurance,* and still greater are your gifts!* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable ones of Christ, you struggled,* voluntarily disdaining the temporal course of this life,* valiantly subduing carnal reasoning;* and having died under the sword for Christ,* you worthily became fellow-dwellers with the angels.* Therefore, honoring your memories,* we entreat you:* pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You lived well in community, O saints,* and having valiantly emulated the struggles of the martyrs,* you lawfully received crowns from heaven:* for like them, you disregarded the swords* and endured the torments* in order to be jointly crowned* with the crowns of suffering.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory: O venerable fathers,* having studied day and night in the Law of the Lord,* you were counted worthy to be planted beside the Tree of Life,* and the fruit of your suffering blossomed into crowns.* Having boldness before the Master of the contest — God,* obtain for us also purification* and great mercy.

Now and ever: O Lord, desiring to fulfill* what You ordained from eternity,* from all creation You received servants of Your mystery:* from the angels — Gabriel, from mankind — the Virgin,* from the heavens — a star, and from the waters — the Jordan,* in which You destroyed the iniquities of the world.* O our Savior, glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2
(Tone 2, Podoben: O House of Ephratha): The Radiance of the Father's* glory has appeared* in the streams of the Jordan* and by baptism purifies* the defilement of our souls.
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.
Receive as a servant,* O Prophet John,* the Redeemer of the world,* and baptize the Creator* for the rebirth of mortals!
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
The Enlightenment has come,* the Redemption has appeared* in the streams of the Jordan;* let us come together to be purified* and hymn the Lover of mankind.

Glory (Tone 8): Having lived an equal-to-the-angels life in common ascetic efforts, O venerable fathers,* subduing the body by temperance,* you subjected it to the spirit,* becoming workers of the Lord's commandments.* You preserved the primordial beauty of the image* and accomplished martyric feats after ascetic labors.* And adorned with double crowns,* earnestly entreat the Savior that we may be saved.

Now and ever, of the Feast: The angelic hosts were astonished* at what was seen today at the Jordan:* when You, O Savior, stood naked in the waters* and bowed Your most pure head* to be baptized by John.* For when You voluntarily impoverished Yourself, the world was enriched.* O Lord, glory to You!

Your mercy, O Lord, shall be with me all the days of my life.
Verse: The Lord is my Shepherd; I shall not want.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): Seeing our Illuminator,* who enlightens every person,* coming to be baptized,* the Forerunner rejoices in soul, and with trembling hand points to Him and says to the people:* Behold the One who saves Israel and delivers us from corruption.* O sinless Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The angelic powers were astonished,* seeing how our Savior accepts baptism from a servant,* while the Holy Spirit descends to bear Him witness,* and the voice of the Father is heard from heaven:* The One whom the Forerunner baptizes with his hand* is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.* O Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The stream of the Jordan received You, the Source;* the Comforter descended in the form of a dove.* He bows His head, who bowed the heavens;* and the clay cries out to the Creator:* Why do You command me to do what is above me?* I need Your baptism.* O sinless Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having resolved to save the human race that had gone astray,* You did not refuse to take on the form of a servant.* For it was fitting for You, the Lord and God,* to accept for our sake what is ours.* Having accepted bodily baptism, O Savior,* You made us worthy of forgiveness.* Therefore we cry out to You: O Christ our God — glory to You!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Today Christ comes to the Jordan to be baptized.* Today John touches the head of the Master.* The heavenly powers were astonished, beholding the most glorious mystery:* the sea saw it and fled, and the Jordan, seeing it, turned back.* And we, having been enlightened, proclaim:* Glory to God who has appeared and become visible on earth* and has illumined the world.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You, O Lord, resolved to accomplish all that You ordained from eternity.* From all creation You received service for Your Mystery:* from the angels — Gabriel; from men — the Virgin;* from the heavens — the star; and from the Jordan — the water,* in which You destroyed the iniquities of mankind.* O Lord — glory to You!

Glory, now and ever (Tone 2): You bowed Your head before the Forerunner and crushed the heads of the serpents.* Entering the water, You enlightened all* who glorify You, O Savior, Illuminator of our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2

(Tone 2, Idiomelon): John, seeing at the Jordan River that You came to him,* said to You: O Christ God!* Why have You come to Your servant, O sinless Lord?* In whose name shall I baptize You?* The Father's? But You carry Him within Yourself.* The Son's? But that is You Yourself, incarnate.* The Holy Spirit's? But You know how to give Him by Your own lips to the faithful.* O God who has appeared — glory to You!
Verse: The sea saw it and fled;* the Jordan turned back.

The waters saw You, O God, the waters saw You and were afraid,* for the Cherubim and Seraphim cannot gaze upon Your glory.* They stand before You with awe:* some bear You, others glorify Your majesty.* With them we too proclaim Your glory, O Merciful One, crying:* O God who has appeared — glory to You!
Verse: What ails you, O sea, that you fled?* And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?

Today the Creator of heaven and earth comes in the flesh to the Jordan,* and the Sinless One asks for baptism* to cleanse the world from the deception of the enemy.* The Lord of all is baptized by a servant,* and by the water grants purification to the human race.* To Him let us cry: O God who has appeared — glory to You!

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6, Idiomelon): John, the radiant lamp born of a barren mother,* seeing Christ — the Sun from the Virgin —* as He asked to be baptized in the Jordan,* cried out with fear and joy to Him:* You, O Lord, sanctify me by Your divine manifestation.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): As a beginning for the world on the path of life along the way of virtue,* which clearly leads to heaven,* you appeared, O thrice-blessed Paul;* for you rightly established in your soul spiritual ascents,* by which you reached the summit of the virtues* through temperance and many labors.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Having soared with a winged mind toward the Divine Desire,* you ascended to the heights of the desert,* as though approaching the darkness;* there you were radiantly enlightened by the knowledge of God* and on the tablets of your soul* you were anointed with the brightness of virtues.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The desert bloomed with beautiful flowers of your virtues:* for you weeded from it the thorns of the passions* and planted the glorious labors of your divine deeds.* Therefore, like a lily,* you now pour forth from God* the fragrance of your life to us.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 2, Podoben: When from the tree): When by divine command* you, O Father, joyfully fled from worldly beauty,* then you chose over the wide and spacious way* the narrow, sorrowful, and very difficult one.* Therefore you took on the care of souls,* clothing yourself in the divine image,* by fleeing and casting off the rags* of all the passions, O God-blessed one.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

A wondrous life you devised for yourself, a wondrous manner,* struck by the love of Christ,* whose ineffable poverty you loved.* Before the gates of your parents, homeless, O Father, you suffered,* oppressed and afflicted.* Therefore you were filled with spiritual gifts* and inherited the inalienable riches* that are in heaven.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Bearing in your hands the sacred Gospel, O Father,* you became its fulfiller.* Having renounced your parents' estate, O John,* in tears and in a narrow cell* you spent your life, O venerable one.* Therefore now you enjoy* the divine consolation* and unceasing gladness, O God-glorified one.

Glory (Tone 2): Having renounced the world and the things of the world, O venerable one,* taking the Gospel,* you followed Christ in an evangelical manner.* Living in a hut as in paradise, mystically,* you slew the man-slaying serpent by the extreme austerity of life according to the flesh.* Therefore, having your dwelling in heaven,* O blessed John,* intercede for us and obtain great mercy.
Now and ever (Theotokion): O impassable Gate, mystically sealed, O blessed Theotokos Virgin, receive our prayers and bring them to Your Son and God: that through You He may save our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6

(Tone 6): O Lord, once the Apostles reached the depths of the sea with their nets,* and then with their teachings the heights of the Kingdom.* With skill they explored the unreachable depth;* by faith they arrived at Your inscrutable essence;* and they preached to the world Your co-eternal Son.* By their prayers and those of all the saints, have mercy on us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.

When Your disciples, O Christ,* were tossed by the storm in the boat, they cried out:* Teacher, save us! We are perishing!* And now we cry out: O Savior, Lover of mankind,* deliver us from our afflictions, we pray!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.

Your Cross, O Lord,* has become an invincible weapon for Your martyrs:* for they, seeing death near at hand* and foreseeing the life to come, were strengthened by hope in You.* By their prayers have mercy on us.
Glory, now and ever: I lift up the eyes of my heart to You, O Lady.* Do not despise my humble sighing* when Your Son judges the world.* Be then my shelter and protectress.
