(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): You bound deception,* being yourself bound for the Lord* and imprisoned in a dungeon, O Apostle.* Therefore we honor you with love* and with faith we venerate your chains;* drawing from them health of body and salvation of soul,* we gratefully praise* you, O seer of God,* companion of the Bodiless Powers.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): You bound deception,* being yourself bound for the Lord* and imprisoned in a dungeon, O Apostle.* Therefore we honor you with love* and with faith we venerate your chains;* drawing from them health of body and salvation of soul,* we gratefully praise* you, O seer of God,* companion of the Bodiless Powers.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Through you, knowledge is received* by the fruitful nations of peoples* formerly enslaved by ignorance,* whose image was shown by the cloth lowered from on high,* which contained within* every living creature, O Apostle.* Therefore every age glorifies you,* venerating the chains that you wore* for Christ with total devotion.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Through you, knowledge is received* by the fruitful nations of peoples* formerly enslaved by ignorance,* whose image was shown by the cloth lowered from on high,* which contained within* every living creature, O Apostle.* Therefore every age glorifies you,* venerating the chains that you wore* for Christ with total devotion.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Him whom you denied when He was judged,* you hymned when He rose from the dead, O Apostle,* and you preached in all the expanses of the world* as God and Man,* who out of great goodness* became a companion of mortals.* Therefore with love we call you blessed, O Peter,* venerating the chains that you wore* for Christ, O all-glorious one.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Him whom you denied when He was judged,* you hymned when He rose from the dead, O Apostle,* and you preached in all the expanses of the world* as God and Man,* who out of great goodness* became a companion of mortals.* Therefore with love we call you blessed, O Peter,* venerating the chains that you wore* for Christ, O all-glorious one.

Glory (Tone 6): Today the foundation of the Church,* Peter, the rock of faith,* offers us his venerable chains for the strengthening of our souls.* Come, all of you, and kissing them,* let us crown him with laudatory odes:* Rejoice, O fervent champion of the faith,* who with fervent mind confessed Christ the Son of God* with great courage.* Rejoice, O joy of the universe and keeper of the keys of the Heavenly Kingdom.* Bestow grace on those who with love honor you* and embrace your venerable chains with desire,* as one counted worthy to stand before the throne of the King of all — Christ,* offering prayer for us, the greatly sinful!
Now and ever (Stavrotheotokion): Seeing our Life hanging upon the Tree,* the all-immaculate Theotokos,* weeping as a mother, cried out:* O my Son and my God,* save those who with love sing to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): Your Cross, O Lord,* is life and protection for Your people,* and all of us who hope in it hymn You,* O God crucified in the flesh: have mercy on us!

Glory, now and ever: Seeing You crucified, O Christ,* Your Mother cried out:* What strange mystery do I behold, O my Son?* How is it that You die on the tree, suspended in the flesh,* O Giver of life?

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities,* who could stand, O Lord? But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You were called): Illumined by the rays of the Spirit,* when the truly supreme desire for God* set you ablaze and gave wings to your soul* to desire His love,* then you despised flesh and blood* and dwelt outside the world,* uniting yourself to Him by rigorous asceticism and silence.* Therefore you were filled, as you asked, with blessings beyond this world,* and you shone like a star,* illumining our souls, O Anthony.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Having shattered the bows and arrows of the demons* by the grace of the Divine Spirit,* and having made their malice and snares manifest to all* through your divinely inspired teachings,* you, in the flames of divine illumination,* became a radiant luminary for the first monks,* the first adornment of the desert,* a skillful and honest physician of the sick,* and an original model of life* that grows in virtue, O Father Anthony.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Finding you filled with divine gifts, O Father,* Christ found you as a spotless mirror of divine reflections* and irradiated you with the radiant flashes of His own light.* From that time you appeared as an inexhaustible fountain of healings,* food for the hungry, refreshment and desired showers for the thirsty;* and seeing the inclinations of souls,* you wisely made them better by your word, turning them to God,* whom entreat* to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Pure in soul and heart,* an earthly angel, a heavenly man,* teacher of virginity,* strict measure of temperance, O Anthony,* who lived with your Master* and unceasingly offered, O blessed one,* praise together with the angels and the venerable* and all the martyrs to Him!* Deliver us who always celebrate your sacred memory* from grievous afflictions and transgressions.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

(Tone 2): You gathered an ascetic gymnasium on earth, O venerable Anthony,* drowning all the assaults of the passions in the torrent of your tears.* By the divine and sacred ladder* that leads to heaven,* all came to know your God-pleasing life.* For having shown in yourself the fruits of piety,* you heal with them the infirmities and passions* of all who faithfully cry out to you:* Rejoice, O golden-shining star of the East* and luminary and shepherd of monasticism!* Rejoice, O co-glorious one, finest nursling of the desert* and unshakable foundation of the Church!* Rejoice, O great guide of those who have gone astray!* Rejoice, O our boast* and bright joy of the universe!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Let us honor an angel on earth* and a man of God in heaven,* the bright ornament of the world,* the delight of blessings and virtues,* the boast of ascetics — Anthony!* For planted in the house of God,* he blossomed in righteousness,* and like a cedar in the desert,* he multiplied Christ's flock of rational sheep* in holiness and righteousness.

Glory (Tone 6): Having preserved the God-like image unharmed,* and having ascetically set the mind as ruler over the ruinous passions,* you ascended, as far as you could, to the God-likeness.* For having courageously compelled your own nature,* you strove to subject the worse to the better* and to make the flesh a servant of the spirit.* Therefore you appeared as the summit of monasticism,* citizen of the desert, trainer of spiritual athletes,* perfect rule of virtue.* And now in heaven, when the dim mirrors have melted away, O Anthony,* you purely contemplate the Holy Trinity,* praying face to Face* for those who with faith and love honor you.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 6): Who would not call you blessed, O most holy Virgin?* Or who would not hymn your most pure childbirth?* For the Only-begotten Son, who shone forth timelessly from the Father,* came forth from you, O Pure One, having been ineffably made flesh.* Being God by nature, He became true man for our sake,* not divided into two persons* but known in two unconfused natures.* Entreat Him, O pure and all-blessed one,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5): Rejoice, O you who were the first leader of ascetics* and an invincible champion!* For cutting out the roots of the passions* and the assaults of the demons,* you manfully exposed their powerlessness in combat* and their soul-destroying deception.* You revealed the power of the Savior's Cross* and its invincible might,* and girding yourself with it,* you conquered all* who rejected the divine manifestation* in the flesh of Christ.* Entreat Him* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse:

You appeared as a column of virtue,* radiant and firmly established,* and as a cloud* that overshadowed those in the desert,* leading the God-beholders from earth to heaven.* Having parted the sea of passions with the staff of the Cross, O God-blessed one,* and having conquered the spiritual Amalek,* you found an unobstructed passage to heaven* and an incorruptible inheritance,* joyfully standing with the Bodiless Powers* before the throne of Christ.* Entreat Him* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse:

Rejoice, O Anthony,* who joined the angelic choirs* in the heights:* for on earth, O Father,* you truly lived their life, practicing virtue,* appearing as a most pure, spotless mirror* that received the light-bearing* flashes of the Holy Spirit, O all-rich one.* Therefore, illumined,* you beheld the future,* foretelling all things through initiation* into the divine manifestation of the light of Christ.* Entreat Him* to grant our souls great mercy.

Glory (Tone 8): The assemblies of monastics* honor you as their instructor,* our Father Anthony:* for by your path we have truly learned to walk in righteousness.* Blessed are you, who labored for Christ* and exposed the enemy's power,* companion of angels,* co-heir of the venerable and the righteous.* With them pray to the Lord* for the mercy of our souls.

Now and ever (Theotokion): O unwedded Virgin, who ineffably conceived God in the flesh, O Mother of God Most High! Accept the prayers of Your servants, O all-immaculate one, granting to all the cleansing of transgressions. Now, receiving our supplications, pray for the salvation of us all.

troparia.x: Troparia
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 7
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 7): Come, let us rejoice in the Lord,* who conquered the power of death and enlightened the human race,* crying out with the bodiless ones:* O our Creator and Savior — glory to You!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
You endured the Cross and burial for us, O Savior,* and as God, You slew death by death.* Therefore we worship Your Resurrection on the third day,* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

The Apostles, seeing the Resurrection of the Creator,* marveled, singing angelic praise:* This is the glory of the Church, this is the treasure of the Kingdom.* O Lord who suffered for us — glory to You!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Though the lawless seized You, O Christ,* still You are my God, and I am not ashamed.* They struck Your shoulders, and I do not deny it.* They nailed You to the Cross, and I do not hide it.* I glory in Your Resurrection, for Your death is my life.* O almighty and man-loving Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): You endured persecutions* and withstood hardships,* O divinely eloquent venerable Athanasius,* until you drove away* the godless deception of Arius* and saved your flock from impiety,* orthodoxly establishing by your dogmas* the Son as consubstantial with the Father and the Spirit,* O all-blessed hierarch.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

By the lightning of your preaching* you enlightened those in darkness* and drove away all deception,* firmly enduring hardships for the faith, O Athanasius,* as a true shepherd,* as an unshakable pillar,* O all-glorious one, of the Church of Christ.* Therefore, gathering together, we honor you,* rejoicing in song.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

You learned every virtue* with perseverance, O divinely inspired one,* and anointed with the holy anointing of the Spirit,* you clearly became a most holy hierarch* and a true shepherd* and a defender of the faith.* Therefore the whole Church glorifies your memory,* sacredly celebrating it* and glorifying the Savior.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): By the illuminations of the Spirit,* having enlightened your mind,* you became a radiant sun,* sending commandments like rays* to all the ends of the earth,* and illumining the fullness of the faithful,* O all-blessed one,* and driving away the darkness of heresies, O God-bearer,* by the power of Him who shone forth from the Virgin.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

By the eloquence of your words,* O most sacred Cyril,* the whole Church is adorned* and piously arrayed, as in beautiful garments,* and sacredly honors* your holy and auspicious memory, O glorious one,* boast of the orthodox,* chief of the Council of the Fathers,* defender of the Thrice-Holy!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

From the Church of Christ* you drove away the spiritual wolves, O Cyril,* with the staff of your teachings,* and fortified it all around* with the ramparts of your words,* presenting it to Christ unharmed and unshaken.* Entreat Him to deliver from corruption and afflictions* us who with faith celebrate* your eternally sacred memory.

Glory (Tone 6): Let us all hymn the hierarch of Christ,* Athanasius,* for he destroyed all the schemes of Arius* and clearly proclaims throughout all the world the dominion of the Holy Trinity,* the one God, undivided in three Persons,* whom he also entreats on our behalf,* as we with faith celebrate his memory.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 7): You became a Mother supernaturally, O Theotokos,* and ineffably and incomprehensibly remained a Virgin.* The tongue cannot express the wonder of your childbearing.* For your conception, O Most Pure, was wondrous,* and the manner of your giving birth is beyond reach.* For when God wills, the order of nature is overruled.* Therefore, all of us, knowing you as the Mother of God, earnestly entreat you:* Pray that our souls may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): You rose from the grave, O Savior of the world,* and raised up mankind together with Your body.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.
Come, let us worship Him who rose from the dead* and enlightened all,* for He delivered us from the torments of Hades* by His Resurrection on the third day,* granting us life and great mercy.
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.
Having descended into Hades, O Christ, You captured death,* and rising on the third day, You raised us up together with Yourself,* as we glorify Your mighty Resurrection,* O Lord, Lover of mankind.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.

Wondrous did You appear, O Lord, lying in the tomb as one asleep,* but rising on the third day,* as the Almighty You raised up Adam with Yourself, who cried out:* Glory to Your Resurrection, O only Lover of mankind!

Glory (Tone 3): Once again the golden-flowing Nile,* who bears the same name as immortality,* has come with his annual memorial,* filling the golden-streaming channels of immortality with good floods* and bearing fruit with sweet nourishment — lofty divine proclamations.* He teaches to worship the dominion of the undivided Trinity* and pours forth divine teachings,* nourishing the thoughts of the faithful* and interceding for our souls.

Now and ever: Without seed, by the will of the Father, from the Divine Spirit* you conceived the Son of God,* who eternally was from the Father without a mother,* but for our sake came to be from you without a father.* You bore Him in the flesh and nursed Him as a child with milk.* Therefore, do not cease to pray* that our souls be delivered from all afflictions.

(Tone 7): You shattered death by Your Cross; You opened paradise to the thief; You changed the weeping of the myrrh-bearers and commanded Your apostles to preach: You are risen, O Christ our God, granting great mercy to the world.

Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord.
Verse: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You gave as a sign): Desiring blessedness beyond understanding,* you established, O divinely inspired one,* temperance as your food,* poverty as your wealth,* non-possession as your true estate,* and humility as your good repute.* Therefore you obtained what you desired according to your will,* settling in the tabernacles of the saints,* O Macarius.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Desiring blessedness beyond understanding,* you established, O divinely inspired one,* temperance as your food,* poverty as your wealth,* non-possession as your true estate,* and humility as your good repute.* Therefore you obtained what you desired according to your will,* settling in the tabernacles of the saints,* O Macarius.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The course of asceticism* you completed without ceasing* and preserved the faith.* Therefore you received, O Father, the crown of righteousness,* which Christ prepared for you,* who gives rewards according to merit* and bestows honors and payment for labors.* That we too may now receive them,* pray, O God-bearer.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

The course of asceticism* you completed without ceasing* and preserved the faith.* Therefore you received, O Father, the crown of righteousness,* which Christ prepared for you,* who gives rewards according to merit* and bestows honors and payment for labors.* That we too may now receive them,* pray, O God-bearer.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Every pleasure* you cast aside, O wise in God,* subduing your body,* tempering your senses with the efforts of abstinence* and the austerity of life,* with patience in temptations and steadfastness in adversity.* In return for these you receive unending delight,* imperishable nourishment,* and inexpressible joy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Every pleasure* you cast aside, O wise in God,* subduing your body,* tempering your senses with the efforts of abstinence* and the austerity of life,* with patience in temptations and steadfastness in adversity.* In return for these you receive unending delight,* imperishable nourishment,* and inexpressible joy.

Glory (Tone 8): Rejoice, O Egypt,* having nurtured such a citizen,* a blossom among the blessed — Macarius!* For adorned with the wisdom of the Holy Spirit,* he displayed every ascetic effort in his virtuous life.* Bringing him forward as our mediator,* we ask that he entreat Christ* that our souls may be saved.

Now and ever (Theotokion): Rejoice, O boast of the universe! Rejoice, O temple of the Lord! Rejoice, O overshadowed Mountain! Rejoice, O Refuge! Rejoice, O golden Lampstand! Rejoice, O all-honorable glory of the orthodox! Rejoice, O Mary, Mother of Christ God! Rejoice, O Paradise! Rejoice, O divine Table! Rejoice, O Tabernacle! Rejoice, O all-golden Vessel! Rejoice, O Hope of all!

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 4)
The Lord will hear me when I call to Him.
Verse: When I called to You, O God of my righteousness, You heard me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities,* who could stand, O Lord? But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): O Father Euthymius,* your birth was foretold* by a heavenly angel sent from above,* like that of John, who sprang from a barren womb.* For you appeared as his zealot* and reflection,* without possessions, without a home.* Like the Baptist, nurtured in the mountains,* you shone with boundless miracles.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Father Euthymius,* you appeared as a fruit of barrenness,* yet you proved to be truly fruitful:* for from your spiritual seed* the desert was filled with monks,* formerly impassable.* Now entreat* that our souls be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Father Euthymius,* your life is unsurpassed* and your faith truly orthodox:* for from action you soared to the highest contemplation,* becoming a dwelling of wisdom,* honoring in two natures* one of the Trinity — Christ,* whom entreat* for our souls!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You were called): Sanctified from your mother's womb,* when the All-seeing Eye* foresaw your resolute determination and inclination* leading you toward the better,* then, O all-blessed one,* with a God-given gift, bearing a name synonymous with consolation,* He announced you to your parents, resolving their hopelessness.* Therefore from childhood you grew as a pleaser of the Benefactor — God,* whom entreat* to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Finding you filled with divine gifts,* Christ found you as an unstained mirror of divine revelations* and irradiated you with the brilliant radiance of His light.* Therefore you appeared as a most generous fountain of healings,* nourisher of the hungry,* and one able to give the thirsty showers of desire.* Seeing the inclinations of souls,* you wisely nurtured them by your word toward the better — toward God,* whom entreat* to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

By divine contemplation and action,* your equal-to-the-angels life shone* as a rule of virtue and a most precise model* for those who desire to serve God in perfection.* And your God-wise word, O Euthymius,* taught all to honor the Trinity,* the Son, who is known as one in two natures,* and the Councils, equal in number to the four Gospels of Christ,* whom entreat* to save and enlighten our souls.

Glory (Tone 3): Dedicated to God from your mother's womb,* like a second Samuel, O Father Euthymius,* you became a namesake of consolation for the faithful,* a staff and support of monasticism,* a pure dwelling of the Holy Spirit.* Intercede for us who honor you* and obtain great mercy.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 3): How shall we not marvel, O all-honorable one, at your divine-human childbirth?* For you, O all-immaculate one, not knowing a man,* gave birth bodily without a father to the Son* who was begotten in eternity from the Father without a mother,* suffering no change, confusion, or division,* but preserving whole the properties of both natures.* Therefore, O Virgin Mother and Lady, entreat Him* to save the souls of those who with true faith confess you as the Theotokos.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5, Podoben: Rejoice): Rejoice, for you became the beginning of consolation* in the Church, O venerable Father:* for with your birth,* all the heretical assaults* were repelled by God's Providence.* Therefore He named you* with a name synonymous with consolation.* By the radiance of your life* you filled with joy the ranks of the heavenly Beings,* living with them in common dwellings,* filled with light,* and delighting in the all-rich and divine illumination.* Now entreat Christ* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse: Precious in the sight of the Lord* is the death of His saints.

Taking the staff of the Cross as your support,* you dried up the torrents of lawlessness.* Nourished spiritually, O all-blessed one,* by the torrent of divine sweetness* and the fountain of paradise,* you revealed saving springs in the desert.* Fed by the streams of the Divine Spirit,* O venerable one,* you grew like a palm tree to the height of heaven* and appeared as a pillar of fire,* O God-wise one,* and full of the radiant* illumination of Christ. Entreat Him* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse: Blessed is the man who fears the Lord;* he greatly delights in His commandments.

Truly, even to the wondrous tabernacle,* even to the house of God, O all-blessed one,* you passed with joy* and gladness of soul,* where, O Father, you heard the delightful sound of feasting* and sweet singing* and inexpressible consolation,* and you entered into communion with beauty, O Euthymius!* Desiring this alone,* you mortified the flesh,* and this you received,* adorned with divine righteousness.* Entreat Christ,* who grants the world great mercy.

Glory: O venerable Father,* you did not give sleep to your eyes* nor slumber to your eyelids* until you freed your soul and body from the passions* and prepared yourself as a dwelling of the Spirit.* For Christ, coming with the Father,* made His abode in you.* And having become a pleaser of the consubstantial Trinity,* O great preacher Euthymius,* intercede for our souls.

Now and ever (Theotokion): We call you blessed, O Theotokos Virgin,* and we glorify You, O faithful, as is fitting,* the unshakable city, the indestructible wall,* the firm protectress,* the refuge of our souls.

Your mercy, O Lord, shall be with me all the days of my life.
Verse: The Lord is my Shepherd; I shall not want.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: As a valiant one): Him, who out of mercy* became man as He willed,* and is known by the mind in two wills and actions,* Him you preached,* O thrice-blessed Maximus, loudly.* Therefore you are crowned with a crown of glory,* in heaven standing before the throne of Christ God.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

With the sinews of your teachings,* you, O Father, strangled the cunning and babbling Pyrrhus;* and you endured persecution* and violence, O ever-memorable one,* and exile, and cutting off of tongue and hand,* confessing in two wills and natures* one of the Trinity — our God,* whom entreat to save and enlighten our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The pen of a swift scribe,* sharpened by the Spirit,* was your holy tongue,* which beautifully inscribed by grace on the tablets of our hearts* the dogmas of the Faith.* Therefore we call you blessed, O Maximus,* and your sacred memory we honor.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8, Podoben: O most glorious wonder): O wondrous wonder!* A dove that flew from on high* converses with the martyr Neophytus in a human voice;* through this the life of the youth shone forth,* and he valiantly went to martyrdom,* proclaiming Christ as God,* whom entreat to grant us great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O wondrous wonder!* Wise from his very swaddling-clothes,* Neophytus works miracles by the action of the Spirit:* he draws water from a rock by his prayers,* raises the dead,* and illumines the blind.* Therefore we honor his suffering,* crying out: O Christ, by his intercessions* grant us great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O wondrous wonder!* Suffering for Christ, the valiant Neophytus* terrified his enemies with unprecedented miracles:* he extinguishes fire when cast into the furnace,* tames the beasts,* and breaks the instruments of torture.* Therefore we faithfully honor his memory,* crying out to Christ: Grant us great mercy.

Glory (Tone 6): O venerable Father,* throughout all the earth has gone forth the proclamation of your righteous deeds,* and therefore in heaven you have received the reward for your labors.* You destroyed the hosts of demons,* reached the ranks of the angels,* whose blameless life you emulated.* Having boldness before Christ God,* ask peace for our souls.

Now and ever (Stavrotheotokion): When the immaculate Lamb* once beheld her own Lamb lifted upon the Cross,* she cried out as a mother, weeping:* O my sweetest Child, what new and strange sight is this?* How does the ungrateful assembly* deliver You to the cross, O long-suffering One?

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): Placing our hope in Your Cross,* we have access to the tree of life.* O Lord — glory to You!

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.
When You, the only Immortal One, were hung upon the tree,* then You tore the snares of the devil.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
You cared for nothing earthly, O holy martyrs:* at the judgment you manfully proclaimed Christ* and received from Him a reward for your sufferings.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Glory, now and ever: When you saw your Son upon the tree,* a sword of sorrow pierced your heart, O Most Pure.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): Clearly illumined by the rays of the Spirit,* you, O revealer of sacred things, Timothy,* appeared as a brightly visible luminary* that encircled the world with radiance,* dispelling the darkness of ignorance.* Therefore we celebrate your memory,* glorifying the Savior.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O God-wise Timothy,* you drank the sweetness* from the abundant stream* and wisely refreshed those who fervently thirsted for it.* You poured forth for us the divine knowledge of eternal life,* for which we honor your memory* and glorify the Savior.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O God-wise Timothy,* overcoming the painful illnesses of the body* and its infirmities,* you easily halted the power of deception over the mind,* blessed one.* You preached Christ as true God* and brought many to the knowledge of the truth.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): O boast of the martyrs,* you forced deception to retreat,* raising bright signs of victory against it.* You bear a great name synonymous with resurrection,* all-praised Anastasius;* therefore you shine as you rejoice* and intercede for our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O Martyr Anastasius,* having raised mighty* signs of victory against deception,* you received the double* crown of your contest:* for you shone forth in asceticism and in martyrdom,* and were counted worthy to stand before the throne of the King.* Therefore pray for those who honor you.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

The ends of the world* now hymn your miracles,* O wonder-worker Anastasius;* for Christ adorned you with miracle-working,* as a reward for your struggles.* Therefore we celebrate your memory,* glorifying the Savior.

Glory (Tone 8): Foreordained by God and taught by the wise Paul,* you were initiated into things divine,* having lived well in the episcopate,* O wise Timothy.* You put to shame the impiety of idols* and the deception of the enemy.* Therefore, dwelling now in the heavenly Kingdom,* remember us too, most blessed one.
Now and ever (Theotokion): Rejoice, O Virgin Bride of God!* Rejoice, O hope of the faithful!* Rejoice, O purification of the world!* Rejoice, from every curse deliver us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): O glorious Apostles, pillars of the Church,* preachers of truth, radiant luminaries!* With spiritual fire you consumed every deception,* pray that our souls be saved.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.

O Apostles of Christ, workers of the Savior!* Bearing the cross on your shoulders like a plough,* you cleansed the land fouled by idolatrous deception,* and having sown the word of faith,* you reaped the sheaves of the nations.* Pray that our souls be saved.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.

O all-praised martyrs, spiritual sheep,* rational whole-burnt offerings and acceptable sacrifices, well-pleasing to God!* Though the earth has not covered you in obscurity,* heaven has received you.* Pray that our souls be saved.
Glory, now and ever: Let us glorify the one who remained a pure Virgin after giving birth,* as the Mother of God the Word,* saying: Glory to you!

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs, by Hieromonk Stephen): O blessed martyrs,* you conquered the enemy by the power of your spirit,* manfully rising against the tyrant.* You endured all torments and violence for Christ,* and now rejoice eternally in the heavenly Kingdom.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
You shone like the sun* in a land of darkness and violence,* showing the light of the catholic faith.* For it you suffered,* dying by sword or by fire,* and now abide in the heavenly dwellings.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Neither hunger nor hardship,* nor the cruelty of enemies,* nor truly blessed death* could separate you from the love of God.* And as lambs led to slaughter,* you freely offered your souls to the Shepherd of all.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8, Podoben: What shall we call you): What shall we call you, O blessed ones?* Soldiers of the great King and God,* who manfully conquered godlessness?* Warriors of Christ,* who preferred death for the faith* over a shameful life?* Therefore pray for the salvation of our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

How shall we glorify you,* O blessed Nikita, Vincent, John and Constantine,* Michael, Onuphrius, and glorious Philip,* who shone with divine light* in the darkness of the enemy's night?* You are the pride and glory of the Podlachian land.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

How you enlighten us,* O passion-bearers Maximus, Daniel and Constantine,* Bartholomew, Luke, and Ignatius,* that we not go astray* and that by your prayers the Lord grant us strength* to confess the true faith unto the end.

Glory (Tone 6): Having rejected the godless counsel,* having endured manfully in temptation,* and having received the martyric crown,* O glorious martyrs,* you now stand before the throne of Christ.* Pray for us that our souls be saved.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 6): Who would not call you blessed, O most holy Virgin?* Or who would not hymn your most pure childbirth?* For the Only-begotten Son, who shone forth timelessly from the Father,* came forth from you, O Pure One, having been ineffably made flesh.* Being God by nature, He became true man for our sake,* not divided into two persons* but known in two unconfused natures.* Entreat Him, O pure and all-blessed one,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 8, Podoben: O most glorious wonder): Thirteen most glorious martyrs,* you counted the fearsome death as nothing,* valiantly standing in defense of your holy faith.* You laid down your lives for the flock of Christ* and now reign with Him in the heavenly Kingdom.
Verse: The righteous cry out and the Lord hears them.
Thirteen most blessed martyrs,* receiving the cup of suffering,* you invoked the divine name* of God Almighty,* who strengthened you in the hour of trial* and crowned you with the crown of victory.
Verse: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the Lord delivers them from them all.
Thirteen invincible martyrs,* you voluntarily gave yourselves to death,* sanctifying your native land with your blood,* in which the seeds of your martyrdom* blossomed into a harvest of faith.

Glory (Tone 3): Come today and let us magnify our martyrs,* who without hatred met their enemies,* who came with their godless* demand to betray the holy faith.* But they chose rather to die* than to abandon the truth of Christ.

Now and ever: Without seed, by the will of the Father, from the Divine Spirit* you conceived the Son of God,* who eternally was from the Father without a mother,* but for our sake came to be from you without a father.* You bore Him in the flesh and nursed Him as a child with milk.* Therefore, do not cease to pray* that our souls be delivered from all afflictions.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): Having first moved to the soul, O holy woman,* and confirmed your resolve by deeds,* you departed from the smooth path of empty pleasures* and chose the thorny path of virtue,* weeping and lamenting and burning with divine desire,* O Xenia, most praiseworthy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

To the harbor of divine calm* you sailed quietly,* and the storm of worldly waves* did not engulf you with the bitterness of pleasures, O holy one.* But having wisely navigated your life,* you arrived at the quiet refuge,* where now you enjoy eternal gladness.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Worthily imitating God,* who descended from on high to exalt the fallen,* you remained a stranger, O wise woman,* willingly choosing poverty* and afflictions of the flesh,* and by your patience received as a reward* the riches of the heavenly Kingdom.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.
(Tone 7): The martyrs drove away the darkness of godlessness* and showed all people the light of the knowledge of God.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!
With orthodox teachings, like divine stars,* you illumined the Church of the Lord,* O most wise Shepherds.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.
You tore asunder the snares of the demons,* having settled forever in the inaccessible deserts,* O venerable Fathers.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 7): You became a Mother supernaturally, O Theotokos,* and ineffably and incomprehensibly remained a Virgin.* The tongue cannot express the wonder of your childbearing.* For your conception, O Most Pure, was wondrous,* and the manner of your giving birth is beyond reach.* For when God wills, the order of nature is overruled.* Therefore, all of us, knowing you as the Mother of God, earnestly entreat you:* Pray that our souls may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): You cared for nothing earthly, O holy martyrs:* at the judgment you manfully proclaimed Christ* and received from Him a reward for your sufferings.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.

O all-praised martyrs, spiritual sheep,* rational whole-burnt offerings and acceptable sacrifices, well-pleasing to God!* Though the earth has not covered you in obscurity,* heaven has received you.* Pray that our souls be saved.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who suffered well and were crowned!* Pray to the Lord* that our souls may be saved.
Glory, now and ever: The divine holy mountain, Daniel once beheld,* and the wise Isaiah proclaimed you, O Pure One,* as the Mother of God, who bodily contained the Uncontainable.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 8): An evening hymn and spiritual worship* we offer to You, O Christ,* for You deigned to have mercy on us by Your Resurrection.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
O Lord, O Lord,* do not cast us away from Your face,* but deign to have mercy on us by Your Resurrection.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Rejoice, O holy Zion,* mother of the churches, the dwelling-place of God!* For you were first to receive the forgiveness of sins through the Resurrection.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
The Word, born of God the Father before the ages,* and in the latter times voluntarily incarnate from the Most Pure,* endured the Cross and death,* and saved the human race dead of old.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: O most glorious martyrs): O Father Gregory,* the grave, that spokesman of oblivion, did not close your lips;* for you appeared as the mouth of theology,* the trumpet of piety and the lyre of the mysteries.* Therefore we honor you and celebrate your memory.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Father Gregory,* from the malice and treachery of the flesh* you wisely withdrew;* and having mounted the chariot drawn to heaven* by the team of the virtues,* you were lifted up to the heights of contemplation,* from which you pour forth theology upon us.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
O Father Gregory,* you were a faithful mediator* between God and mankind by grace;* and now you entreat for reconciliation,* beseeching Christ to grant* great mercy to our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 2, Podoben: With what garlands of praise): Come, with sacred songs let us praise the divinely eloquent one:* the sleepless eye of grace and the all-wise mouth of the Spirit,* the bright luminary of the Church of Christ;* for with the sword of his words* he cuts down the weeds of heresy* and implants the dogmas of piety.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

With spiritual love let us today hymn the hierarch,* of one mind with the Apostles and champion of piety,* the flood of divine eloquence, the torrent of theology.* For with the stream of his words* he waters the rational fields of the Church* and bears abundant fruit of Orthodox teaching.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

With fitting songs let us all praise the Theologian:* guardian of Christ's flock, hunter of wolves, and all-wise pursuer of foxes;* for by the net of his words* he drew the nations from the deep of ignorance* and offered them as a pure sacrifice to the Trinity.

Glory (Tone 8): Your tireless tongue for instruction,* finding an echo in the ears of the heart,* rouses the souls of the lazy;* and your labors shine forth from heaven, enlightening the world.* Standing now before Christ with boldness, O Gregory,* pray for those who faithfully celebrate* your all-venerable and sacred memory.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 8): The King of heaven, out of love for mankind, appeared on earth* and dwelt among men;* for having taken flesh from a pure Virgin* and come forth from her as incarnate,* He is the one Son, twofold in nature but not in person.* Therefore, confessing Him as perfect God and perfect Man,* we confess Christ our God.* Entreat Him, O unwedded Mother,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8
(Tone 8): You ascended the Cross, O Jesus, having descended from heaven!* You came to death, O immortal Life,* the true Light for those in darkness,* the Resurrection of all — O Savior, glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

We glorify Christ, risen from the dead.* He accepted soul and body,* and in His suffering separated the one from the other.* His most pure soul descended to Hades and plundered it;* His holy body knew no corruption in the tomb.* He is the Redeemer of our souls.
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.
With psalms and songs we glorify, O Savior,* Your Resurrection from the dead,* by which You delivered us from the torments of Hades* and, as God, granted eternal life and great mercy.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.

O Master of all, unapproachable One!* Creator of heaven and earth!* Through the Cross You suffered and bestowed on me impassibility;* accepting burial, You raised up with Yourself the prisoners of Hades,* as the all-powerful God and Lover of mankind.

Glory (Tone 8): Cultivating the hearts of the faithful with your tongue, O Gregory,* you raised for God the ever-blooming fruits of piety,* and the tree of theology, planted by you, bears fruit abundantly.* Therefore, standing now before Christ, pray for us* who celebrate your sacred memory.

Now and ever (Theotokion): O unwedded Virgin, who ineffably conceived God in the flesh, O Mother of God Most High! Accept the prayers of Your servants, O all-immaculate one, granting to all the cleansing of transgressions. Now, receiving our supplications, pray for the salvation of us all.

(Tone 8): Your Cross, O Lord, is life and protection for Your people,* and trusting in it, we hymn You, our crucified God.* Have mercy on us.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
Your burial, O Master, opened paradise to the human race.* Delivered from corruption, we glorify You.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
With the Father and the Spirit, let us praise Christ risen from the dead* and cry out to Him: You are our life and resurrection — have mercy on us.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): You were vested with the apostolic and hierarchical dignity of grace,* O wise Xenophon, enlightening all with the light of your teachings;* you faithfully proclaimed the life-giving Trinity* and offered your words as nourishment of immortality.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Having despised the corruptible and transient things of this life,* you wisely sought the incorruptible treasures of the Kingdom,* O blessed Xenophon,* together with your sons and your wife,* who followed the narrow path that leads to eternal life.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having renounced the world and its allurements,* you labored in fasting and prayer,* mortifying the passions and illumining your soul with divine virtues,* O all-praised ones.* Now with boldness you stand before Christ,* praying for those who honor your memory.
Glory, now and ever: The angelic hosts marveled at You, O Christ,* as in the Jordan You were baptized for our sake.* Now have mercy on us and save our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): O Lord, wishing to save the world You created from deception,* You accepted the form of a servant from the pure Virgin.* And having descended into Hades, You captured death* and on the third day rose from the dead,* granting great mercy to the world.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.
O Lord, who went down into Hades and despoiled it:* You rose as God on the third day in glory,* and raised us up together with You,* granting the world great mercy.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who well contended and were crowned:* pray to the Lord that our souls be saved.

Glory, now and ever: O Theotokos, you are the true vine that budded forth the Fruit of life.* We implore you, O Lady: intercede together with the Apostles and all the saints* that our souls may receive mercy.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You were called): Illumined by the rays of the Spirit,* you, O God-bearing John Chrysostom,* became a golden-mouthed trumpet of theology.* You instructed the universe by the wealth of your words,* teaching every soul to seek the imperishable riches.* Therefore we celebrate your translation, O Father,* glorifying Christ who glorified you.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

With the golden ray of the Spirit,* you illumined the whole universe, O blessed one;* for your tongue, adorned by grace,* became a source of divine teachings.* You are a father of orphans, a support for widows,* a rebuker of kings, a consoler of the afflicted.* We celebrate your translation, O golden-tongued hierarch.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

You adorned the Church of Christ with dogmas of Orthodoxy* and made it beautiful with the splendor of your words, O Father.* You rebuked the heresies of the impious* and confirmed the faithful in the true confession.* Now transferred to the heavenly altar,* intercede for those who honor your translation with love.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having received the divine fire of the Spirit upon your tongue,* you burned up the thorns of heresy, O God-bearing Father.* You planted the seeds of true faith in the hearts of the faithful* and by your teaching led them to the pastures of paradise.* Therefore we celebrate your sacred translation,* O John, the glory of hierarchs.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

(Tone 2): O venerable Father,* you heard the voice of the Gospel of the Lord and abandoned the world,* counting riches and glory as nothing.* Therefore you cried out to all:* Love God, and you will find eternal grace.* Place nothing above His love.* That when He comes in His glory,* we may find rest with all the saints.* Their prayers, O Christ, grant us and save our souls.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O venerable Father,* you heard the voice of the Gospel of the Lord and abandoned the world,* counting riches and glory as nothing.* Therefore you cried out to all:* Love God, and you will find eternal grace.* Place nothing above His love.* That when He comes in His glory,* we may find rest with all the saints.* Their prayers, O Christ, grant us and save our souls.

Glory (Tone 6): O man of God and faithful servant,* minister of the Lord and man of desires,* chosen vessel and pillar and foundation of the Church,* heir of the Kingdom!* Do not cease to cry out to the Lord for us.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 4): The Prophet David, who through you became an ancestor of God,* prophetically sang of you to Him who worked great things in you:* The Queen stood at Your right hand.* For God made you a life-giving Mother,* since without a father He willed to be born of you,* to restore His own image, corrupted by the passions,* and having found the lost sheep,* wandering on the mountains, to carry it on His shoulders* and bring it to the Father,* and to unite it to the heavenly powers* by His own will, O Theotokos,* and to save the world,* Christ who has great and rich mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5, Podoben: Rejoice): Rejoice, O golden-mouthed Father,* whose tongue, sweeter than honey, poured forth streams of divine wisdom.* Your words were a net that caught souls for Christ from the sea of ignorance.* Rejoice, O pillar of the Church and luminary of the world,* whose relics, returning to the royal city,* filled all the faithful with gladness.* Entreat Christ* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse: My mouth shall speak wisdom,* and the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.

Rejoice, O treasury of the divine gifts,* for you poured forth as from a spring the waters of divine teachings,* nourishing the faithful who draw from you the living waters of theology.* The royal city, receiving your sacred relics, rejoices* and glorifies Christ, who glorified you.* Entreat Him* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom,* and his tongue speaks what is right.

Rejoice, O John, sun of the universe,* whose radiance of teachings illumines all the ends of the earth.* Darkness flees before you as before the rising dawn.* You silenced the mouths of heretics with the sword of the Spirit.* And now, translated to the heavenly altar,* you intercede for the world.* Entreat Christ* to grant our souls great mercy.

Glory: O golden-mouthed John,* trumpet of grace and mouth of the Spirit,* depth of theology and height of contemplation!* You illumined the world with your teachings* and led the faithful to the pastures of the heavenly Kingdom.* Now standing before the throne of the King of glory,* pray for our souls.
Now and ever: We call you blessed, O Theotokos Virgin,* and we glorify You, O faithful, as is fitting,* the unshakable city, the indestructible wall,* the firm protectress,* the refuge of our souls.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): Enriched with the divine gifts of the Spirit,* O venerable Father Ephrem,* you became a pillar of the Church and a lamp of the world.* You illumined the faithful with the brightness of your teachings* and led the erring to the path of salvation.* Now dwell in the mansions of the blessed.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Burning with divine desire,* you withdrew to the desert, O blessed Ephrem,* where you labored in fasting and prayer,* shedding tears as streams.* Your words, sweeter than honey,* console all who read them,* and your prayers ascend to God as fragrant incense.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Having acquired the treasure of humility,* you were enriched with the grace of the Spirit, O Ephrem.* You composed hymns of compunction* that move the hearts of the faithful to repentance.* Now, standing before the throne of the Almighty,* pray that we too may find mercy at the dread judgment.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8): Your life shone forth as a great luminary,* illumining all who walked in the darkness of sin, O Father.* By your compunctionate words, you pierced the hearts of the hardened* and led them to the springs of repentance.* Therefore the Church honors your memory with joy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The desert rejoiced, receiving you, O blessed one,* for you adorned it with the flowers of your virtues.* You made it a paradise of prayer and a garden of the Spirit,* where the streams of your teaching water the souls of the faithful.* Now dwell in the true paradise of Christ.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O Father Ephrem, harp of the Holy Spirit,* your writings are a treasury of wisdom* and a spring of consolation for the afflicted.* Your compunctionate prayers teach us to weep for our sins* and seek the mercy of the Lord.* Intercede for us, O blessed one.

Glory (Tone 8): O venerable Father Ephrem,* you walked in the footsteps of the ancient Fathers* and emulated their virtues.* The grace of the Holy Spirit, dwelling in you,* made you a teacher of repentance and a preacher of compunction.* Now standing before Christ, pray for our souls.

Now and ever (Theotokion): O immaculate Virgin, Mother of God,* who bore the eternal Light in your womb,* illuminate our souls darkened by sin* and lead us to the path of salvation.* For we have no other intercessor before God* more powerful than you, O Lady.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): O Lord, who for our sake accepted the suffering of the Cross* and rose from the dead on the third day,* strengthen our hearts in the true faith* and guide our steps on the path of Your commandments.* For You alone are merciful and the Lover of mankind.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.

O Christ, who descended to Hades and despoiled it,* who rose on the third day and ascended to the Father:* send down upon us the grace of the All-Holy Spirit* and enlighten the eyes of our hearts.* O Lover of mankind, have mercy on us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, intercessors for the human race!* By your sufferings you obtained boldness before the Lord.* Therefore pray that our souls be delivered from every affliction.
Glory, now and ever: O all-immaculate Theotokos,* do not despise us who need your help,* but intercede for us before your Son and our God,* that He may save our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 5)
O God, by Your name save me, and by Your power defend me.
Verse: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4, Podoben: You gave as a sign): The translation of the relics of the holy martyr Ignatius* fills the Church with gladness today.* For he who was called the God-bearer* and bore Christ in his heart* now returns to the faithful as a source of healing and grace.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Having burned with the fire of divine love,* O God-bearing Ignatius, you cried out:* I am the wheat of God, ground by the teeth of beasts,* that I may become the pure bread of Christ.* By such a death you were glorified, O blessed one.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

A disciple of the Apostles* and a teacher of divine dogmas,* you shepherded the flock of Christ in Antioch, O Father.* You wrote epistles aflame with love for God* that still warm the hearts of the faithful.* Intercede for us, O hierarch and martyr.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8): The memory of the just is celebrated with praise,* and for you, O God-bearer, the testimony of the Lord suffices.* For you were truly shown to be the God-bearers' first,* having spoken with the Apostles* and having drunk the cup of Christ's sufferings,* O Ignatius, glory of martyrs.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O wise Father Ignatius,* you bore the name of the God-bearer worthily,* for Christ was inscribed upon your heart.* Led to the beasts as to a feast,* you rejoiced, counting it gain to depart and be with Christ.* We honor your courage and magnify your faith.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Come, O faithful, let us honor the God-bearing Ignatius,* who watered the Church with the rivers of his blood* and nourished it with the bread of his teachings.* His relics, translated to the city of Antioch,* pour forth healings upon the faithful.* Pray for us, O hierarch and martyr.
Glory, now and ever: Seeing You crucified, O Christ,* Your Mother cried out: What strange mystery do I behold, O my Son?* How is it that You, who give life to all, die in the flesh upon the tree?
Now and ever: O most pure Theotokos,* who contained the uncontainable God in your womb,* contain our wandering thoughts* and direct them toward the path of salvation.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8
(Tone 8): Your Cross, O Lord, is life and protection for Your people,* and trusting in it, we hymn You, our crucified God.* Have mercy on us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.
Your burial, O Master, opened paradise to the human race.* Delivered from corruption, we glorify You.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who did not deny Christ* nor depart from His commandments:* by your prayers, have mercy on us.

Glory, now and ever: Seeing You crucified, O Christ,* Your Mother cried out:* What strange mystery do I behold, O my Son?* How is it that You die on the tree, suspended in the flesh,* O Giver of life?

troparia.x: Troparia
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Verse: I lift up my eyes to the hills; from where does my help come?

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

(Tone 4): O Christ, when the three holy hierarchs,* Basil the Great, Gregory the Theologian, and John Chrysostom,* are gathered in one celebration,* they illumine the whole inhabited world* with the brilliant light of the Trinity.* Their prayers save all the faithful.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

The three great luminaries of the three-sunned Godhead* dispersed the darkness of ignorance with divine dogmas.* They are the golden channels of wisdom,* through which the whole creation is watered with the streams of divine knowledge.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

The three great hierarchs fought the good fight:* Basil, the great and wise; Gregory, the theologian divine;* and John of the golden mouth.* They adorned the Church with teachings and destroyed all heresies.* Together let us honor them in one feast.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Equal in grace and equal in honor,* the three hierarchs shine with one radiance.* They are the three pillars of the Church,* the three fountains of divine wisdom,* the three defenders of the Orthodox faith.* With one voice they proclaim the Holy Trinity.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 1): Basil planted the garden of theology;* Gregory cultivated it with the hoe of wisdom;* and John reaped the golden harvest of divine eloquence.* Together they nourish the Church* with the bread of life and the wine of gladness.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O all-praised hierarchs!* You are the three rivers of paradise,* watering the whole world with the streams of divine teaching.* You proclaimed the one God in Trinity and the Trinity in Unity.* By your prayers, strengthen us in the Orthodox faith.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The sounding trumpets of the Spirit,* the golden-mouthed heralds of the Gospel,* the bright-shining lamps of the Church:* Basil, Gregory, and John!* Come, O faithful, and let us celebrate their common feast,* for together they intercede before Christ for our souls.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Let us unite in one chorus, O faithful,* and praise with one voice the three great Fathers:* Basil the God-wise, Gregory the Theologian,* and golden-tongued John.* They defended the faith, adorned the Church,* and now stand before the throne of the Holy Trinity,* praying for the salvation of our souls.

Glory (Tone 4): These three luminaries of the triple-sunned Godhead* enkindled the world with the rays of divine teachings,* rivers of honey pouring forth wisdom* that gladden the whole face of the Church.* O Basil, Gregory, and John,* standing now before the throne of the Most Holy Trinity,* pray for us who celebrate your feast with love.

Now and ever: The prophecies concerning you are fulfilled, O Theotokos Virgin,* for the prophets foretold you and the Apostles proclaimed you.* Angels and men glorify you,* for you brought forth God in the flesh for the salvation of the world.* Entreat Him, O Lady, that He may have mercy on us.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 1): The three great hierarchs,* luminaries of the inhabited world and teachers of the universe,* guide the Church with their teachings as with reins* and adorn it with the beauty of divine wisdom.* Let us honor them with fitting praises.
Verse: My mouth shall speak wisdom,* and the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.

Basil, the great among the saints,* Gregory, the all-wise theologian,* and John, the golden trumpet of Christ:* together they proclaimed the dogma of the Trinity* and enlightened the faithful with the light of true theology.
Verse: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom,* and his tongue speaks what is right.

O thrice-blessed hierarchs,* co-enthroned with the Apostles and equal in honor with the martyrs:* the Church celebrates your common feast* and glorifies the Holy Trinity whom you preached.* Pray that our souls be saved.

Glory (Tone 3): Let us honor the three great hierarchs with songs and hymns:* for they extinguished the darkness of heresy with the triple-sunned radiance of their teachings.* They are the rivers of wisdom, who watered the whole creation* with the streams of divine knowledge.* They are the intercessors for our souls before the throne of Christ.

Now and ever: Without seed, by the will of the Father, from the Divine Spirit* you conceived the Son of God,* who eternally was from the Father without a mother,* but for our sake came to be from you without a father.* You bore Him in the flesh and nursed Him as a child with milk.* Therefore, do not cease to pray* that our souls be delivered from all afflictions.

Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.

More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 7)
O God, You are my defender, and Your mercy shall come to meet me.
Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: Of the heavenly choirs): Divinely inspired by the rays of the Spirit,* O wonder-working Cyrus and John,* you healed every illness and infirmity,* freely giving what you freely received.* Having suffered martyrdom for Christ,* you now intercede for those who honor your memory.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Unmercenary physicians of the sick,* you healed bodies and souls together,* pouring forth the grace of healings without charge.* Having endured the torments of martyrdom,* you were crowned by Christ with imperishable crowns.* Now pray for those who celebrate your feast.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Armed with the Cross as with an invincible weapon,* you overcame the power of the enemy,* O all-praised Cyrus and John.* You healed the bodies of the sick and enlightened the souls of sinners,* leading them from darkness to the light of Christ.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 8): The Church of Christ is adorned today* with the luminous feast of the unmercenary healers.* Come, O faithful, and let us honor them,* for they poured forth healings without charge* and suffered for Christ without hesitation.* O Cyrus and John, pray for our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

You rivaled the Apostles in zeal, O blessed ones,* and the martyrs in courage.* You healed the sick without payment and preached Christ without fear.* Therefore the Lord crowned you with a double crown* of healers and martyrs.* O Cyrus and John, intercede for us.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O holy unmercenary healers,* springs of healings that never run dry!* You freely received the gift of healing from Christ* and freely gave it to the suffering.* Now, dwelling in the heavenly mansions,* pour forth your grace upon us who celebrate your memory.

Glory (Tone 2): Enriched with the gifts of the Spirit,* O Cyrus and John, all-praised unmercenary healers,* you healed the infirmities of all who came to you with faith.* You suffered for Christ and received from Him imperishable crowns.* Now standing before the Lord with boldness,* pray that our souls may be saved.
Now and ever (Theotokion): O impassable Gate, mystically sealed, O blessed Theotokos Virgin, receive our prayers and bring them to Your Son and God: that through You He may save our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): O Lord who loves mankind, great is the power of Your Cross!* For it was planted on Golgotha and it acts in all the world.* It made fishermen into Apostles and pagans into the faithful.* Therefore we all glorify You, O Savior. Have mercy on us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.

O Lord, You accepted suffering on the Cross,* to save us from slavery to the enemy.* You were pierced in the side* and poured forth a fountain of forgiveness.* You were nailed in the hands* and drew all nations to the knowledge of Your divine authority.* O Savior Christ, have mercy on us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who contended well and were crowned:* pray to the Lord that He may have mercy on our souls.

Glory, now and ever: O Theotokos, you are the true vine that budded forth the Fruit of life.* We implore you, O Lady: intercede together with the Apostles and all the saints* that our souls may receive mercy.

troparia.x: Troparia
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
