prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 1): Accept our evening prayers,* O holy Lord,* and grant us the forgiveness of sins,* for You alone* have shown the world the Resurrection.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
Encircle Zion, O peoples,* and surround it,* and give glory in it to Him who rose from the dead,* for He is our God* who has delivered us from our transgressions.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Come, O peoples, let us sing* and worship Christ,* glorifying His Resurrection from the dead,* for He is our God* who has delivered the world from the deception of the enemy.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Be glad, O heavens; let the foundations of the earth resound;* O mountains, sing with gladness!* For behold, Emmanuel has nailed our sins to the Cross;* the Giver of life has slain death* and raised Adam, as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1): Let us not pray like the Pharisee, brethren,* for whoever exalts himself will be humbled.* Therefore let us humble ourselves before God,* crying out like the Publican with fasting:* God, be merciful to us sinners.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Let us not pray like the Pharisee, brethren,* for whoever exalts himself will be humbled.* Therefore let us humble ourselves before God,* crying out like the Publican with fasting:* God, be merciful to us sinners.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The Pharisee, conquered by pride,* and the Publican, bowed down by repentance,* both approached You, the only Master.* The one lost his boasting through vainglory;* the other, silencing his lips, was counted worthy of gifts.* In these groans confirm me, O Christ God,* as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 4): The holy Church is being prepared* to receive within itself the Lord,* who comes as an infant* and by grace enriches all things with immaterial beauty.* Therefore let us with gladness of soul greet Him.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The bridal chamber of great light,* and the most precious Tabernacle,* and the holy and spacious Temple* brings the Lord into the chambers of the Temple.* Let us praise the Lord, the Savior of the world.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.
O pious Simeon, come hither* to attain your Hope of seeing Christ,* and in the temple take on your arms* the Lord of all, and cry:* Now You dismiss Your servant in peace.

Glory (Tone 8): O Lord Almighty!* I know what tears can do:* they led Hezekiah from the gates of death;* they saved the sinful woman from her years of transgression;* and the Publican was justified above the Pharisee by them.* Together with them I pray: God, be merciful to me and save me.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 1): Let us praise Mary the Virgin,* glory of the whole world, who came forth from mankind* and gave birth to the Master,* the gate of heaven, the praise of the bodiless ones, and the adornment of the faithful.* For she was revealed as heaven and the temple of the Godhead.* She destroyed the middle wall of enmity* and established peace, opening the Kingdom.* Therefore, holding her fast as the anchor of faith,* we have as our champion the Lord born of her.* Courage then, courage, O people of God!* For He will fight our foes, He the almighty.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1
(Tone 1): By Your Passion, O Christ,* we have been freed from suffering,* and by Your Resurrection we have been delivered from corruption.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

Let creation rejoice, let the heavens be glad,* and let the nations clap their hands with joy.* For Christ our Savior has nailed our sins to the Cross,* and having slain death, has given us life,* raising up fallen Adam as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Being the unapproachable King of heaven and earth,* because of Your love for mankind You willingly were crucified.* Hades, encountering You below, was embittered,* and the souls of the righteous, receiving You, rejoiced.* Adam, seeing You, the Creator, in the netherworld, rose up.* O wonder! How has the Life of all tasted death?* Having willed to enlighten the world, which cries out:* O You who rose from the dead — Lord, glory to You!
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.

The myrrh-bearing women, carrying myrrh,* hastily and with weeping came to Your tomb,* and not finding Your most pure body,* they learned from the angel the new and most glorious wonder.* They told the Apostles:* The Lord is risen, granting the world great mercy.
Glory (Tone 5): With eyes weighed down by my iniquities,* I cannot look up and behold the height of heaven;* but receive me as the Publican, O Savior, who repents,* and have mercy on me.

Now and ever (Tone 2): The sacred Virgin* brought the Sacred One to the holy temple of the priest.* And Simeon, stretching out his arms,* joyfully received Him and cried out:* Now You dismiss Your servant, O Master,* according to Your word, in peace.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 1): The stone was sealed by the Jews, soldiers guarded Your most pure body; but You arose on the third day, O Savior, granting life to the world. Therefore the powers of heaven cried to You, O Giver of life: Glory to Your resurrection, O Christ! Glory to Your kingdom! Glory to Your dispensation, O You who alone loves mankind!
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament lessons for this feast are proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
(Tone 1): Tell us, O Simeon: whom do you hold in your arms in the temple, rejoicing?* To whom do you cry out and exclaim:* Now I am set free,* for I have seen my Savior!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
Tell us, O Simeon: whom do you hold in your arms in the temple, rejoicing?* To whom do you cry out and exclaim:* Now I am set free,* for I have seen my Savior!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities,* who could stand, O Lord? But with You there is forgiveness.

Receive, O Simeon, into your arms* Him whom Moses beheld in the cloud on Sinai when He gave the Law,* and who has now, as an infant, submitted to the Law.* This is He of whom the Law spoke.* This is He whom the Prophets proclaimed:* He who became incarnate for our sake and saved mankind.* Let us worship Him.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Come, and let us too, with pious songs, go to meet Christ,* and receive Him as our Savior, whom Simeon saw.* This is He whom David proclaimed;* this is He who spoke through the prophets,* who became incarnate for our sake and speaks to us through the Law.* Let us worship Him.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
(Tone 2): He before whom the heavenly ministers pray with trembling* was today taken bodily into the arms of Simeon on earth,* who proclaimed Him to be God before the ages* and Lord of the world.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.
He before whom the heavenly ministers pray with trembling* was today taken bodily into the arms of Simeon on earth,* who proclaimed Him to be God before the ages* and Lord of the world.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Now Simeon takes on his arms the Lord of glory,* whom Moses once beheld in a cloud on Mount Sinai,* as He gave the tablets of the Law.* This is He of whom the prophets spoke.* This is He who was incarnate for our sake and saved mankind.* Let us worship Him.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

The holy Virgin brought the All-Holy One to the temple of the holy priest.* And Simeon, stretching out his arms, joyfully received Him* and cried out: Now You dismiss Your servant in peace, O Master,* according to Your word.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): Let the gates of heaven be opened today,* for the eternal Word of the Father, taking a beginning in time,* yet not departing from His divinity,* is willingly brought by the Virgin Mother as a forty-day-old infant* to the temple of the Law.* And the Elder receives Him in his arms, crying out:* Now dismiss Your servant,* for my eyes have seen Your salvation.* O Lord who came into the world to save the human race — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7

(Tone 7): Adorn your bridal chamber, O Zion, to receive Christ the King!* Greet Mary, the heavenly gate,* for she has appeared as a cherubic throne;* she carries the King of glory.* The Virgin is a cloud of light,* bearing in her flesh the Son who existed before the morning star.* Receiving Him in his arms, Simeon proclaimed to the peoples:* This is the Lord of the living and the dead,* the Savior of the world — glory to Him!
Verse: Now You dismiss Your servant, O Master,* according to Your word, in peace.

He who shone before the ages from the Father* and finally came forth from a virgin womb,* and He who on Sinai gave the Law to Moses* and kept the prescriptions of human nature:* let us, assembling, hymn Him with songs.
Verse: A light for revelation to the Gentiles* and for glory to Your people Israel.

He whom the Cherubim carry in their chariot and the Seraphim praise in song* is today carried in the arms of the Virgin Mother Mary,* the God-bearer.* It is God who is born of her.* Come, let us praise Him with songs.

Come, and let us too, with pious songs, go to meet Christ,* and receive Him as our Savior, whom Simeon saw.* This is He whom David proclaimed.* This is He who spoke through the prophets.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 2): Today Simeon takes the Lord of glory on his arms,* whom once in the cloud Moses beheld on Sinai.* He is the one who speaks to us through the prophets.* He is the Creator of the Law and the Fulfiller of the Law.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!
Today Simeon takes the Lord of glory on his arms,* whom once in the cloud Moses beheld on Sinai.* He is the one who speaks to us through the prophets.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.
Now Simeon takes on his arms the Lord of glory,* whom Moses once beheld in a cloud on Mount Sinai,* as He gave the tablets of the Law.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): Let the gates of heaven be opened today,* for the eternal Word of the Father, taking a beginning in time,* yet not departing from His divinity,* is willingly brought by the Virgin Mother as a forty-day-old infant* to the temple of the Law.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): Adorn your bridal chamber, O Zion, to receive Christ the King!* Greet Mary, the heavenly gate,* for she has appeared as a cherubic throne,* carrying the King of glory.
Verse: Now You dismiss Your servant, O Master,* according to Your word, in peace.
He who shone before the ages from the Father* and finally came forth from a virgin womb:* let us hymn Him with songs.
Verse: A light for revelation to the Gentiles* and for glory to Your people Israel.
He whom the Cherubim carry in their chariot and the Seraphim praise in song* is today carried in the arms of the Virgin Mother Mary, the God-bearer.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 8): He who is borne upon the Cherubim and praised by the Seraphim* is today brought to the holy temple according to the Law,* and sits on the arms of the Elder as on a throne.

(Tone 2, Podoben: When from the tree): Having surpassed all in sins,* from whom shall I learn repentance?* Where shall I find tears?* I mourn and weep, remembering the inextinguishable fire,* and the outer darkness, and the worm,* and the gnashing of teeth.* But deliver me, O Lord, from all these* and grant me repentance.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

I cry to You with pain, O Savior,* receive me who repent, and deliver me from the fire.* For the Publican groaned and was justified;* the Prodigal Son received his Father's mercy;* and the sinful woman, when she wept, obtained forgiveness.* But I, O Lord, have surpassed them all in sins.* Have mercy on me, O God.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O my wretched soul, the end is near and you do not care or prepare.* The time is short — arise!* The Judge is near, even at the door.* Like a dream, like a flower, the time of this life passes.* Why do we trouble ourselves in vain?
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

(Tone 6): O Hieromartyr Haralampius,* you planted faith in Christ in the hearts of the faithful,* uprooting the weeds of impiety.* Having been sprinkled with your own blood,* you nourished the shoots of piety.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!
The radiance of your miracles, O Father Haralampius,* illumines all who come to you with faith.* You drive away the darkness of afflictions* and grant healing to the sick.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O glorious Haralampius,* you endured every torment for Christ:* the scraping of the body, the burning of the skin,* and finally the cutting of the head.* Now in heaven you stand before Christ,* praying for our souls.
Glory, now and ever: O Gate of the Lord,* the prophets proclaimed you.* Through you the only Lord passed,* yet the seal remained unbroken.* O Theotokos, pray for the salvation of our souls.

(Tone 2): I will arise and return to my merciful Father,* crying with tears: Receive me as one of Your hired servants,* for I have sinned before You, O Lord.* But take pity on me and have mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in the heavens. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy on us.
Your inheritance, O Father, I have squandered in debauchery,* and barren of virtues, I cry out in hunger:* O merciful Father, meet me and have mercy on me.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has had more than its fill of the scorn of the complacent, the contempt of the proud.
I stand before Your doors, O Lord,* and do not dare to raise my eyes.* But if You receive the Publican, accept also me, a sinner,* and have mercy on me.
Glory, now and ever: Rejoice, O full of grace, the Lord is with you;* the angel said to the Virgin.* Rejoice, O Pure One! Rejoice, O Blessed One!* The Lord is with you, and through you He is with us.

(Tone 8): O sacred hieromartyr Blaise,* you quenched the flame of falsehood* and kindled the fire of divine love in the hearts of the faithful.* You healed the sick and endured martyrdom.* Now standing before the Lord, pray for our souls.

Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

With hierarchical dignity and the crown of martyrdom* you are adorned, O Blaise, divine minister.* You preached the Gospel fearlessly* and sealed your confession with your blood.* O intercessor for our souls, pray for us.

Glory, now and ever: The shadow of the Law has passed* since grace has come.* For as the bush burned yet was not consumed,* so the Virgin gave birth yet remained a Virgin.* Instead of a pillar of fire, the Sun of righteousness has risen.* Instead of Moses — Christ, the salvation of our souls.

(Tone 3): Before the end comes, my soul,* hasten to gain the richness of good works.* For You alone know* how many of those who wait will not complete the course.* The Bridegroom is near — awake!* Let the lamp not go out for lack of oil,* lest you be shut out like the five foolish virgins.* But watch, O my soul, and cry out:* Open to me, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): O Theotokos, you are the true vine* that brought forth the Fruit of life.* We implore you:* intercede, O Lady, with the Apostles and all the Saints,* that our souls may be granted mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): Fasting from food, O my soul,* yet not cleansing yourself from the passions,* you rejoice in vain with idleness.* For the fast will become worthless* if there is no amendment of life.* God will despise you as deceitful.* Imitate therefore the angels who never eat,* and cry out to the Lord:* Be merciful to me, a sinner.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The time of fasting has come,* the beginning of spiritual warfare.* Let us set out on this journey with joy,* fasting from food and passions,* that we may reach* the life-giving Third Day of Resurrection.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

O all-praised martyrs!* The earth did not cover you,* but heaven received you.* The gates of paradise opened for you,* and entering therein you partake of the tree of life.* Pray to Christ to grant our souls* peace and great mercy.
Glory, now and ever: O Immaculate One, unsearchable is the depth* of the wonder of your birthgiving, O most holy.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): We flee to your protection, O holy Theotokos.* Despise not our petitions in adversity,* but deliver us from danger,* O only pure and only blessed one.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): The arena of virtues has been opened.* Let those who wish to compete enter therein,* girding themselves for the good struggle of the fast.* For those who compete lawfully are justly crowned.* Taking up the armor of the Cross,* let us wage war against the enemy.* We have an impregnable wall — faith,* prayer as a breastplate, and almsgiving as a helmet.* Instead of a sword we have fasting,* which cuts away every evil from the heart.* Whoever does these things receives the true crown* from the King of all, Christ, on the Day of Judgment.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The arena of virtues has been opened.* Let those who wish to compete enter therein,* girding themselves for the good struggle of the fast.* For those who compete lawfully are justly crowned.* Taking up the armor of the Cross,* let us wage war against the enemy.* We have an impregnable wall — faith,* prayer as a breastplate, and almsgiving as a helmet.* Instead of a sword we have fasting,* which cuts away every evil from the heart.* Whoever does these things receives the true crown* from the King of all, Christ, on the Day of Judgment.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

Prophets, apostles of Christ, and martyrs* have taught us to hymn the consubstantial Trinity.* They have enlightened the nations that had gone astray,* making the children of men fellow citizens with the angels.

Glory, now and ever: O Lady, forget not your humble servants,* but by your prayers deliver us from eternal punishment* and from every threatening evil,* and turn from us, O Theotokos, the wrath of the Lord.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 1)
Your mercy, O Lord, shall be with me all the days of my life.
Verse: The Lord is my Shepherd; I shall not want.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 3): O Lord, You stretched Your most pure hands upon the Cross* and embraced all the ends of the earth.* Therefore I cry out to You:* Gather my scattered mind,* held captive and led astray by the passions.* Cleanse me wholly by the fast* and make me a partaker of Your Passion.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

In ancient times the fast tempered the youths* and, as it is written, made them stronger than the blazing fire.* Take up the fast, O my wretched soul,* kindling within yourself the love of the Lord,* by which you will consume the destructive passions* and escape the fire to come.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The time of the fast is a time of joy.* Therefore, sufficiently nourished* by the radiance of purity,* sincere love, the brightness of prayer,* and other virtues, let us shine forth and cry out:* O most holy Cross of Christ,* that brings us life and joy,* grant that we all may venerate you with a pure heart* and bestow on all cleansing and great mercy.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O God-wise Father Gerasimus,* on the wings of the mind* you soared to God through faith,* scorning the instability of worldly turmoil.* Taking up your cross,* you followed the All-Seeing One,* subduing the willful impulses of the flesh by temperance* and your thoughts by the power* of the Divine Spirit.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable Father Gerasimus,* dwelling constantly in the deserts, caves, and mountains* with faith,* you sought God* and found the Desired One.* Having instead of sinews the strings of an intrepid soul,* with good ascents you ever* led the ranks of monks* to be saved in Christ.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having spent your whole life* in great silence,* in sorrow and in tears,* you became a guide of monks through faith,* adorned with poverty* and embellished with temperance,* revealing yourself a stranger and pilgrim on earth.* Therefore you honorably obtained your reward,* O blessed of God.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): Beholding You, O Jesus, nailed to the Cross* and willingly accepting suffering, O Master,* the Virgin and Mother cried out:* Woe is me, O beloved Child!* What unjust wounds You endure, O Physician of human weakness,* who by Your compassion have delivered all from corruption!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 1): O Lord, crucified in the flesh,* You crucified with Yourself our old self.* Pierced in the side by a lance,* You pierced the soul-destroying serpent.* Nail my flesh with Your fear* and wound my soul with Your love,* that, gazing upon Your sufferings,* I may spend the appointed time of fasting in abstinence,* restraining not only the stomach* but also other sinful desires.* And I will offer You as sacrifice the contrition of my heart* and the humility of my spirit for all my sins,* from which deliver me, O Lover of mankind.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

By Your Cross, O Lord, You conquered death,* which entered the world through the eating of the fruit* of the forbidden tree in Paradise.* Therefore we implore You:* O Lord, in this fast turn us away from every harmful pleasure,* and grant us to acquire spiritual nourishment* which abides unto life everlasting,* that we may receive the imperishable crowns of joy* prepared for those who truly fast.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O all-praised passion-bearers,* you have appeared as luminaries for the world,* crying out to Christ: O Lord — glory to You!

Glory, now and ever: When the All-Immaculate One beheld You* willingly nailed to the Cross,* weeping she cried out: O my Child,* where has your radiant beauty gone,* with which You adorned human nature?

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 5)
O God, by Your name save me, and by Your power defend me.
Verse: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 3): Great and fearful will be Your coming, O Lord,* when You sit down to render righteous judgment.* Do not condemn me who deserve punishment,* but as God, spare me for the sake of the favorable prayers of Your Apostles.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
By the prayers of Your divine Apostles, O Lord,* grant us to complete with a repentant heart the time of the fast as is fitting,* that we all who are saved may glorify You,* O good and merciful One.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Apostles of Christ,* you are luminaries for those on earth,* most rich treasures of the wise knowledge of our God.* By your holy prayers free from temptations those who sing your praises* and give us strength to zealously spend the time of fasting* and to live in peace,* that we may piously behold Christ's Passion,* boldly offering hymns to our God.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O unerring and divine star,* radiant light of God's commandments* and wondrous passion-bearer ever-memorable among the martyrs!* You drove away the mist of darkness* like a light-bearing morning star, O blessed one.* O goodly offering* and spotless sacrifice!* Therefore ceaselessly pray to Christ for the salvation of our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O preacher of the truth!* Appearing as a sword-bearing warrior* against impiety,* by your suffering you cut off the heads of the enemies, O most wise one,* and clearly proclaimed the divine commandment,* exhorting the untaught people to what is better.* O Conon, fellow-citizen of the martyrs!* Pray to the Deliverer that He deliver* His servants, your singers, from the passions.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O good change* wrought by the right hand of the Most High* upon you, O all-glorious passion-bearer!* From an unbelieving root you appeared as a blossoming fruit,* O Conon, all-blessed and all-praised.* Having Christ as your supreme commander,* you vanquished the legions of demons* with sovereign power.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Rejoice, O Chariot of the divine Light!* Rejoice, O Temple and Tabernacle of sanctification of the Lord!* Light has dawned upon us* from your most pure womb,* illuminating the ends of the earth* and sanctifying us by grace.* Rejoice, O Foundation of salvation, O Most Pure One!* Rejoice, O awesome Proclamation and Praise* of those who hope in you!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): Having entered the spiritual fast, O brethren,* let us not speak deceitful words* nor give our brother occasion for stumbling,* but kindling the lamp of the soul with repentance,* let us cry out with tears to Christ:* O Lover of mankind, forgive us our trespasses.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The labor of fasting is for us a time of repentance* and a step toward acquiring life eternal,* especially if we stretch out a hand of charity.* Nothing so saves the soul as helping the poor.* Almsgiving, joined with fasting, delivers one from death.* Let us love it, for nothing is its equal;* it suffices to save our souls.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

Duly putting on the breastplate of faith* and arming yourselves with the Cross,* you became valiant warriors,* bravely opposing the tormentors,* overthrowing the deception of the devil,* and becoming victors — you were deemed worthy of crowns.* Therefore ever pray for us* that our souls may be saved.
Glory, now and ever: O Virgin, holy Theotokos!* Accept me under your protection,* for I know that in you is my salvation* and you alone can help me, O Most Pure One.

My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Verse: I lift up my eyes to the hills; from where does my help come?

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 3): Pierced between the ribs* and lifted upon the tree, You poured out life for me,* who was put to death by the malice of the serpent and the eating from the tree.* Therefore I glorify You, O Christ,* and beseech Your mercy:* Allow me to complete the arena of the fast in repentance* and to worship Your Passion and Resurrection.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

You banished from Paradise the first-created human,* who in the garden plucked the fruit of sorrow.* But, nailed to the Cross in earthly fashion, O Savior, You again led her back.* Therefore I cry out to You:* Free me today from my afflictions* and cleanse me through fasting and tears of repentance,* O Jesus most good, Savior of our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, we hymn* Your life-giving Cross,* for by putting death to death* it caused life to spring forth in the world.* With it we come to prayer:* Strengthen us, the fasters, in this holy time* and give us power against the passions and victory over all that is earthly,* in Your great mercy, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Shining forth in these latter times,* O victorious martyrs,* like unquenchable stars on the holy firmament of the Church,* by the radiance of your sufferings* you have illumined all that is under the sun* and dispelled the darkness of deception, O all-praised ones.* And now you have passed to the eternal Light.* Therefore with faith we commemorate your luminous and sacred struggle,* enriched by your good intercession.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Forcibly led away as captives,* you were imprisoned, O martyrs of Christ,* and confined in a dungeon for a long time,* truly divine guardians of the faith.* From there the furious beast of evil name,* having led you out, slew you with the sword,* for you refused to submit to his wicked commands.* And now you have joyfully inherited the mansions of the Kingdom on high,* forty-two in number,* O all-glorious ones!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

With Constantine, Bassoë, and Callistus,* let us all praise Theophilus and Theodore* and the rest of the divine company of passion-bearers with loud voices;* for they chose to die for the Life of all,* going gladly to the slaughter.* And now they rest in the City of the Living God* and pray that we may find* remission of sins on the Day of Judgment* and final redemption.

Let us now worthily honor Tarasius,* the true minister and hierarch of Christ,* who received from Him the grace of the Spirit,* the wise archpastor,* for he has boldness to pray before God* that we may be saved.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): Seeing then the Ewe-Lamb her own Lamb* hastening to death,* she followed quickly after Him and said to Him:* Where are You going, my sweetest Child, O Christ?* Why do You walk this road without delay, O Long-Suffering One?* O Jesus most dear, sinless and most merciful Lord!* Speak a word to me, Your handmaid, my beloved Son,* and do not dismiss me with silence, O Merciful One,* who wondrously gave You birth, O God and Giver of life,* who grants the world great mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2

(Tone 2): Not having fasted as the Creator commanded,* our first parents reaped death from the tree through disobedience* and lost the tree of Life and the delight of Paradise.* Therefore let us abstain, O faithful, from corruptible foods* and avoid destructive passions,* that from the divine Cross we may receive life* and with the wise thief return to our original homeland,* receiving from Christ God great mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

This present fast puts the passions to death* and promises healing to those wounded by sin.* Let us honor it as a helper given to us by God.* Let us accept it as Moses accepted the God-inscribed tablets of the Law.* Let us not give precedence to intemperance, which shattered them.* Let us not share the fate of those whose bodies perished in the wilderness.* Let us not grieve like the Jews but rejoice in the manner of the Church.* Let us not be hypocrites like the Pharisees but conduct ourselves according to the Gospel,* boasting in the Cross of Christ, the Savior of our souls.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

How shall we call you, O Saints?* Cherubim? For Christ rested upon you.* Seraphim? For you ceaselessly glorified Him.* Or perhaps angels? For you turned away from the body.* Or powers? For you work wonders.* Many are your names and still more your gifts!* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever: How strange and wonderful this is!* cried the Virgin in a motherly way to the Lord.* The pangs which I did not endure at Your birth, O Son,* now sharply pierce my heart.* I cannot bear to see You on the Cross, O Light of my eyes!* But hasten to arise, and I will glorify with all the world Your wondrous Providence.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 7)
O God, You are my defender, and Your mercy shall come to meet me.
Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
(Tone 5): Today is the favorable time,* today is the day of salvation!* Out of Your great mercy, visit my soul,* and lighten the burden of my lawlessness,* O only Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

You became fellow citizens of the angels, O holy martyrs,* fearlessly confessing Christ before the tribunal.* You forgot the transient beauty of the world, keeping faith as a sure anchor.* Therefore, having driven away deception, you bestow upon the faithful gifts of healing,* praying unceasingly that our souls may be saved.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

How can one not marvel at your struggles, O holy martyrs?* Clothed in mortal flesh, you conquered the bodiless foes.* The threats of the tormentors did not frighten you,* the intensified tortures did not terrify you.* Truly you were worthily glorified by Christ.* Therefore entreat great mercy for our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Invincible martyrs,* all-praised hierarchs, and luminaries of the world,* immovable pillars of the divine Church,* the foundation of doctrines,* the leaders of the pious,* and the persecutors of deception you have become, O heaven-minded fathers,* light-bearers of our souls,* companions of the angels,* champions of the Trinity.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Ephrem the praise-worthy,* Basil the God-wise,* Capiton the great,* divine Agathodoruswith Elpidius, Etherius, and renowned Eugenius,* let them be magnified as blessed by God-inspired words;* for living in a venerable manner* and suffering in a holy way,* they have appeared as citizens of the Heavenly Kingdom.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having put to death the wisdom of the flesh by his labors,* praise-worthy Basil raised a dead man to life* by divine invocation.* And again Capiton, the holy shepherd,* stood in the midst of the flames rejoicing,* and they beheld him unburned.* By their prayers, O Lover of mankind,* grant us Your favor* and Your great mercy.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 5): In the Red Sea of old,* an image of the unwedded Bride was depicted.* There Moses was the divider of the waters;* here Gabriel is the minister of the miracle.* Then Israel walked through the deep as on dry land;* now the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.* The sea, after the crossing of Israel, remained impassable;* the Immaculate One, after the birth of Emmanuel, remained incorrupt.* O God, existing before the ages and appearing as man — have mercy on us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2

(Tone 2): O all-glorious martyrs!* The earth did not hide you, but heaven received you.* The gates of paradise opened for you,* and entering therein you delight in the tree of life.* Pray to Christ that He grant our souls peace* and great mercy.
Verse: Blessed are those whom You have chosen and received, O Lord.

What pleasure of this life is free from sorrow?* What glory stands unchanged upon earth?* All things are more fleeting than a shadow, all more deceptive than a dream.* A single moment — and death replaces them all.* But in the light of Your countenance and in the delight of Your beauty,* grant rest, O Christ, Lover of mankind, to those whom You have chosen.
Verse: Their souls shall dwell among good things.

None among mortals is without sin,* only You, the only Immortal One.* Therefore, as a merciful God, receive Your servants into the light,* together with Your angelic choirs,* not remembering their trespasses, and for Your compassion's sake,* grant them forgiveness.

Glory, now and ever: Immeasurably beyond understanding is the wondrous greatness of Your birthgiving, O Bride of God,* which all the prophets proclaimed.* Most glorious was both the conception and the Nativity, O All-Praised One,* — incomprehensible and ineffable,* for by it Your Son saved the world, as the Merciful One.

Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 4): By Your precious Cross, O Christ,* You put the devil to shame,* and by Your Resurrection You blunted the sting of sin* and saved us from the gates of death.* We glorify You, O Only-Begotten One.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

He who grants resurrection to the human race* was led to the sacrifice like a lamb.* The princes of Hades trembled before Him, and the gates of sorrow were opened,* for the King of Glory — Christ — entered, saying to the prisoners: Come forth!* and to those in darkness: Be revealed!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

A great wonder! The Creator of the invisible* visibly suffered because of His love for mankind,* and rose as the Immortal One.* Come, then, O kindreds of the nations, and worship Him,* for by His mercy we have been freed from deception* and have learned to glorify the one God in three Persons.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

We bring our evening worship to You, the unwaning Light,* who at the end of the ages, through the body, as in a mirror,* visibly shone upon the world and descended even to Hades.* There You dispelled the stagnant darkness,* and the radiance of the Resurrection shone upon the nations.* O Light-giving Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

We glorify Christ, the author of our salvation,* for when He rose from the dead* the world was freed from deception.* The choir of angels rejoices,* the deception of the demons flees,* fallen Adam is raised up,* and the devil has lost his power.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The impious instructed the guard:* Conceal the Resurrection of Christ!* Take silver and say:* While we slept, the dead man was stolen from the tomb.* Who has seen, who has heard of a dead man being stolen,* and one embalmed and naked at that,* who left his burial cloths in the tomb?* Do not deceive yourselves, O faithless ones,* but study the sayings of the prophets and understand* that He is truly the Savior of the world and the Almighty.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The prophets, inspired by Your Spirit, O Lord,* foretold You, the incomprehensible One,* who before the dawn shone forth without beginning* from the spiritual and incorporeal bosom of the Father,* that You would become a child incarnate of the Immaculate One* and would be numbered among men,* visible to those who dwell on earth.* For their sake, O Merciful One, grant Your illumination to us* who sing Your ineffable and holy Resurrection.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

The God-inspired prophets,* who foretold You in word,* honored You in deed and obtained life without end.* Refusing to serve the creature* more than You, O Master Creator,* they renounced all things according to the Gospel* and conformed themselves to Your sufferings which they had foretold.* By their prayers, grant us to pass through the time of abstinence blamelessly,* O only Most Merciful One.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Being beyond description* by Your divine nature, O Master,* in these last times You became incarnate* and deigned to become describable.* For in assuming flesh You took on all its properties.* Therefore, following the divine apostolic tradition, we depict Your likeness on icons and piously venerate it, that we may be raised to Your love* and from it receive the grace of healing.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

By Your divine nature beyond description,* incomprehensibly incarnate in these last times,* You deigned, O Master, to be describable.* For in assuming flesh You assumed all its properties.* Therefore, depicting the likeness of Your image on panels and disks,* we kiss it with love,* rising to Your love* and drawing from it the grace of healing,* following the apostolic divine tradition.

Glory (Tone 2): The grace of truth has shone forth,* prefigured of old by shadows,* and now plainly fulfilled.* For behold, the Church has clothed herself in the bodily image of Christ as in a beautiful garment,* recalling the foreshadowing of the tabernacle of witness* and holding fast the Orthodox faith,* that she may honor Him.* Therefore we keep the image, not straying from the right path.* Let those who do not believe thus be covered with shame.* For us it is glory to venerate piously the image of the Incarnate One,* not worshiping it as God.* Kissing it, O faithful, let us cry out:* O God, save Your people and bless Your inheritance.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 5): In the Red Sea of old,* an image of the unwedded Bride was depicted.* There Moses was the divider of the waters;* here Gabriel is the minister of the miracle.* Then Israel walked through the deep as on dry land;* now the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.* The sea, after the crossing of Israel, remained impassable;* the Immaculate One, after the birth of Emmanuel, remained incorrupt.* O God, existing before the ages and appearing as man — have mercy on us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): We magnify You, O Christ our Savior, who took on flesh and did not depart from heaven,* with voices and songs.* For You accepted the Cross and death for our race as the Lord who loves mankind,* and having cast down the gates of Hades, on the third day You rose,* saving our souls.
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

From Your pierced side, O Giver of Life,* You poured out streams of life and salvation.* Having accepted bodily death, You granted us immortality.* Having dwelt in the tomb, as God You freed us,* co-raising us with Yourself.* Therefore we cry out to You:* O Lord who loves mankind — glory to You!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Wondrous is Your crucifixion and descent into Hades, O Lover of mankind!* For You took it captive and co-raised with Yourself its former prisoners.* As God, opening Paradise, You made them worthy to attain it.* Therefore, to us also who glorify Your Resurrection on the third day,* grant cleansing from sins and deign to make us dwellers in Paradise,* as the only Compassionate One.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
O Lover of mankind, You accepted bodily passions for our sake* and rose from the dead on the third day.* Heal our bodily passions,* raise us from our grievous transgressions, and save us!

Glory (Tone 2): Having passed from impiety to piety* and been illumined by the light of understanding,* let us clap our hands as in the Psalms,* offering grateful praise to God.* Let us honorably venerate the sacred images of Christ,* the Most Pure One, and all the Saints,* depicted on walls, panels, and sacred vessels,* rejecting the impious faith of the ungodly,* asking through the prayers of Your Most Pure Mother* and all the Saints, O Christ our God,* to grant us great mercy.

Now and ever: O new wonder surpassing all ancient wonders!* For who has ever known a mother who gave birth without a man* and carried in her arms Him who holds all creation?* For what was born is the good pleasure of God,* whom you, O Most Pure One, carried in your arms as a child* and had toward Him a mother's boldness.* Therefore, cease not to pray to Him for those who honor you,* that He may have mercy and save our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 4): The joyful message of the resurrection was heard from the angel, and the ancestral condemnation was undone, when the women disciples cried out in exaltation: The Lord is risen, granting great mercy to the world.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Turn not Your face from Your servant, for I am in distress; make haste to hear me. Look upon my soul and deliver it.
Verse: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to the grave.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 4): O Lord, I cease not to sin,* yet am deemed worthy of mercy, and this I cannot comprehend.* Overcome my senselessness, O only Good One, and have mercy on me.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

O Lord, though I tremble with fear before You,* I do not stop doing evil.* Who at the trial does not fear the judge?* Or who, wishing to be healed, angers the physician as I do?* O long-suffering Lord, have mercy on my weakness and have mercy on me.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Pay no heed to the multitude of my sins, O Lord, born of the Virgin,* but cleanse all my sins, giving me the desire for conversion.* To You, as the only Lover of mankind, I pray: Have mercy on me.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Woe is me! To whom have I become like?* To the barren fig tree.* Therefore I fear lest I be cursed and cut down.* But You, O heavenly Husbandman, Christ God,* make fruitful my barren soul,* and as the Prodigal Son, receive me and have mercy on me.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

O God, King of all!* Grant us contrition of heart,* turning from evil,* and perfect amendment,* for we have sunk into fleshly passions* and have distanced ourselves from You* and lost all hope.* Therefore, O Jesus Almighty, Savior of our souls,* save us who have gone astray, in Your great goodness.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The wondrous Moses,* having been purified by fasting,* beheld the Desired One.* Following his example, O my soul,* in this time of abstinence hasten to be cleansed from evil,* that you may behold the Lord* who gives you forgiveness,* purification, and deliverance,* for He is good and loves mankind.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Today let us all joyfully begin the second week of the fast,* valiantly passing through it day by day.* Having fashioned for ourselves a fiery chariot, like Elijah the Tishbite,* from the four great virtues,* let us raise the mind through dispassion,* guard the body with the armor of chastity,* hastening to vanquish the enemy.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O God-wise and blessed Theodotus!* With your crimson blood* you adorned your priestly* and divine vestment,* passing piously from strength to strength* and from glory to glory.* Pray now that our souls be granted peace* and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O God-wise and blessed Theodotus!* With your crimson blood* you adorned your priestly* and divine vestment,* passing piously from strength to strength* and from glory to glory.* Pray now that our souls be granted peace* and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

As a true priest* you first offered to God* the bloodless sacrifice,* and at the end, as a true martyr,* O all-honorable herald of God,* you offered yourself to Christ* as an acceptable whole-burnt offering.* Pray to Him for those* who glorify you.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): You are the joy and gladness of my soul,* the healing of my passions, O Virgin consecrated to God,* and the speedy cure of my soul's infirmities, O Most Pure One.* You are my salvation in afflicting temptations and my protection in misfortune;* therefore I magnify you.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): Come, let us purify ourselves with almsgiving and generosity to the poor,* not trumpeting or publishing our charity,* that the left hand may not know what the right hand does.* Let not vanity destroy the fruit of almsgiving,* but let us secretly call upon Him who sees in secret:* Father, forgive us our trespasses, as the Lover of mankind.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Wondrous is this weapon — prayer and fasting.* It made Moses a lawgiver* and Elijah a zealous minister of sacrifice.* In it let us also abide, O faithful, and cry to the Savior:* Against You alone have we sinned — have mercy on us!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord!* You sanctify every place* and heal every infirmity.* We beseech you: Pray now* that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy.
Glory, now and ever: The heavenly hosts sing your praises, O full of grace, Virgin Mother,* and we glorify your incomprehensible birthgiving, O Theotokos.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)

Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.

Salvation belongs to the Lord; upon Your people be Your blessing.
Verse: O Lord, how many are my foes! Many are rising up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): To whom have you become like, O lazy and wretched soul,* doing no good* but choosing to remain constantly in evil?* The judgment is at the door — repent!* Purify yourself by fasting and prayer* and cry out to your Master:* I have sinned against You!* Forgive me, as the merciful One,* and grant me amendment of life.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Being boundlessly gracious, O Christ,* dry up, as the sinless God,* the abyss of my wicked sins.* Give my heart repentance,* which calms the torrents of lawlessness,* that I may sing and glorify Your ineffable mercy,* O long-suffering Benefactor of our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O God, You have brought us again today to the holy fast* and granted us the grace to enter its second week.* O Lord, grant us also in the future to pass through it well.* Nourish and strengthen both body and soul generously,* that we all may worthily complete this course,* and, crowned, joyfully reach the Lord's Day of Your Resurrection,* ceaselessly glorifying You.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O three-numbered martyrs,* firmly resisting* the severe judges* and enduring with faith* every kind of cruelest torment,* you received the Kingdom on high.* Therefore pray* that our souls be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Let us praise with spiritual songs Eutropius,* unwavering Cleonicus,* and Basiliscus:* for by fire they burned* the thickets of ungodliness* — by the grace of piety.* And now they enlighten the ends of the world* with divine flashes,* as most brilliant luminaries,* as those who have dimmed all deception.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Your heads were cut off,* yet you crushed the head of the enemy* with your valiant feet,* O courageous sufferers,* unerring stars, living sacrifices,* precious treasures of the Heavenly Temple:* O glorious Eutropius,* Basiliscus, and Cleonicus,* entreat peace for all.

Glory: Grievously ailing* with wicked passions is my soul.* Heal it, O Most Holy One,* who gave birth to the Healer and Savior of all, Christ,* who healed every wound of mankind* wounded by the evil design of the devil* and led us out of death.

Now and ever (Theotokion): Accept, O Theotokos, the prayer of your servants* which we bring to you.* O Lady, deliver and raise up us sinners and endangered ones — this your flock* which runs to you with faith in your holy temple.* For you who gave birth to the Savior have, as Mother, boldness before Him.* Therefore fervently pray that we may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): Why do you languish, O my soul, in the captivity of sin?* Why, growing weak, do you not hasten to the Healer?* Behold, the favorable time is here; now is truly the day of salvation!* Arise and wash yourself with tears of repentance,* and with the oil of charity light your lamp,* that you may receive from Christ God purification and great mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Let us keep a spiritual fast:* Let us break every bond of iniquity,* let us abandon what leads us to sin,* let us forgive our brethren their faults,* that our own trespasses may be forgiven.* Only then can we sing:* Let my prayer rise before You, O Lord,* like incense!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Prophets, apostles of Christ, and martyrs* taught us to hymn the consubstantial Trinity* and enlightened the nations that had gone astray,* making the sons of men fellow citizens of the angels.

Glory, now and ever: O Lady, never forget your poor servants,* but by your prayers deliver us from eternal punishment* and from every threatening affliction,* and turn away from us, O Theotokos, the wrath of the Lord.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): You placed Your most pure hands upon the Cross, O Christ,* and embraced all the ends of the earth.* Therefore I cry to You:* Gather my scattered mind,* captive and led by the passions.* Purify me wholly by the fast* and make me a partaker of Your Passion.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

In ancient times the fast tempered the youths* and, as it is written, made them mightier than the blazing fire.* Take up the fast, O my wretched soul,* kindling within yourself the love of the Lord,* by which you will consume the destructive passions* and escape the fire that is to come.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The time of fasting is a time of joy.* Therefore, amply nourished* by the radiance of purity,* sincere love, the brightness of prayer,* and other virtues, let us shine forth and cry out:* O most holy Cross of Christ,* which brings us life and joy,* grant us all to venerate you with a pure heart* and bestow upon all cleansing and great mercy.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O God-wise Father Gerasimus,* on the wings of the mind* you soared to God through faith,* disdaining the instability of worldly tumult.* Taking up your cross* you followed the All-Seeing One,* subduing the willful impulses of the flesh by temperance* and your thoughts by the might* of the Divine Spirit.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable Father Gerasimus,* dwelling constantly in deserts, caves, and mountains* with faith,* you sought God* and found the Desired One.* Having instead of sinews the strings of an intrepid soul,* by good ascents you ever* led ranks of monks* to be saved in Christ.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having spent your entire life* in great silence,* in sorrow and in tears,* you became a guide of monks through faith,* adorned with poverty* and embellished with temperance,* showing yourself a stranger and pilgrim on earth.* Therefore you honorably obtained your reward,* O blessed of God.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): Beholding You, O Jesus, nailed to the Cross* and willingly accepting suffering, O Master,* the Virgin and Mother cried out:* Woe is me, beloved Child!* What unjust wounds You endure, O Physician of human weakness,* who by Your compassion delivered all from corruption!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): O Lord, crucified in the flesh,* You crucified with Yourself our old self.* Pierced in the side by a lance,* You pierced the soul-destroying serpent.* Nail my flesh with fear of You* and wound my soul with Your love,* that, gazing upon Your Passion,* I may spend the appointed time of fasting in abstinence,* restraining not only the stomach* but also other sinful desires.* And I will offer You as a sacrifice the contrition of my heart* and the humility of my spirit for all my sins,* from which deliver me, O Lover of mankind.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

By Your Cross, O Lord, You conquered death* that entered the world through eating the fruit* of the forbidden tree in Paradise.* Therefore we implore You:* O Lord, in this fast turn us from every harmful pleasure* and grant us to acquire spiritual nourishment* that remains unto eternal life,* that we may receive the imperishable crowns of joy* prepared for those who truly fast.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O all-praised passion-bearers,* you appeared as luminaries to the world,* crying out to Christ: O Lord — glory to You!
Glory, now and ever: When the All-Immaculate saw You* willingly nailed to the Cross,* weeping she cried: O my Child,* where has Your radiant beauty gone,* with which You adorned human nature?

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): Great and fearful shall be Your coming, O Lord,* when You sit to render righteous judgment.* Do not condemn me who deserve punishment,* but as God, spare me for the sake of the favorable prayers of Your Apostles.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

By the prayers of Your divine Apostles, O Lord,* grant us to complete the time of fasting with a repentant heart as is fitting,* that all of us who are being saved may glorify You,* O good and merciful One.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Apostles of Christ,* you are luminaries to those on earth,* most rich treasures of the wise knowledge of our God.* By your holy prayers deliver from temptations those who sing your praises* and give us strength to zealously complete* the time of fasting and to live in peace,* that we may piously behold Christ's Passion,* boldly offering hymns to our God.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O unerring and divine star,* radiant light of God's commandments* and wondrous passion-bearer, ever-memorable among the martyrs!* You drove away the mist of darkness* like a light-bearing morning star, O blessed one.* O goodly offering* and spotless sacrifice!* Therefore pray ceaselessly to Christ for the salvation of our souls.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O preacher of the truth!* Appearing as a sword-bearing warrior* against impiety,* by your suffering you cut off the heads of the enemies, O most wise,* and clearly proclaimed the divine commandment,* exhorting the untaught people to what is better.* O Conon, fellow citizen of the martyrs!* Pray to the Deliverer that He deliver His servants,* your singers, from the passions.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O good change* wrought by the right hand of the Most High* upon you, O all-glorious passion-bearer!* From an unbelieving root you appeared as a blossoming fruit,* O all-blessed and all-praised Conon.* Having Christ as your supreme commander,* you vanquished the legions of demons* with sovereign power.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Rejoice, O Chariot of the divine Light!* Rejoice, O Temple and Tabernacle of sanctification of the Lord!* Light has dawned upon us* from your most pure womb,* illuminating the ends of the earth* and sanctifying us by grace.* Rejoice, O Foundation of salvation, O Most Pure One!* Rejoice, O awesome Proclamation and Praise* of those who hope in You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): Having entered the spiritual fast, O brethren,* let us not speak deceitful words* nor give our brother occasion for stumbling.* Rather, kindling the lamp of the soul with repentance,* let us cry with tears to Christ:* O Lover of mankind, forgive us our trespasses.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The labor of fasting is for us a time of repentance* and a step toward acquiring eternal life,* especially if we stretch out a charitable hand.* Nothing so saves the soul as helping the poor.* Almsgiving joined with fasting delivers one from death.* Let us love it, for nothing is its equal;* it suffices to save our souls.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

Duly putting on the breastplate of faith* and arming yourselves with the Cross,* you became valiant warriors,* bravely opposing the tormentors,* overthrowing the deception of the devil,* and becoming victors you were deemed worthy of crowns.* Therefore ever pray for us* that our souls may be saved.
Glory, now and ever: O Virgin, holy Theotokos!* Accept me under your protection,* for I know that in you is my salvation* and you alone can help me, O Most Pure One.

troparia.x: Troparia
The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)
Now and ever: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of sins. (Prostration)
Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 4)
I bless the Lord who has given me counsel.
Verse: Preserve me, O God, for in You I take refuge.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): Pierced between the ribs* and lifted upon the tree, You poured forth life for me,* who was slain by the malice of the serpent and the eating from the tree.* Therefore I glorify You, O Christ,* and implore Your mercy:* Allow me to pass the arena of fasting in repentance* and to venerate Your Passion and Resurrection.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

You banished from Paradise the first-created human* who in the garden plucked the fruit of sorrow.* But, nailed to the Cross on earth, O Savior, You led her back again.* Therefore I cry to You:* Free me today from my afflictions* and cleanse me through fasting and tears of repentance,* O Jesus most good, Savior of our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, we hymn* Your life-giving Cross,* for by putting death to death* it made life spring forth in the world.* With it we come to prayer:* Strengthen us, the fasters, in this holy time* and give us power against the passions and victory over all earthly things,* in Your great mercy, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Shining forth in these latter times,* O victorious martyrs,* like unquenchable stars on the sacred firmament of the Church,* by the radiance of your sufferings* you enlightened all under the sun* and dispelled the darkness of deception, O all-praised.* Now you have passed to the eternal Light.* Therefore with faith we commemorate your luminous and sacred struggle,* enriched by your good intercession.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Forcibly led away as captives,* you were imprisoned, O martyrs of Christ,* confined in a dungeon for a long time,* truly divine keepers of the faith.* From there the furious beast of evil name,* having led you out, slew you with the sword,* for you refused to obey his wicked commands.* And now you have joyfully inherited the heavenly mansions of the Kingdom,* forty-two in number,* O all-glorious!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

With Constantine, Bassoë, and Callistus,* let us praise Theophilus and Theodore* and the rest of the divine company of passion-bearers with loud voices.* For they chose to die for the Life of all,* going gladly to the slaughter.* Now they rest in the City of the Living God* and pray that we may find* remission of sins on the Day of Judgment* and final redemption.

Let us now worthily honor Tarasius,* the true minister and hierarch of Christ,* who received the grace of the Spirit from Him,* the wise archpastor;* for he has boldness before God to pray* that we may be saved.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): Seeing then the Ewe-Lamb her own Lamb* hastening to death,* she followed quickly after Him and said:* Where do You go, my sweetest Child, O Christ?* Why do You walk this road without delay, O Long-Suffering One?* O dearest Jesus, sinless and most merciful Lord!* Speak a word to me, Your handmaid, beloved Son,* and do not dismiss me with silence, O Merciful One,* who wondrously gave You birth, O God and Giver of life,* who grants the world great mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): Not having fasted as the Creator commanded,* our first parents reaped death from the tree through disobedience* and lost the tree of Life and the delight of Paradise.* Therefore let us abstain, O faithful, from corruptible foods* and avoid destructive passions,* that from the divine Cross we may receive life* and with the wise thief return to our original homeland,* receiving from Christ God great mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

This present fast puts the passions to death* and promises healing to those wounded by sin.* Let us honor it as a helper given by God.* Let us accept it as Moses accepted the God-inscribed tablets of the Law.* Let us not give precedence to intemperance which shattered them.* Let us not share the fate of those whose bodies perished in the wilderness.* Let us not grieve like the Jews but rejoice as the Church.* Let us not be hypocrites like the Pharisees but conduct ourselves according to the Gospel,* boasting in the Cross of Christ, the Savior of our souls.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

How shall we call you, O Saints?* Cherubim? For Christ rested upon you.* Seraphim? For you ceaselessly glorified Him.* Or perhaps angels? For you turned from the body.* Or powers? For you work wonders.* Many are your names and still more your gifts!* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever: How strange and wonderful this is!* cried the Virgin in a motherly way to the Lord.* The pangs I did not endure at Your birth, O Son,* now sharply pierce my heart.* I cannot bear to see You on the Cross, O Light of my eyes!* But hasten to arise, and I will glorify with all the world Your wondrous Providence.
