prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 5)
May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.
Verse: May He send you help from the sanctuary and give you support from Zion.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
(Tone 5): Today is the favorable time,* today is the day of salvation!* Out of Your great mercy, visit my soul* and lighten the burden of my lawlessness,* O only Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

You became fellow citizens of the angels, O holy martyrs,* fearlessly confessing Christ before the tribunal.* You forgot the transient beauty of the world, keeping faith as a sure anchor.* Therefore, having driven off deception, you bestow gifts of healing on the faithful,* praying unceasingly that our souls may be saved.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

How can one not marvel at your struggles, O holy martyrs?* Clothed in mortal flesh, you vanquished the bodiless foes.* The threats of the tormentors did not frighten you,* the intensified tortures did not terrify you.* Truly you were worthily glorified by Christ.* Therefore entreat great mercy for our souls.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Invincible martyrs,* all-praised hierarchs, luminaries of the world,* immovable pillars of the divine Church,* the foundation of doctrines,* the leaders of the pious,* the persecutors of deception — you became, O heaven-minded fathers,* light-bearers of our souls,* companions of angels,* champions of the Trinity.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Ephrem the praise-worthy,* Basil the God-wise,* Capiton the great,* divine Agathodoruswith Elpidius, Etherius, and renowned Eugenius,* let them be magnified as blessed by God-inspired words;* for living venerably* and suffering in holiness,* they appeared as citizens of the Heavenly Kingdom.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having put to death the wisdom of the flesh by his labors,* praise-worthy Basil raised a dead man to life* by divine invocation.* And again Capiton, the holy shepherd,* stood in the midst of the flames rejoicing,* and they beheld him unburned.* By their prayers, O Lover of mankind,* grant us Your favor* and Your great mercy.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 5): In the Red Sea of old,* an image of the unwedded Bride was depicted.* There Moses was the divider of the waters;* here Gabriel is the minister of the miracle.* Then Israel walked through the deep as on dry land;* now the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.* The sea, after the crossing of Israel, remained impassable;* the Immaculate One, after the birth of Emmanuel, remained incorrupt.* O God, existing before the ages and appearing as man — have mercy on us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): O all-glorious martyrs!* The earth did not hide you, but heaven received you.* The gates of paradise opened for you,* and entering therein you delight in the tree of life.* Pray to Christ that He grant our souls peace* and great mercy.
Verse: Blessed are those whom You have chosen and received, O Lord.

What pleasure of this life is free from sorrow?* What glory stands unchanged on earth?* All things are more fleeting than a shadow, all more deceptive than a dream.* A single moment — and death replaces them all.* But in the light of Your countenance and the delight of Your beauty,* grant rest, O Christ, Lover of mankind, to those You have chosen.
Verse: Their souls shall dwell among good things.

None among mortals is without sin,* only You, the only Immortal.* Therefore, as a merciful God, receive Your servants into the light,* with Your angelic choirs,* not remembering their trespasses, and for Your compassion's sake* grant them forgiveness.

Glory, now and ever: Immeasurably beyond understanding is the wondrous greatness of your birthgiving, O Bride of God,* which all the prophets proclaimed.* Most glorious were both the conception and the Nativity, O All-Praised One,* — incomprehensible and ineffable,* for by it your Son saved the world, as the Merciful One.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 2): O Apostles, Prophets, Martyrs, Bishops, Venerable and Righteous, who have worthily fulfilled your course and kept the faith: having boldness before the Savior, intercede for us, we pray, that He may save our souls.

Glory: Commemorate, O Lord, as a good God, Your servants, and forgive them every sin, voluntary and involuntary; for You are the Resurrection, the Life, and the Repose of Your servants who have fallen asleep, O Christ our God, and to You do we send up glory, with Your eternal Father, and Your all-holy, good, and life-creating Spirit, now and ever, and forever. Amen.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother, and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save our souls, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 5): By Your precious Cross, O Christ,* You put the devil to shame;* by Your Resurrection You blunted the sting of sin* and saved us from the gates of death.* We glorify You, O Only-Begotten One.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

He who grants resurrection to the human race* was led like a lamb to the slaughter.* The princes of Hades trembled before Him* and the gates of sorrow were lifted,* for the King of Glory, Christ, entered,* saying to the prisoners: Come forth!* and to those in darkness: Be revealed!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

A great wonder!* The Creator of invisible things* suffered visibly in His love for mankind* and rose as the Immortal One.* Come then, O kindreds of the nations, and worship Him,* for by His mercy we have been freed from deception* and have learned to glorify the one God in three Persons.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

We bring our evening worship to You, the never-waning Light,* who at the end of the ages, through the body, as in a mirror,* visibly shone upon the world and descended even to Hades,* dispelling its stagnant darkness.* The radiance of the Resurrection shone upon the nations.* O Light-giving Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

Having completed the second week of fasting,* let us glorify the Lord,* who by His suffering overcame the enemy.* Let us pass the remaining time in abstinence and prayer,* that we may be worthy to venerate the honorable Cross* and to greet the Resurrection of Christ.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Illumined by abstinence,* let us haste to venerate the life-giving Cross of Christ,* crying out to it with faith:* Rejoice, O Cross! Rejoice, guide of the blind!* Rejoice, resurrection of the dead!* Rejoice, O tree of incorruption,* by which we have been delivered from the corruption of death!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O God-bearing Eudocia,* forsaking the darkness of sin* and turning to the light of godliness,* you washed yourself clean in the waters of repentance* and shone with the radiance of virtues.* Therefore now you stand before Christ,* praying for those who honor your memory.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O glorious Eudocia,* having cast off the garment of sin,* you clothed yourself in the bright robe of repentance,* and became a dwelling of the Holy Spirit.* Therefore pray to Christ* that He grant us peace and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O blessed Eudocia,* by your ascetic struggles you mortified the desires of the flesh* and illumined your soul with the light of fasting.* Now, dwelling in the heavenly mansions,* entreat Christ God* to grant our souls great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O blessed Eudocia,* by your ascetic struggles you mortified the desires of the flesh* and illumined your soul with the light of fasting.* Now, dwelling in the heavenly mansions,* entreat Christ God* to grant our souls great mercy.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 5): In the Red Sea of old,* an image of the unwedded Bride was depicted.* There Moses was the divider of the waters;* here Gabriel is the minister of the miracle.* Then Israel walked through the deep as on dry land;* now the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.* The sea, after the crossing of Israel, remained impassable;* the Immaculate One, after the birth of Emmanuel, remained incorrupt.* O God, existing before the ages and appearing as man — have mercy on us!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5): We magnify You, O Christ the Savior, who took on flesh yet did not depart from heaven.* With voices and songs we glorify You.* For You accepted the Cross and death for our race, as Lord who loves mankind.* Having cast down the gates of Hades, on the third day You rose, saving our souls.
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

From Your pierced side, O Giver of Life,* You poured out streams of life and salvation.* Having accepted bodily death, You granted us immortality.* Having dwelt in the tomb, as God You freed us,* co-raising us with Yourself.* Therefore we cry out to You:* O Lord who loves mankind — glory to You!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Wondrous is Your crucifixion and Your descent into Hades, O Lover of mankind!* For You took it captive and co-raised with You its former prisoners.* As God, opening Paradise, You made them worthy to attain it.* Therefore grant us also who glorify Your Resurrection on the third day* cleansing from sins and deem us worthy to dwell in Paradise,* as the only Compassionate One.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
O Lover of mankind, You accepted bodily passions for our sake* and rose from the dead on the third day.* Heal our bodily passions,* raise us from our grievous transgressions, and save us!

Glory, now and ever: O new wonder surpassing all ancient wonders!* For who has known a mother who gave birth without a man* and carried in her arms Him who holds all creation?* What was born is the good pleasure of God,* whom you, O Most Pure One, carried as a child in your arms* and had toward Him a mother's boldness.* Therefore cease not to pray to Him for those who honor you,* that He may have mercy and save our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 5): Co-eternal Word of the Father and the Spirit, born of a virgin for our salvation: let us the faithful praise and worship You, for You willed to go to the Cross in the flesh and to endure death, and to raise the dead by Your glorious resurrection.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
You have given those who fear Your name an inheritance, O Lord.
Verse: Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.

The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 5): As the Prodigal Son, I come to You, O Merciful One:* receive me, falling at Your feet, among Your hired servants, O God,* and have mercy on me, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Like those who fell among thieves and were wounded,* so also have we fallen into a multitude of sins, and our soul is wounded.* To whom then shall we run, being guilty?* Only to You, O merciful Physician of souls:* Pour out upon us, O God, Your great mercy!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Do not cut us sinners down like the barren fig tree,* but leave us for more years,* watering our souls with tears of repentance,* that we may bring forth the fruits of charity.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
Being the Sun of Righteousness,* illumine the hearts of those who sing to You:* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

The multitude of our sins, cleanse, O Lord,* by the abundance of Your mercy, O All-Merciful,* and deign us, as Lover of mankind,* with a pure soul to behold Your Cross* and to venerate it in the coming week of fasting.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The multitude of our sins, cleanse, O Lord,* by the abundance of Your mercy, O All-Merciful,* and deign us, as Lover of mankind,* with a pure soul to behold Your Cross* and to venerate it in the coming week of fasting.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O wondrous sight!* We behold the tree on which Christ was willingly crucified in the flesh.* Therefore the world bows down in worship, and being enlightened, cries:* O power of the Cross! Your very appearance consumes the demons* and the sign of your cross burns them like fire.* We magnify you, O most glorious Tree,* we honor you and with awe we venerate you,* glorifying God who through you gives us life everlasting.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

How shall we call you, O glorious one?* — a mighty warrior of the King and God,* who manfully overthrew the ungodliness of the bodiless ones,* a luminary shining with noetic light,* a most powerful destroyer of idols,* a most fervent athlete,* and a lawful sufferer.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

How shall we now call you, O glorious one?* — a river of life-bearing water* that pours upon us spiritual abundance,* an inexhaustible flood of healings for those in affliction,* a cup overflowing with sacred drink,* a most true wonder-worker,* a peer of the bodiless ones.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

How shall we call you, O holy one?* — a guide of the lost, an intercessor for sinners,* a true physician of those amid storms of adversity,* a fragrant sprout of Paradise most precious,* a first-fruit and foundation of the martyrs,* an ever-flowing fountain of wonders,* a valiant champion.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): To whom have you become like, O my miserable soul,* in no way arising to repentance* and having no dread of the fire awaiting the wicked?* Arise, and call upon the one who is swift to help,* and cry out: O Virgin Mother,* pray to Your Son and our God* to deliver me from the snare of the deceiver!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): We dare not, in our wretchedness, because of our wicked deeds,* lift our eyes to heaven.* But like the Publican, groaning, we cry out to You:* O God, cleanse us sinners* and as the only good One, deliver us from the hypocrisy of the Pharisee.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

We dare not, in our wretchedness, because of our wicked deeds,* lift our eyes to heaven.* But like the Publican, groaning, we cry out to You:* O God, cleanse us sinners* and as the only good One, deliver us from the hypocrisy of the Pharisee.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord!* You sanctify every place* and heal every infirmity.* We beseech you: pray now* that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy.
Glory, now and ever: The heavenly choirs sing your praises, O full of grace, Virgin Mother,* and we glorify your incomprehensible birthgiving, O Theotokos.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

The Lord, mighty and strong; the Lord, mighty in battle.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8, Podoben: "How shall we call you"): Having reached midway through the fast,* strengthened by the power of the Cross,* let us glorify the Savior Lord who ascended upon it in the midst of the earth and cry:* Grant us, O Master,* to behold Your Passion and holy Resurrection,* bestowing upon us purification and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Let us tame our bodily passions by abstinence from food and renunciation of pleasures.* Let us kiss with faith the wood of the Cross,* for it is set forth for veneration* and sanctifies all by its divine grace.* Let us cry out to the Lord:* We give thanks to You, O Merciful One,* for by it You save our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 8): Venerating the Cross today, let us all cry out:* Rejoice, O Tree of Life!* Rejoice, O holy scepter of Christ!* Rejoice, heavenly glory of mankind!* Rejoice, praise of rulers!* Rejoice, strength of faith! Rejoice, invincible weapon!* Rejoice, O banisher of enemies!* Rejoice, radiant light that saves the world!* Rejoice, great glory of the martyrs!* Rejoice, power of the righteous!* Rejoice, O light of angels!* Rejoice, O all-venerable Cross!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

We know you as a Man of God* by calling and in truth,* for you shone with virtues:* having experienced boundless poverty and hardship on earth,* you confirmed the faithful in their faith by miracles.* Therefore pray that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

With the dew of divine love* you extinguished the desires of the flesh, O Alexius.* You wisely chose the Wedding Chamber above all chambers* and preferred divine likeness to the angels over bodily sweetness.* With them pray that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You lived unrecognized, O wise one,* at the gate of your noble parents,* enduring the abuse of your own servants for a long time.* And in dying, by miracles* you revealed your struggle,* healing infirmities* and driving away unclean spirits.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Grievously ailing* with wicked passions is my soul.* Heal it, O Most Holy One,* who gave birth to the Healer and Savior of all, Christ,* who healed every wound of mankind* wounded by the evil design of the devil* and led us out of death.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): The many-worded Pharisee was rejected for his boasting,* while the humble Publican who prayed in silence was justified.* Therefore, O my soul, seeing the difference between them,* choose humility and take it to heart,* for Christ, as Lover of mankind,* promised to give His grace to the humble.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The many-worded Pharisee was rejected for his boasting,* while the humble Publican who prayed in silence was justified.* Therefore, O my soul, seeing the difference between them,* choose humility and take it to heart,* for Christ, as Lover of mankind,* promised to give His grace to the humble.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord, pray to our God* and entreat upon our souls an abundance of mercies* and cleansing from the multitude of sins, we beseech you.
Glory, now and ever: O Theotokos Virgin, Mother of God,* I take refuge under your holy protection,* for I know well that you heal the wounds of my soul.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: "In the midst of the earth"): In the midst of the earth You endured, O Merciful One,* suffering and the Cross,* granting all redemption and dispassion.* Therefore today, at the midpoint of the fast,* we set forth the holy Cross* and we all venerate it,* kissing it with joy, we beseech:* Grant us, O Word of God,* in the light of divine virtues to behold Your Passion* and life-giving Resurrection, O only Most Merciful One.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Long-Suffering One, You hung dead upon the Cross* and were pierced by the lance* and were given gall to drink, O Master,* who by the hand of Moses made sweet the bitter waters of Meribah.* Therefore I beseech and ask You:* Free my heart from the bitterness of the passions* and sweeten my soul with the honey of repentance,* revealing me as a worshiper* of Your holy sufferings.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Mighty is the Cross which Moses prefigured of old* with outstretched hands when he conquered Amalek.* Today it is set forth and magnificently presented to our eyes.* Let us touch it with a pure mind and lips,* for upon it Christ was lifted up and put death to death.* Having been granted such great grace,* let us glorify the Savior with God-inspired songs* and pray to attain* the saving Resurrection.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Come, let us venerate today the tree* that has brought us life —* the Cross of Christ our God,* for by it death was wounded* and the Resurrection of us who had fallen was again revealed.* Let us cry out to the Deliverer:* You who willingly suffered for us* to save the world — O our God, glory to You!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

As an invincible power,* Your Cross, O our Savior, was given to us Christians,* by which the multitude of pagans is overthrown.* And Your Church, O Christ, that truly glorifies You,* it overshadows with peace.* Kissing it now,* we fervently cry out to You:* Grant that we also become partakers* in the lot of Your Saints.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having purified the senses of our souls with tears* and washed ourselves with fasting,* come, let us venerate the wood of the Cross,* for by it the soul-destroying impulses* are vanquished through abstinence.* Let us cry to the Crucified One:* Grant, O Savior, to venerate* the splendor of Your Resurrection on the third day.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 8): Now the Master of creation and Lord of glory* is nailed to the Cross and pierced in the side.* The Sweetness of the Church tastes gall and vinegar.* He who covers the heavens with clouds is crowned with thorns* and clothed in a garment of mockery.* He who fashioned man with His hand is struck by a mortal hand.* He who clothes the heavens with clouds is smitten on the back.* He receives spitting and wounds, reproach and blows to the face.* And all this He endures for my sake, the condemned one,* that He may save the world from deception,* for He is the Deliverer and merciful God.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): I have surpassed the Publican in sins* but have not imitated his repentance,* nor have I fulfilled the Pharisee's good deeds* but only mimicked his boasting.* But You, O Christ God, who by Your extraordinary humility* brought low the arrogance of the devil on the Cross,* keep us far from the former malice of the Publican and the Pharisee's madness,* establishing in our souls the good* that was in each of them, and save us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

I have surpassed the Publican in sins* but have not imitated his repentance,* nor have I fulfilled the Pharisee's good deeds* but only mimicked his boasting.* But You, O Christ God, who by Your extraordinary humility* brought low the arrogance of the devil on the Cross,* keep us far from the former malice of the Publican and the Pharisee's madness,* establishing in our souls the good* that was in each of them, and save us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Prophets, apostles of Christ, and martyrs* taught us to hymn the consubstantial Trinity* and enlightened the nations led astray,* making the sons of men fellow citizens of the angels.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 5): All creation, beholding You* hanging naked upon the Cross,* O Architect and Creator of all,* was transformed with fear and lamented:* the sun hid its light and the earth quaked,* the rocks split and the radiant veil of the Temple was torn,* the dead rose from the graves* and the angelic powers were aghast, saying:* O wonder! The Judge is judged* and suffers willingly* for the salvation and renewal of the world!

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 3): Let us now wash our souls with the water of fasting* and approach the life-giving Cross of the Lord.* Let us venerate it with faith* and draw divine illumination from it,* finding today* eternal salvation, peace, and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O all-holy Cross — praise of the Apostles,* the dominions, powers, and angels surround you.* Save those who venerate you* from every infirmity,* and grant us to complete the course of the divine fast in holiness,* and to reach the bright Resurrection* by which we are saved.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 7): Today we venerate the Cross of the Lord and cry out:* Rejoice, O Tree of Life* that has destroyed the kingdom of Hades!* Rejoice, O joy of the world, for you have annihilated corruption!* Rejoice, for by your power the demons are put to flight!* Rejoice, O support and invincible weapon of the faithful!* We pray: protect and sanctify those who kiss you.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having received the illumination* of divine knowledge,* you enlightened, O wise one, the eyes of your heart,* and wisely drove away the darkness of deception,* and confessed Christ,* the Lord of all who assumed flesh.* Strengthened by Him* by the power of the Spirit, O Chrysanthus,* you showed yourself superior to all torments, O all-praised one.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The snares of the enemy* and the frenzy of pleasure* you considered as a spider's web.* And placed in a gloomy prison,* you were illumined by divine radiance* and filled with spiritual fragrance amid the stench.* As the most excellent wedding steward,* you brought to Christ an undefiled bride —* the one who had sought to defile you.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Wounded by longing* for the sweetest Creator,* you finally turned from the deception of godlessness,* and within the divine bridal chamber you found your place,* becoming a bride of Christ through the many sufferings of the body,* O great-named Daria,* divine vessel of the Spirit,* adornment of women combatants,* glory of virgins.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 8): Today the Unapproachable in essence* becomes approachable for me* and suffers the Passion,* He who delivers me from the passions.* He who gives light to the blind* is spat upon by lawless lips* and gives His back to be scourged for the captives' sake.* When the Pure Virgin and Mother beheld Him on the Cross,* she cried out in anguish:* Woe is me, my Child!* What have You done?* Beautiful in beauty beyond all mankind,* You appear breathless and without comeliness,* having neither form nor beauty.* Woe is me, my Light!* I cannot bear to see You sleeping;* I am wounded in my depths* and a cruel sword pierces my heart.* I praise Your Passion* and worship Your compassion.* O Long-Suffering Lord — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

(Tone 4): The fast — a fountain of good works — has now reached its midpoint.* In the days past it brought us contentment,* and for the future it has shown us profit,* for effort in what is good strengthens piety.* Therefore, let us joyfully cry out to Christ, the Giver of all good things:* O You who fasted and endured crucifixion for us,* grant us to become worthy partakers of Your divine Pascha,* that, living in peace, we may glorify You with the Father and the Spirit.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

(Tone 5): Doers of good works, awaiting their reward,* do not boast of it in the marketplaces and crossroads* but keep it hidden in the heart.* And He who sees in secret rewards us for our abstinence.* Therefore let us pass through this fast not with gloomy faces* but praying joyfully in the chambers of our souls, ceaselessly crying:* Our Father, who art in heaven,* lead us not into temptation, we pray,* but deliver us from the Evil One.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

With insatiable love of soul,* you did not renounce Christ, O holy martyrs.* You endured fierce and varied torments of passion,* cast down the pride of the tormentors,* and preserved your faith unshaken and undamaged.* You have passed to the heavens.* Therefore, having boldness before Him,* entreat that He grant us great mercy.

Glory, now and ever: Blessed do we call You, O Theotokos Virgin,* and we glorify You as is fitting, O faithful,* — the unshakable city, the impregnable wall,* the steadfast Protectress,* and the refuge of our souls.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4): O Lord, we faithful,* having been deemed worthy to kiss Your all-blessed Cross,* by which You saved us,* hymn Your compassion* and earnestly pray:* Give us all, O Savior, the joy of Your salvation,* granting us the grace to behold in repentance* Your holy sufferings* and Your Resurrection.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Stretching out on the Cross,* You endured death,* putting death to death and raising the dead* by Your life-giving word.* Therefore I pray to You: O Lord,* revive my soul, slain by sin!* Grant me, O Lover of mankind,* in these holy days of fasting,* repentance and deliverance from evil deeds.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Deemed worthy with joy to behold* and to kiss Your holy Cross,* we pray to You, O God our Savior,* that, strengthened by fasting,* we may reach Your most pure Passion* and, venerating it, hymn the Crucifixion,* the lance, the sponge, and the reed, by which You, O Lover of mankind, made us immortal* and led us back to the former life of delight.* Therefore, with thanksgiving, we glorify You this day.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Of all desires most fervently,* loving the One unto the end, O venerable ones,* you counted the beauties of life as refuse* and by vigils and prayers,* in cold and in heat,* you acquired the one thing needful that remains forever.* As fellow dwellers,* you appeared as fellow citizens of the angels* by grace.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Under the blows of rods,* and under a hail of stones,* and under the cutting of weapons,* you did not break your unanimity, O martyrs,* bound by brotherly love and desire.* But being slain together* and hewn limb from limb, O passion-bearers,* you were laid on the Divine Table* as spotless sacrifices.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Strangled and scorched by fire,* you gave up your souls as spotless sacrifices, O glorious martyrs,* into the hands of the Ruler of all,* and were united with the choirs of the Bodiless Powers,* inheriting eternal glory.* Pray that we also may share in it,* we who ceaselessly praise you.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): The sun and the moon beheld You, O Lover of mankind,* hanging upon the tree, O Sun of Righteousness, O my Christ,* and they hid their light;* the foundations of the earth trembled in fear of Your might;* and Your Mother, in anguish of heart, cried out to You:* O sweetest Jesus — glory to Your goodness!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5): From the Lord, who humbled Himself even to crucifixion and death for your sake,* learn, O my soul, that exaltation humbles and humiliation exalts.* Be not proud of your good works, nor think yourself righteous,* condemning your neighbor as the boastful Pharisee did.* But only remembering your own offenses,* think humbly of yourself, and like the Publican, cry:* O God, cleanse me — and save me!

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

From the Lord, who humbled Himself even to crucifixion and death for your sake,* learn, O my soul, that exaltation humbles and humiliation exalts.* Be not proud of your good works, nor think yourself righteous,* condemning your neighbor as the boastful Pharisee did.* But only remembering your own offenses,* think humbly of yourself, and like the Publican, cry:* O God, cleanse me — and save me!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Your martyrs, O Lord, did not deny You* nor depart from Your commandments.* By their prayers, have mercy on us.

Glory, now and ever: When the Most Pure One beheld You hung upon the Cross,* she cried out: O my Son, what strange mystery do I behold?* How is it that You, the Giver of Life, die crucified upon the tree in the flesh?

Your mercy and Your faithfulness shall always preserve me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
(Tone 4): The martyrs drove away the darkness of godlessness* and showed all people the light of the knowledge of God.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
With orthodox teachings, as with divine stars,* you illumined the Church of the Lord,* O most wise Pastors.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
You tore asunder the nets of the demons,* settling forever in inaccessible deserts,* O venerable Fathers.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Desiring the good glory* that stretches into eternity* and never passes away,* you spurned* the glory that passes and perishes.* Taking up your cross,* you followed the Crucified One,* and by ascetic labors you subjected the flesh to the spirit.* Therefore we celebrate your memory, O James.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Crucifying yourself to the world and the passions,* O James,* you kept your soul undefiled and undeceived,* unmixed, O Father, with the images below.* By mystical visions you were lifted* to the images above,* to which from the beginning you consecrated your life, O venerable one,* as Christ's closest friend,* as His most faithful servant.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Guileless and whole,* merciful and brotherly,* humble-minded and gentle,* full of love and adorned with mercy,* patient in waiting,* clothed in faith and hope,* you showed yourself ever-memorable to all,* adorned with prayers* and sacredly illumined by divine rays.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 7): You became a Mother beyond nature, O Theotokos,* and ineffably and incomprehensibly remained a Virgin.* The wonder of your birthgiving no tongue can tell,* for your conception, O Most Pure One, was marvelous,* and the manner of your giving birth inscrutable.* For when God wills, the order of nature is overcome.* Therefore we all, knowing you as Mother of God, earnestly beseech you:* Pray that our souls may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 7): Having enslaved the dignity of my soul to the passions,* I became like a beast* and cannot look up to You, the Most High.* But bowing low, O Christ, like the Publican,* I pray, crying out to You:* O God, cleanse and save me.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Having enslaved the dignity of my soul to the passions,* I became like a beast* and cannot look up to You, the Most High.* But bowing low, O Christ, like the Publican,* I pray, crying out to You:* O God, cleanse and save me.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who fought the good fight and were crowned!* Pray to the Lord* that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever: Daniel the prophet beheld the divine holy mountain,* and wise Isaiah proclaimed you, O Pure One,* the Mother of God who contained in the body the God who created Adam.* Before Him the Cherubim tremble,* ceaselessly hymning with thrice-holy voices* and glorifying the Incarnate One,* beseeching Him to save our souls.

The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 6): Having triumphed over Hades,* You ascended the Cross, O Christ,* to raise with Yourself those dwelling in the darkness of death.* Free among the dead, O Savior Almighty,* who pour forth life from Your own light — have mercy on us!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Today Christ has conquered death* and, as He foretold, has risen, granting joy to the world,* that we all, crying out, may sing this song:* O Fountain of life, O unapproachable Light,* O Savior Almighty — have mercy on us!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

Where shall we sinners flee from You, O Lord,* who are present throughout all creation?* In heaven You dwell; in Hades You conquered death.* In the depths of the sea? Even there is Your hand, O Master!* To You we flee, and falling down before You, we pray:* O You who rose from the dead — have mercy on us!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
In Your Cross, O Christ, we glory,* and we hymn and glorify Your Resurrection,* for You are our God;* apart from You we know no other.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
Ever blessing the Lord,* we hymn His Resurrection,* for having endured the Cross, He destroyed death by death.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
Glory to Your power, O Lord,* for You destroyed him who held the dominion of death* and renewed us by Your Cross,* granting us life and incorruption.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
Your burial, O Lord,* destroyed and shattered the chains of Hades,* and Your Resurrection from the dead illumined the world.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O Father Theophylact,* guarded by divine protectors,* you were preserved unharmed* and revealed as an unshakable pillar of the Church,* unyielding to the temptations* of heretical cunning.* And now pray* that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O Father Theophylact,* having departed from the earth,* you ascended to heaven* and were deemed worthy to dwell in the heavenly tents,* O blessed one, on account of the exile* you endured for Christ's sake.* And now pray* that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O Father Theophylact,* now beholding God, O blessed one,* truly tangibly and visibly,* in the delight of closer union with Him* and in the deification of adoption,* the only Desired and Blessed One,* rejoicing, you have received,* O all-blessed hierarch,* now a fellow citizen of the angels.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 6): Who would not call you blessed, O Most Holy Virgin?* Or who would not hymn your most pure birthgiving?* For the Only-Begotten Son, who shone timelessly from the Father,* came forth from you, O Pure One, ineffably incarnate.* Being God by nature, He became man for our sake,* not divided into two persons* but known in two natures without confusion.* Entreat Him, O pure and all-blessed one,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6
(Tone 6): Your Resurrection, O Christ Savior,* the angels hymn in heaven;* grant also us on earth* to glorify You with a pure heart.
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

As the Almighty, You destroyed the gates of brass* and shattered the bars of Hades,* and You raised up the fallen human race.* Therefore with one voice we cry:* O You who rose from the dead, O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Christ, desiring to raise us from the ancient corruption,* let Himself be nailed to the Cross and laid in the tomb.* The myrrh-bearing women sought Him with tears* and, lamenting, said: Woe to us, O Savior of all!* How did You consent to be placed in a tomb?* You who willingly lay therein — how were You stolen?* How were You carried away? What place has hidden Your life-giving body?* But as You promised us, O Master,* appear and quiet our bitter lamentation.* And while they wept, the angel spoke to them:* Cease your weeping and tell the Apostles that the Lord is risen,* granting the world purification and great mercy.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
Willingly crucified, O Christ,* by Your burial You took death captive,* and on the third day You rose as God in glory,* granting the world unending life and great mercy.

Glory, now and ever: Christ the Lord, my Creator and Savior, O Most Pure One,* came forth from your womb and clothed Himself in me,* delivering Adam from the primordial curse.* Therefore to you, O Most Pure One, as the Mother of God and Virgin,* we ceaselessly sing with the angel:* Rejoice! Truly rejoice, O Sovereign Lady,* Guide, Protection, and salvation of our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 6): The angelic powers were at Your tomb and those who guarded it became as dead; and Mary stood at the grave, seeking Your most pure body. You despoiled Hades and were not tempted thereby. You met the Virgin and granted life. O Lord, risen from the dead, glory to You!
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Turn not Your face from Your servant, for I am in distress; make haste to hear me. Look upon my soul and deliver it.
Verse: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to the grave.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 8): Without number have we sinned before You,* and therefore we await innumerable torments:* the gnashing of teeth, inconsolable weeping,* the fire of Gehenna, darkness, and the abyss of Hades.* O most righteous Judge, grant us tears* that through them we may find forgiveness and absolution from our wicked deeds,* praying in fasting:* O Master Christ, have mercy on us* for the sake of Your great and abundant mercy.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Without number have we sinned before You,* and therefore we await innumerable torments:* the gnashing of teeth, inconsolable weeping,* the fire of Gehenna, darkness, and the abyss of Hades.* O most righteous Judge, grant us tears* that through them we may find forgiveness and absolution from our wicked deeds,* praying in fasting:* O Master Christ, have mercy on us* for the sake of Your great and abundant mercy.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

We who have gone astray on the mountains of grievous transgressions —* seek us, O Word of God, and call us back to Yourself,* driving far from us the wicked habits of our hearts.* Raise the dead to life again and cleanse them by the fast,* for in unceasing weeping we cry out to You:* O Master Christ, have mercy on us* for Your great and abundant mercy!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Beginning the third week of the fast,* let us, O faithful, praise the Most Holy Trinity.* Passing the remaining time in joy* and subduing the desires of the flesh,* let us reap divine blossoms in our souls* and weave crowns for Pascha,* that crowned, we may hymn Christ the Victor.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

Bravely enduring the present* and rejoicing in the hope of things to come,* the holy martyrs spoke to one another:* "It is not clothing we cast off* but the old self we lay aside.* The winter is fierce, but Paradise is fair;* the freezing is painful, but sweet is the reward.* Therefore let us not retreat, O soldiers!* Let us endure a little suffering* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Bravely enduring the present* and rejoicing in the hope of things to come,* the holy martyrs spoke to one another:* "It is not clothing we cast off* but the old self we lay aside.* The winter is fierce, but Paradise is fair;* the freezing is painful, but sweet is the reward.* Therefore let us not retreat, O soldiers!* Let us endure a little suffering* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Casting off all their garments* and entering the lake without trembling,* the holy martyrs spoke to one another:* "For the Paradise we have lost, let us not spare the garments of these corruptible days.* Having once clothed ourselves through the corruption-bearing serpent —* let us now strip ourselves for the sake of the Resurrection of all.* Let us despise the melting ice* and let us hate the flesh,* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Casting off all their garments* and entering the lake without trembling,* the holy martyrs spoke to one another:* "For the Paradise we have lost, let us not spare the garments of these corruptible days.* Having once clothed ourselves through the corruption-bearing serpent —* let us now strip ourselves for the sake of the Resurrection of all.* Let us despise the melting ice* and let us hate the flesh,* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Regarding the torments as delight* and rushing to the freezing lake as to warmth,* the holy martyrs said:* "Let us not fear the winter season — and we shall escape the dread fire of Gehenna.* Let the foot be burned that it may dance forever,* and the hand cut off that it may be raised to the Lord!* Let us not spare our mortal nature;* let us choose death,* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Regarding the torments as delight* and rushing to the freezing lake as to warmth,* the holy martyrs said:* "Let us not fear the winter season — and we shall escape the dread fire of Gehenna.* Let the foot be burned that it may dance forever,* and the hand cut off that it may be raised to the Lord!* Let us not spare our mortal nature;* let us choose death,* that we may be crowned with crowns of victory by Christ God,* the Savior of our souls."

Glory: In hymns let us praise, O faithful,* the passion-bearers — the Forty Martyrs — * and sweetly sing to them, saying:* "Rejoice, O sufferers of Christ:* Hesychius, Meliton, Heraclius,* Smaragdus and Domnus,* Eunoicus, Valens and Vivianus,* Claudius and Priscus!* Rejoice, Theodulus, Eutychius and John,* Xanthias, Helianus, Sisinius,* Cyrion, Agias, Aetius and Flavius!* Rejoice, Acacius, Ecdicius,* Lysimachus, Alexander, Elias and Candidus,* Theophilus, Dometian,* and divine Gaius, and Gorgonius!* Rejoice, Eutychius and Athanasius,* Cyril and Sacerdon,* Nicholas and Valerius,* Philoctimon, Severian,* Chudion and Aglaius!* You have boldness before Christ our God,* O all-honored martyrs,* therefore fervently entreat Him* that we who with faith celebrate your all-sacred memory* may be saved."

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 2): The shadow of the Law has passed* when Grace came.* For as the bush, engulfed in flame, did not burn,* so too, O Virgin, you gave birth and remained a virgin.* Instead of the pillar of fire, the Sun of Righteousness has risen;* instead of Moses — Christ,* the salvation of our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): By instability of spirit we have cast off the Father's guidance,* living in sinful bestial desires.* We have squandered our entire life in prodigal fashion, wretched ones,* deprived of the heart-nourishing food,* gorging ourselves on the passing fat of lusts.* But You, O Good Father, do not close Your merciful heart to us,* but opening it, receive us as the Prodigal Son, and save us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

We have clothed ourselves in the repulsive garment of our transgressions* and have been driven from the chamber of joy.* Therefore clothe us, O God, with Your ineffable compassion,* and as the Prodigal Son, have mercy on us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord! You sanctify every place* and heal every malady.* We beseech you: pray now* that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy.

Glory (Tone 6): And again there shines upon us the fateful memory* of the luminaries of the world, the victorious martyrs,* who illumine the hearts of the faithful —* they who struggled for Christ and for the Cross.* Therefore let us bring a song to Christ God, who crowned them with glory and honor.

Now and ever: Christ the Lord, my Creator and Savior, O Most Pure One,* came forth from your womb and clothed Himself in me,* delivering Adam from the primordial curse.* Therefore to you, O Most Pure One, as Mother of God and Virgin,* we ceaselessly sing with the angel:* Rejoice! Truly rejoice, O Sovereign Lady,* Guide, Protection, and salvation of our souls.

In God we have boasted all the day long, and in Your name we give thanks forever.
Verse: O God, we have heard with our ears; our fathers have told us what You did in their days.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1): Let us love the fast,* which by the action of the Spirit* weakens the fiercest passions of the soul* and gives strength to do the works of God.* It raises our thoughts to heaven,* obtaining for us the forgiveness of sins* granted by the merciful God.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, I, wretched one,* have spent all my life dissolutely.* But as the Prodigal Son, I cry out in repentance:* O heavenly Father, I have sinned!* Cleanse and save me!* Do not cast me away, though I myself departed from You* and am impoverished in godly deeds.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Come, O faithful, and with psalms* and spiritual songs let us greet the holy fast and cry out:* By it we shall spiritually annihilate the serpent, the source of evil!* Therefore let us boldly call upon Christ:* Grant us, O Savior, to blamelessly* behold Your holy Cross and venerate it,* joyfully magnifying it with psalms and songs.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

With the all-praised Quadratus,* let the illustrious and great Cyprian be hymned,* two Dionysiuses and Anectus worthily,* Crescens together and the glorious Paul faithfully,* as God-crowned sufferers of the Lord* and most fervent intercessors for us.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

A second choir of the sacred victorious and triumphant martyrs* has appeared today:* a star-radiant assembly of divine passion-bearers,* a firm number, a chosen army* led by Quadratus,* who has appeared as a glorious conqueror.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

The many-woven webs of the rhetoricians' wisdoms* you, with your incisive word,* scattered like mist, O all-praised Quadratus.* You both taught faithfully and suffered patiently for Christ,* becoming an adornment in both,* and being crowned together with the passion-bearers.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Overcome by hopeless transgressions, O Pure One,* and having acquired You as our only Intercessor, O Virgin,* we cry out with gratitude:* Cleanse us, O most holy Bride of God!* For You are the refuge of the world* and the Protectress of our race.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7

(Tone 7): Having sinned prodigally,* I confess to You, O Lord:* I dare not raise my eyes to heaven,* for from there I, the miserable one, was cast down.* I have sinned against heaven and before You* and am no longer worthy to be called Your child.* I condemn myself, needing no accusers or witnesses.* My profligacy and evil life condemn me;* my nakedness puts me to shame* and the rags I wear expose me to disgrace.* O merciful Father, Only-Begotten Son, Holy Spirit,* accept me who repent and have mercy on me.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

O All-Good Father, from everything You gave me,* my folly has stripped me bare.* I departed from You and fell into bondage to a stranger.* There, tending the swine,* I could not even fill myself with their food.* But knowing Your gracious mercy, I flee to You:* Cover my nakedness and save me, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
When the holy martyrs pray for us* and glorify Christ,* all deception is dispelled* and the human race is saved by faith.

Glory, now and ever: From your virginal womb, the Light of the world shone forth,* by the Word — the Word.* Pray to Him, O pure and most holy Virgin,* that our souls may escape the snares of the enemy.

Great is the Lord and greatly to be praised.
Verse: The Lord our God shall be our guide forever and ever.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 3): O Lord, who by the Cross destroyed the deceiver,* deliver me, a sinner led astray, from his deception.* And having cleansed me by fasting,* grant me, O Master, to do Your will,* that I may compassionately behold* Your holy sufferings.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, I bear wounds from the passions* and have mercilessly put myself to death.* O Master, who allowed Yourself to be pierced by the lance,* heal and revive, as the Merciful One, my wretched soul* wounded by the arrow of the enemy,* that I may have manifest part in Your sufferings.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Lord, illumine our souls by fasting,* that we may be deemed worthy to behold without condemnation* and to venerate Your Cross with fear and love,* for it is the sign of Your voluntary Passion,* which deign to let us reach, as Lover of mankind.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

By divine Providence* prophetically called Sophronius —* sharing the name of integral wisdom —* you were chaste in deeds,* righteous, courageous, and wise,* worthily crowned with the virtues of your name.* Wisely you rendered what was due to both* soul and body,* as the most discerning judge and steward.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

From your God-speaking lips* you thundered with theological teachings,* O all-blessed Sophronius,* theologizing most clearly about the Beginningless Father,* the Co-Beginningless Son,* and the Holy Spirit, co-eternal in being:* in Unity — Trinity* and in Trinity — Unity:* God, one and the same in essence.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Most wisely you taught, O all-wise one,* that the Incorporeal Word, Co-Beginningless with the Father,* was united with the flesh hypostatically,* without change or confusion,* with the double activity of both natures,* from which He is composed and in which He is contemplated —* the One who exists,* undivided in essence* and known in both natures.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): The sun and the moon beheld You, O Lover of mankind,* hung upon the tree, O Sun of Righteousness, O my Christ,* and hid their light.* The foundations of the earth trembled with fear before Your power,* and Your Mother, in anguish of heart, cried out to You:* O sweetest Jesus — glory to Your goodness!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2

(Tone 2): O Lord, though I am an earthborn creature,* yet, being made in Your likeness,* I dare to call You, the Creator, my Father.* Living like the Prodigal Son,* through sin I lost the grace of sonship;* and forgetting Your gifts, I squandered my wealth.* But, lest You disown me,* You allowed Your Only-Begotten Son* to endure the Cross and bodily death for my sake.* Therefore make me happy, receiving me back again* as Your own, O Lover of mankind.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Though the dignity of the good Father's son was upon me,* I, in my folly, did not understand it* and deprived myself of glory,* sinfully squandering the wealth of grace.* Not having enough divine nourishment,* I fawned upon the loathsome stranger,* who sent me to his own field, ruinous for the soul.* In the slavery of passions and the deception of life, my days passed* in a pasture with cattle, yet without satisfaction.* Yet I return and cry out to You,* O merciful and compassionate Father:* I have sinned against heaven and before You —* I beseech You, save me.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
The multitude of Your saints, O Christ,* prays for us,* therefore have mercy and save us, as Lover of mankind.

Glory, now and ever: You, O Most Pure One, endured unspeakable pains* at the crucifixion of Your Son and God,* weeping and wailing through your tears:* Woe is me, O sweetest Child!* Why do You, accepting unjust suffering,* desire to save the descendants of Adam?* But we with faith pray to You, O Most Pure Virgin:* Entreat mercy for us from Your Son.

I trust in the mercy of God forever and ever.
Verse: Why do you boast of evil, O mighty man?

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 6): O God-pleasing Apostles!* You, who appeared as rays of the spiritual Sun,* obtain illumination for our souls* and deliverance from the dark and murky passions.* Pray that by fasting and prayer* we may cleanse our hearts, enslaved by the Evil One,* and be able to behold the day of salvation.* Being saved by faith, we always honor you,* for you have guarded the world by your most wise preaching.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The Prodigal Son, having entered the land of wickedness,* lived as I do now.* O merciful Father, You gave me riches,* yet I languish and hunger without good works,* and the shame of sin has enveloped me,* for I have lost, through my own fault, the garment of God's grace.* Therefore I cry to You:* I have sinned before You,* but Your goodness is also known to me.* Accept me, even among the hired servants, O Merciful One —* by the prayers of the Apostles* who loved You, O Christ.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O Apostles of the Savior, luminaries of the world,* benefactors and helpers in human salvation!* Stars of wonders and signs of healings — this is your glory.* You declare the glory of God and tell of the heavens.* Constantly bring your prayers to the Lord* that He may accept our prayers as a sweet fragrance* and grant us to behold with awe and to kiss the life-giving Cross.* Send Your mercy, O Lover of mankind and Savior, to us also* who venerate the Cross.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O God-wise Father Theophanes,* named after the Theophany of Christ,* you followed His life-giving footsteps* and forsook the beauties of life,* having the Desired One before your eyes,* wondrously deified by His beauty* and by the divine attraction to Him,* most wondrous and perfect.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O God-wise Father Theophanes,* the bitter exile,* dwelling in infirmity,* you endured with fortitude, O all-praised one,* not sparing your body for the sacred icons,* driven by the fury of lions.* You mocked their conspiracies* and their truly vain-wise schemes* and their mind of perdition — turned into madness.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Truly the reward for your pains* the Bestower of good things most generously granted you:* giving you, O thrice-blessed, the power* to drive away demons* and to heal infirmities,* granting you the ineffable joy* where the legions of angels rejoice,* ever contemplating* the face of the Almighty.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Rejoice, O Chariot of the divine Light!* Rejoice, O Temple and Tabernacle of sanctification of the Lord!* Light has dawned upon us* from your most pure womb,* illuminating the ends of the earth* and sanctifying us by grace.* Rejoice, O Foundation of salvation, O Most Pure One!* Rejoice, O awesome Proclamation and Praise* of those who hope in You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): Having squandered my Father's wealth,* I became destitute* and settled in a land of wicked citizens.* Having become like senseless and irrational beasts,* I lost all divine grace.* But, returning, I cry to You, O merciful and compassionate Father:* I have sinned; accept me in repentance, O God, and have mercy on me.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Like the Prodigal Son, I too, wretched one, have lost the dignity of sonship.* I eat the food of a bondsman along with the swine,* yet such food does not satisfy me.* Therefore I return to You, as the merciful Father* whom I rashly forsook.* Now returning humbly, I cry out in repentance:* I have sinned against heaven and before You, O Father,* but grant me conversion and great mercy.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Our God has glorified His chosen and holy ones.* Rejoice and be glad, all you His servants,* for He has prepared crowns for you and His Kingdom.* Therefore we entreat you: Forget not us as well.

Glory, now and ever: O Theotokos, you are the true vine* that brought forth the Fruit of life.* We implore you:* Intercede, O Lady, with the Apostles and all the Saints,* that our souls may be granted mercy.

The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)
Now and ever: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of sins. (Prostration)
Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Have mercy on me, O God, for man tramples on me; all day long an attacker oppresses me.
Verse: My enemies trample on me all day long, for many attack me proudly.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 5
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 5): You, O Savior, are the true consolation.* You sweetened the bitter waters of Meribah with the wood* that was a type of Your holy Cross.* On it You allowed Yourself to be crucified and tasted gall.* Your side was pierced,* and water of forgiveness flowed forth for the whole world,* to renew our earthly nature.* Therefore we glorify Your ineffable power and pray:* In this time of fasting, grant us, O Lord, fear of You,* forgiveness of all sins, and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

By Your crucifixion on the Cross, O Word,* You gathered into one those who had already wandered far from You.* Raise my thoughts from the mire of the passions,* enrich me with every virtue,* grant my heart Your most pure fear* and my soul — perfect love,* which turns me from bodily desire,* that in these days I may please You by abstinence, supplication, and prayer,* and, deemed worthy of great mercy,* behold the bright day of Your Resurrection.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Grant us, who have completed the third week of fasting,* to behold the life-giving tree of Your Cross, O Christ the Word,* and to venerate it with due reverence.* Grant us worthily to hymn and glorify Your might,* to exalt Your sufferings,* that pure, we may reach the glorious and holy Resurrection — the mystical Pascha —* through which Adam again entered Paradise.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

The streams of your wisdom,* drawn by the clouds of the mind,* pour forth upon the world a fount of Orthodoxy.* From them we, the faithful, drawing sweetness,* turn away from the flood of heresies* as from the bitter waters of Meribah.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Your most pure body,* lying in the tomb, did not decay in the least,* but was found whole, O all-blessed Nicephorus.* Having carefully translated it,* the children of Orthodoxy rejoiced,* glorifying you with good renown, O God-wise one.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You, who became a temple of God* by divine deeds,* are received by the temple honored with the Apostolic name —* you, O all-blessed, who were sacredly buried after burial* and who buried with yourself* all heretical vain talk.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): The ancient bitter tasting of Adam You sweetened,* having tasted gall and vinegar, O sweetest Son, lifted upon the tree.* Therefore, rising as the Almighty,* be also to me, O righteous Judge,* a sweetness by Your healing Passion —* said the Virgin, weeping.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6

(Tone 6): O Christ God, hung upon the wood of the Cross,* You are the life of all.* Therefore revive my soul as well, put to death by sins,* and do not allow, O Good Shepherd,* Your sheep to perish —* the one that has strayed from Your commandments* and by sinful deeds has squandered the treasure of sinlessness You granted.* Living like the Prodigal Son, she has wasted everything* and defiled herself with impure acts.* But bring her to repentance and renew her,* O only Most Merciful.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Led astray by evil thoughts,* I have spent my life in sensuality.* Thus the treasure of my Father's gift has been squandered.* Delighting in impure desires* and not observing the saving commandments,* I have become like a senseless beast.* But You, O Christ God, who deigned to be crucified for us,* do not reject me from Your sonship,* for I return to You as the Prodigal Son —* receive and save me.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Your martyrs, O Lord, did not deny You* nor depart from Your commandments.* By their prayers, have mercy on us.

Glory, now and ever: When the All-Pure One beheld You, O Christ,* hanging upon the Cross, she cried out:* What dread mystery do I see, O my Son?* How is it that You, the Giver of Life, die hanging on the tree of the Cross in the flesh?

The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)

Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.

Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.

More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
