prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 7)
O God, You are my defender, and Your mercy shall come to meet me.
Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 7
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 7): Like the Prodigal Son, I departed from Your grace, O Lord,* and squandered the riches of Your goodness.* Therefore I flee to You, the Merciful One, and cry:* I have sinned, O God — have mercy on me!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
Celebrating the memory of Your holy passion-bearers,* we glorify You, O Christ, crying out:* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
In the midst of the court of the lawless,* Your passion-bearers joyfully sang:* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having truly renounced the world by faith and love from your youth, O venerable Father,* you joyfully followed the crucified Christ,* and having mortified the flesh by many labors,* you abundantly received the grace of healing,* curing various infirmities and driving away the spirits of wickedness,* becoming a great marvel for all.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Having become an adornment of monasticism,* you gathered an innumerable army* to hymn the Lord, O venerable Father,* and you set all upon the heavenly path* who worthily followed your divine teachings* and imitated your virtuous life, O Blessed One.* And again you gathered them as one at your repose, O Benedict.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

As Elijah of old, O Father,* you brought down rain from heaven by divine prayer,* caused the vessel to pour forth oil,* and raised the dead,* and performed countless other wonders* to the full glory of God and Savior, O venerable one.* Therefore we celebrate your memory in God* with love, O Benedict.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 6): Who would not call you blessed, O Most Holy Virgin?* Or who would not hymn your most pure birthgiving?* For the Only-Begotten Son, who shone timelessly from the Father,* came forth from you, O Pure One, ineffably incarnate.* Being God by nature, He became man for our sake,* not divided into two persons* but known in two natures without confusion.* Entreat Him, O pure and all-blessed one,* to have mercy on our souls.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 2

(Tone 2, Podoben: "When from the tree"): By Your life-giving death, O Master,* You conquered the power of death and corruption* and granted eternal life, giving resurrection to the departed.* Therefore we pray, O Savior:* Grant rest to those who with faith have passed on to You,* and deem them worthy, O Lover of mankind, of Your eternal glory.
Verse: Blessed are those whom You have chosen and received, O Lord.

Before Your dread, awesome, and fearful throne, O Christ,* all who have died from the ages stand and await Your just verdict.* Grant rest, O Savior, to our fathers, brothers, and sisters* who with faith have departed to You,* and settle them where ineffable joy abides.
Verse: Their souls shall dwell among good things.

I glorify Your holy Cross, O only Most Merciful,* by which You granted life and utter delight to those* who lovingly hymn You.* And I cry out to You, O Christ God:* Grant rest to those who have departed from us* and settle them among Your saints.

Glory, now and ever: We hymn you, O Theotokos, as a spiritual candle* that brought forth the light of the Godhead* united with human nature.* Therefore pray to Your Son and our God* to grant rest to those who have departed from us* to the joyful dwelling of the Saints.

troparia.x: Troparia
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 7
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 7): Come, let us rejoice in the Lord,* who conquered the dominion of death and enlightened the human race,* crying out with the bodiless ones:* O our Creator and Savior — glory to You!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
You endured the Cross and burial for us, O Savior,* and as God, You put death to death by death.* Therefore we worship Your Resurrection on the third day.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

The Apostles, seeing the Resurrection of the Creator,* marveled, singing angelic praise:* This is the glory of the Church, this is the treasure of the Kingdom.* O Lord, who suffered for us — glory to You!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Though the lawless seized You, O Christ,* You are my God, and I am not ashamed.* They scourged You on the back, and I do not deny it.* They nailed You to the Cross, and I do not hide it.* I glory in Your Resurrection, for Your death is my life.* O Lord Almighty and Lover of mankind — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

Christ, fulfilling the prophecy of David,* revealed His majesty to His disciples in Zion,* showing Himself glorified together with the Father and the Holy Spirit,* ever worthy of praise and glory:* as the Word who was incorporeal from the beginning,* then for our sake took on flesh,* and as man was put to death* and rose by His own power, as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

You descended to Hades, O Christ, as You willed,* and as God and Master conquered death,* and rose on the third day,* co-raising Adam from the bonds of Hades and corruption, who cried out:* Glory to Your Resurrection, O only Lover of mankind!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Shine forth, O Cross of the Lord,* with the bright rays of your grace,* upon the hearts of those who honor you* and who, with love for God, embrace you, O hope of the world.* By you tears of sorrow are wiped away,* we are delivered from the snare of death,* and we attain unceasing joy.* Show forth the grandeur of your beauty* and give the reward of abstinence to your servants,* who faithfully implore your great protection and abundant mercy.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Rejoice, O life-giving Cross,* fair paradise of the Church,* tree of incorruption* that made the delight of eternal glory blossom for us!* You put to flight the legions of demons,* the angelic hosts rejoice,* and the assemblies of the faithful celebrate.* O invincible weapon,* unshakable fortress, glory of priests!* Grant us now also to reach the Passion of Christ and His great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Rejoice, O Cross of the Lord,* by which the curse of humanity has been destroyed;* the sign of eternal joy, whose most glorious appearance puts the enemies to flight!* Our help, the might of kings,* the glory of the righteous, the adornment of worshipers!* Signing ourselves with you, we deprive evil of its power;* by you we are saved, O weapon of peace.* The angels sing of it with awe —* the power of the Cross that grants us great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Come, O our forebears,* who through the malice of the man-slayer fell from the heights,* through the bitter pleasure of tasting the fruit from the tree of old.* Behold, the all-venerable tree truly comes forth!* Come therefore to it* and joyfully kiss it, crying out in faith:* O most precious Cross, our defense!* O tree blessed by God, O heavenly garden!* Tasting your fruit, we have attained the incorruption of our original Paradise,* receiving with assurance its great mercy.

Glory (Tone 3): O Christ our God, You voluntarily accepted crucifixion* for the universal resurrection of the human race,* and with the reed of the Cross, dipping Your fingers in royal blood,* You deigned out of love for mankind to sign our pardon.* Do not despise us, wretched as we are, who have again distanced ourselves from You;* but have pity, O only Long-Suffering One, on the misery of Your people.* Arise and conquer those who assail us,* for You are almighty.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 7): You became a Mother beyond nature, O Theotokos,* and ineffably and incomprehensibly remained a Virgin.* The wonder of your birthgiving no tongue can tell,* for your conception, O Most Pure, was marvelous,* and the manner of your giving birth inscrutable.* For when God wills, the order of nature is overcome.* Therefore we all, knowing you as the Mother of God, earnestly beseech you:* Pray that our souls may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7
(Tone 7): You rose from the tomb, O Savior of the world,* and with Your body raised up mankind.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.
Come, let us worship Him who rose from the dead* and enlightened all,* for He delivered us from the torments of Hades* by His Resurrection on the third day,* granting us life and great mercy.
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.
Descending to Hades, O Christ, You took death captive,* and rising on the third day, You raised us with Yourself,* we who glorify Your almighty Resurrection,* O Lord who loves mankind.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.
O Lord, You appeared wondrous, as though sleeping in the tomb,* but rising on the third day as the Almighty, You co-raised Adam, who cried:* Glory to Your Resurrection, O only Lover of mankind!

Glory, now and ever (Tone 4): You, O Lord, helped humble David* to conquer the alien stranger;* so also help Your holy Church* and by the weapon of the Cross subdue our enemies.* Show us, O Merciful One, Your mercies of old,* that we may truly know that You are our God.* Trusting in You, we conquer,* and we earnestly beseech Your Most Pure Mother* that we may be given great mercy.

(Tone 7): You shattered death by Your Cross; You opened paradise to the thief; You changed the weeping of the myrrh-bearers and commanded Your apostles to preach: You are risen, O Christ our God, granting great mercy to the world.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.

More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
You have given those who fear Your name an inheritance, O Lord.
Verse: Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 7
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 7): As the Prodigal Son I come to You, O Merciful One.* Receive me, falling at Your feet, among Your hired servants, O God,* and have mercy on me, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Like those who fell among thieves and were wounded,* so we have fallen into a multitude of sins, and our soul is wounded.* To whom then shall we run, being guilty?* Only to You, O merciful Physician of souls:* Pour out upon us, O God, Your great mercy.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Do not cut us sinners down like the barren fig tree,* but leave us for more years,* watering our souls with tears of repentance,* that we may bring forth fruits of charity.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
Being the Sun of Righteousness,* illumine the hearts of those who sing to You:* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

O Lord, You willingly stretched out Your hands upon the Cross.* Therefore, as good and Lover of mankind,* grant also us, enlightened by fasting, prayer, abstinence, and charity,* to worship it with a contrite heart.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Lord, cleanse the multitude of our sins* by the abundance of Your mercies, O All-Merciful,* and deign, as Lover of mankind, to let us behold Your Cross with a pure soul* and kiss it in the coming week of fasting.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

O wondrous sight!* We behold the tree on which Christ willingly was crucified in the flesh.* The world therefore bows down and, being enlightened, cries:* O power of the Cross! Your very sight burns the demons,* and the sign of your cross, like fire, destroys them.* We magnify you, O most glorious Tree,* we honor and with awe venerate you,* and glorify God, who through you gives us life everlasting.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

How shall we call you, O glorious one?* A mighty warrior of the King and God,* who manfully overthrew the ungodliness of the bodiless ones,* a luminary shining with spiritual light,* a most powerful destroyer of idols,* a most fervent athlete,* a lawful sufferer.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

How shall we now call you, O glorious one?* A river of life-bearing water* that pours on us spiritual abundance,* an inexhaustible flood of healings for those in trouble,* a cup overflowing with sacred drink,* a most true wonder-worker,* a peer of the bodiless ones.* Pray that our souls may be saved.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

How shall we call you, O holy one?* A guide of the lost, an intercessor for sinners,* a true physician amid the storms of misfortune,* a fragrant sprout of Paradise most precious,* a first-fruit and foundation of the martyrs,* an ever-flowing fount of wonders,* a valiant champion.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): To whom have you become like, O miserable soul,* in no way arising to repentance* and having no dread of the fire awaiting the wicked?* Arise and call upon the one swift to help,* and cry out: O Virgin Mother,* pray to Your Son and our God* to deliver me from the snare of the deceiver!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): We dare not, in our wretchedness, for our wicked deeds,* lift our eyes to heaven.* But like the Publican, groaning, we cry to You:* O God, cleanse us sinners,* and as the only good One, deliver us from the hypocrisy of the Pharisee.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

We dare not, in our wretchedness, for our wicked deeds,* lift our eyes to heaven.* But like the Publican, groaning, we cry to You:* O God, cleanse us sinners,* and as the only good One, deliver us from the hypocrisy of the Pharisee.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord! You sanctify every place* and heal every infirmity.* We beseech you: pray now* that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy.
Glory, now and ever: The heavenly choirs hymn your praises, O full of grace, Virgin Mother,* and we glorify your ineffable birthgiving, O Theotokos.* Pray that our souls may be saved.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8, Podoben: "How shall we call you"): Having reached today the midpoint of the fast,* strengthened by the power of the Cross,* let us glorify the Savior Lord who ascended upon it in the midst of the earth, and cry:* Grant us, O Master,* to behold Your Passion and holy Resurrection,* bestowing on us purification and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Let us tame the bodily passions by abstinence from food and renunciation of pleasures.* With faith let us kiss the wood of the Cross,* for it is set forth for veneration* and sanctifies all by divine grace.* Let us cry out to the Lord:* We thank You, O Merciful One,* for by it You save our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 8): Venerating the Cross today, let us all cry out:* Rejoice, O Tree of Life!* Rejoice, O holy scepter of Christ!* Rejoice, O heavenly glory of mankind!* Rejoice, O praise of rulers!* Rejoice, strength of faith! Rejoice, invincible weapon!* Rejoice, O banisher of enemies!* Rejoice, radiant light that saves the world!* Rejoice, great glory of martyrs!* Rejoice, power of the righteous!* Rejoice, light of the angels!* Rejoice, O all-venerable Cross!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

We know you as a Man of God* by calling and in truth,* for you shone with virtues:* having experienced boundless poverty and hardships on earth,* you confirmed the faithful by miracles.* Therefore pray that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

With the dew of divine love* you quenched the desires of the flesh, O Alexius.* You wisely chose the Wedding Chamber above all chambers* and the divine likeness of the angels over bodily sweetness.* With them pray that our souls may be granted* peace and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

You lived unrecognized, O wise one,* at the gate of your noble parents,* enduring the mockery of your own servants for a long time.* And in dying, by miracles* you revealed your feat,* healing infirmities* and driving away unclean spirits.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Grievously ailing* with wicked passions is my soul.* Heal it, O Most Holy One,* who gave birth to the Healer and Savior of all, Christ,* who healed every wound of mankind,* wounded by the evil design of the devil,* and led us out of death.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): The many-worded Pharisee was rejected for his boasting,* while the humble Publican who prayed in silence was justified.* Therefore, O my soul, seeing the difference between them,* choose humility and take it to heart,* for Christ, as Lover of mankind,* promised to give His grace to the humble.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

The many-worded Pharisee was rejected for his boasting,* while the humble Publican who prayed in silence was justified.* Therefore, O my soul, seeing the difference between them,* choose humility and take it to heart,* for Christ, as Lover of mankind,* promised to give His grace to the humble.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord, pray to our God* and entreat an abundance of mercies upon our souls* and cleansing from our multitude of sins, we beseech you.
Glory, now and ever: O Theotokos Virgin, Mother of God,* I take refuge under your holy protection,* for I know well that you heal the wounds of my soul.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 6, Podoben: "Having placed all their hope"): In the midst of the earth You endured, O Merciful One,* suffering and the Cross,* granting all redemption and dispassion.* Today, at the midpoint of the fast,* we set forth the holy Cross* and all venerate it.* Kissing it with joy, we beseech:* Grant us, O Word of God,* in the light of divine virtues to behold Your Passion* and life-giving Resurrection, O only Most Merciful.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O Long-Suffering One, You hung dead upon the Cross* and were pierced by the lance* and given gall, O Master,* who by the hand of Moses made sweet the bitter waters of Meribah.* Therefore I beseech and ask You:* Free my heart from the bitterness of the passions* and sweeten my soul with the honey of repentance,* revealing me as a worshiper* of Your holy sufferings.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Mighty is the Cross that Moses prefigured of old* with outstretched hands when he conquered Amalek.* Today it is set forth, magnificently presented to our eyes.* Let us touch it with a pure mind and lips,* for upon it Christ ascended and put death to death.* Having been granted such great grace,* let us glorify the Savior with God-inspired hymns* and pray to reach* the saving Resurrection.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Come, let us venerate today the tree that brought us life —* the Cross of Christ our God.* For by it death was wounded* and the Resurrection was again revealed to us who had fallen.* Let us cry to the Deliverer:* You who willingly suffered for us to save the world —* O our God, glory to You!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

As an invincible power,* Your Cross, O our Savior, was given to us Christians,* by which the multitudes of pagans are overthrown.* Your Church, O Christ, that truly glorifies You,* it overshadows with peace.* Kissing it now,* we fervently cry out to You:* Grant that we also become partakers* in the lot of Your Saints.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having purified the senses of our souls with tears* and washed ourselves with fasting,* come, let us venerate the wood of the Cross.* For by it, through abstinence,* the soul-destroying impulses are vanquished.* Let us cry to the Crucified One:* Grant, O Savior, to venerate* the splendor of Your Resurrection on the third day.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 8): Now the Master of creation and Lord of glory* is nailed to the Cross and pierced in the side.* The Sweetness of the Church tastes gall and vinegar.* He who covers the heavens with clouds is crowned with thorns,* clothed in a garment of mockery.* He who fashioned man with His hand is struck by a mortal hand.* He who clothes the heavens with clouds is smitten on the back.* He receives spitting and wounds, reproach and blows.* All this He endures for me, the condemned one,* to save the world from deception,* for He is the Deliverer and merciful God.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 3

(Tone 3): I surpassed the Publican in sins* but did not imitate his repentance,* nor did I fulfill the Pharisee's good deeds* but only imitated his boasting.* But You, O Christ God, who by Your extraordinary humility* brought low the arrogance of the devil on the Cross,* keep us far from the former malice of the Publican and the madness of the Pharisee,* establishing in our souls the good* that was in each of them, and save us.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

I surpassed the Publican in sins* but did not imitate his repentance,* nor did I fulfill the Pharisee's good deeds* but only imitated his boasting.* But You, O Christ God, who by Your extraordinary humility* brought low the arrogance of the devil on the Cross,* keep us far from the former malice of the Publican and the madness of the Pharisee,* establishing in our souls the good* that was in each of them, and save us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Prophets, apostles of Christ, and martyrs* taught us to hymn the consubstantial Trinity* and enlightened the nations led astray,* making the sons of men fellow citizens of the angels.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 5): All creation, beholding You* hanging naked upon the Cross,* O Architect and Creator of all,* was transformed with fear and lamented.* The sun hid its light and the earth quaked,* the rocks split and the radiant veil of the Temple was torn.* The dead rose from the tombs* and the angelic powers were aghast, saying:* O wonder! The Judge is judged* and suffers willingly* for the salvation and renewal of the world!

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1): Let us now wash our souls with the water of fasting* and approach the life-giving Cross of the Lord.* Let us venerate it with faith* and draw divine illumination from it,* finding today* eternal salvation, peace, and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

O all-holy Cross — praise of the Apostles!* Dominions, powers, and angels surround you.* Save those who venerate you from every infirmity* and grant us to complete in holiness the course of the divine fast* and to reach the bright Resurrection by which we are saved.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 7): Today we venerate the Cross of the Lord and cry out:* Rejoice, O Tree of Life that destroyed the kingdom of Hades!* Rejoice, joy of the world, for you have annihilated corruption!* Rejoice, for by your power the demons are put to flight!* Rejoice, O support and invincible weapon of the faithful!* We pray: protect and sanctify those who kiss you.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Having received the illumination of divine knowledge,* you enlightened the eyes of your heart, O wise one,* and wisely drove away the darkness of deception.* You confessed Christ, the Lord of all who assumed flesh.* Strengthened by Him and by the power of the Spirit, O Chrysanthus,* you showed yourself superior to all torments, O all-praised.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The snares of the enemy and the frenzy of pleasure* you considered as a spider's web.* Placed in a gloomy prison,* you were illumined by divine radiance* and filled with spiritual fragrance amid the stench.* As the most excellent wedding steward,* you brought to Christ an undefiled bride —* the one who sought to defile you.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Wounded by the sweetest longing for the Creator,* you finally turned from the deception of godlessness* and found your place within the divine bridal chamber,* becoming a bride of Christ through the many sufferings of the body,* O great-named Daria,* divine vessel of the Spirit,* adornment of women combatants,* glory of virgins.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 8): Today the Unapproachable in essence* becomes approachable for me* and suffers the Passion,* He who delivers me from the passions.* He who gives light to the blind* is spat upon by lawless lips* and gives His back to scourging for the captives' sake.* When the pure Virgin and Mother beheld Him on the Cross,* she cried in anguish:* Woe is me, my Child!* What have You done?* Beautiful in beauty beyond all mankind,* You appear breathless and without comeliness,* having neither form nor beauty.* Woe is me, my Light!* I cannot bear to see You sleeping.* I am wounded to the depths* and a cruel sword pierces my heart.* I praise Your Passion* and worship Your compassion.* O Long-Suffering Lord — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): The fast, a fountain of good works, has now reached its midpoint.* In past days it brought us contentment,* and for the future it has shown profit,* for effort in what is good strengthens piety.* Therefore let us joyfully cry to Christ, the Giver of all good things:* O You who fasted and endured the crucifixion for us,* grant us to become worthy partakers of Your divine Pascha,* that living in peace we may glorify You with the Father and the Spirit.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

(Tone 5): Doers of good works, expecting their reward,* do not boast in the marketplaces and crossroads* but keep it in the heart.* He who sees in secret rewards us for our abstinence.* Therefore let us pass this fast not with gloomy faces* but praying joyfully in the chambers of our souls, ceaselessly crying:* Our Father, who art in heaven,* lead us not into temptation, we pray,* but deliver us from the Evil One.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

With insatiable love of soul,* you did not renounce Christ, O holy martyrs.* You endured fierce and varied tortures of passion,* cast down the pride of the tormentors,* and preserved your faith unshaken and undamaged.* You have passed to the heavens.* Therefore, having boldness before Him,* entreat that He grant us great mercy.

Glory, now and ever: Blessed do we call You, O Theotokos Virgin,* and we glorify You, O faithful, as is fitting:* the unshakable city, the impregnable wall,* the steadfast Protectress,* and the refuge of our souls.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 4
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 4): O Lord, we faithful,* deemed worthy to kiss Your all-blessed Cross by which You saved us,* hymn Your compassion and earnestly pray:* Give us all, O Savior, the joy of Your salvation,* granting us the grace to see in repentance* Your holy sufferings and Your Resurrection.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Stretching out upon the Cross, You endured death,* putting death to death and raising the dead by Your life-giving word.* Therefore I pray: O Lord, revive my soul slain by sin!* Grant me, O Lover of mankind, in these holy days of fasting,* repentance and deliverance from evil deeds.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Deemed worthy with joy to behold and kiss Your holy Cross,* we pray, O God our Savior,* that strengthened by fasting we may reach Your most pure Passion.* Venerating it, let us hymn the Crucifixion, the lance, the sponge, and the reed,* by which You, O Lover of mankind, made us immortal* and led us back to our former life of delight.* Therefore, with thanksgiving, we glorify You today.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Of all desires most fervently,* loving the One unto the end, O venerable ones,* you counted the beauties of this life as refuse.* By vigils and prayers, in cold and heat,* you acquired the one thing needful that remains forever.* As fellow dwellers,* you appeared as fellow citizens of the angels by grace.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Under the blows of rods, under a hail of stones,* under the cutting of weapons,* you did not break your unity, O martyrs,* bound by brotherly love and desire.* Slain together and hewn limb from limb, O passion-bearers,* you were placed on the Divine Table as spotless sacrifices.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Strangled and scorched by fire,* you surrendered your souls as spotless offerings, O glorious martyrs,* into the hands of the Ruler of all.* United with the choirs of the Bodiless Powers,* you inherited eternal glory.* Pray that we also may share in it,* we who ceaselessly praise you.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): The sun and the moon beheld You, O Lover of mankind,* hung upon the tree, O Sun of Righteousness, O my Christ,* and hid their light.* The foundations of the earth trembled with fear before Your power,* and Your Mother, in anguish of heart, cried to You:* O sweetest Jesus — glory to Your goodness!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6

(Tone 6): From the Lord who humbled Himself even to crucifixion and death for your sake,* learn, O my soul, that exaltation humbles and humiliation exalts.* Be not proud of your good works, nor think yourself righteous,* condemning your neighbor as the boastful Pharisee.* But remembering only your own offenses,* think humbly of yourself and like the Publican cry:* O God, cleanse me — and save me!

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

From the Lord who humbled Himself even to crucifixion and death for your sake,* learn, O my soul, that exaltation humbles and humiliation exalts.* Be not proud of your good works, nor think yourself righteous,* condemning your neighbor as the boastful Pharisee.* But remembering only your own offenses,* think humbly of yourself and like the Publican cry:* O God, cleanse me — and save me!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Your martyrs, O Lord, did not deny You* nor depart from Your commandments.* By their prayers, have mercy on us.

Glory, now and ever: When the Most Pure One beheld You hung upon the Cross,* she cried: O my Son, what strange mystery do I see?* How is it that You, the Giver of Life, die crucified upon the tree in the flesh?

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 7)
O God, You are my defender, and Your mercy shall come to meet me.
Verse: Deliver me from my enemies, O God; protect me from those who rise up against me.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 7
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
The martyrs drove away the darkness of godlessness* and showed all people the light of the knowledge of God.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
With orthodox teachings, as with divine stars,* you illumined the Church of the Lord,* O most wise Pastors.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
You tore asunder the nets of the demons,* settling forever in inaccessible deserts,* O venerable Fathers.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Desiring the good glory* that stretches into eternity and never passes away,* you disdained the glory that passes and perishes.* Taking up your cross,* you followed the Crucified One* and subjected the flesh to the spirit by ascetic labors.* Therefore we celebrate your memory, O James.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Crucifying yourself to the world and the passions, O James,* you kept your soul undefiled and undeceived,* unmixed, O Father, with earthly images.* By mystical visions you were lifted to heavenly images,* to which from the beginning you consecrated your life, O venerable one,* as Christ's closest friend,* as His most faithful servant.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Guileless and whole, merciful and brotherly,* humble-minded and gentle,* full of love and adorned with mercy,* patient in waiting,* clothed in faith and hope,* you showed yourself ever-memorable to all,* adorned with prayers* and sacredly illumined by divine rays.

Glory, now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 7): You became a Mother beyond nature, O Theotokos,* and ineffably and incomprehensibly remained a Virgin.* The wonder of your birthgiving no tongue can tell,* for your conception, O Most Pure, was marvelous,* and the manner of your giving birth inscrutable.* For when God wills, the order of nature is overcome.* Therefore we all, knowing you as the Mother of God, earnestly beseech you:* Pray that our souls may be saved.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7

(Tone 7): Having enslaved the dignity of my soul to the passions,* I became like a beast* and cannot look up to You, the Most High.* But bowing low, O Christ, like the Publican,* I pray, crying to You:* O God, cleanse and save me.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Having enslaved the dignity of my soul to the passions,* I became like a beast* and cannot look up to You, the Most High.* But bowing low, O Christ, like the Publican,* I pray, crying to You:* O God, cleanse and save me.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O holy martyrs, who fought the good fight and were crowned!* Pray to the Lord that our souls may be saved.

Glory, now and ever: Daniel the prophet beheld the divine holy mountain,* and wise Isaiah proclaimed you, O Pure One,* the Mother of God who contained in the body the God who created Adam.* Before Him the Cherubim tremble,* ceaselessly singing with thrice-holy voices* and glorifying the Incarnate One,* beseeching Him to save our souls.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 6)
The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
Verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 8): An evening hymn and spiritual worship* we offer to You, O Christ,* for You were pleased to have mercy on us by Your Resurrection.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
O Lord, O Lord,* do not cast us from Your presence,* but be pleased to have mercy on us by Your Resurrection.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Rejoice, O holy Zion,* mother of churches, dwelling-place of God!* For you were the first to receive the forgiveness of sins through the Resurrection.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

The Word of God the Father, begotten before all ages,* and in the last times willingly incarnate of the Most Pure One,* endured crucifixion and death,* and saved humanity long dead by His Resurrection.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
We glorify Your Resurrection from the dead, O Christ,* for by it You delivered the race of Adam from the torment of Hades,* and as God gave the world life eternal and great mercy.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.
Glory to You, O Christ the Savior,* Only-Begotten Son of God, who was nailed to the Cross* and rose from the tomb on the third day.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

We glorify You, O Lord,* for You willingly endured the Cross for us.* We worship You, O Almighty Savior.* Do not cast us from Your presence,* but hear us and save us by Your Resurrection, O Lover of mankind.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O venerable Father John,* you ever truly proclaimed* the praises of God,* learning the creative God-inspired words, O all-wise one.* And enriched by the grace that flows from them,* you became blessed,* overcoming all the evil designs of the impious.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable and all-glorious Father John,* cleansing your soul by streams of tears* and appeasing God by all-night vigils,* you, O blessed, soared* to the love and beauty of God.* In them you now worthily delight,* ceaselessly rejoicing* together with your fellow ascetics,* O God-wise and venerable one.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O venerable Father John,* raising your mind in faith to God,* you disdained the instability of worldly turmoil.* Taking up your cross,* you followed the All-Seeing One,* subjecting the willful body to the mind* by the labors of discipline through the power of the Divine Spirit.

Glory (Tone 5): O venerable Father,* having heard the voice of the Gospel of the Lord, you forsook the world,* counting glory and riches as nothing.* From then on you called upon all:* Love God — and you will find eternal grace.* Set nothing above love for Him,* and when He comes in His glory,* you will find rest with all the saints.* By their prayers, O Christ, preserve and save our souls.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 8): The heavenly King, out of love for mankind, appeared on earth* and dwelt among men,* for taking flesh from the pure Virgin* and coming forth from her, He accepted the body.* He is one Son, two in nature but not in person.* Therefore, confessing Him perfectly as God and perfectly as man,* we profess Christ our God.* Entreat Him, O Mother undefiled,* that our souls may be granted mercy.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): You ascended the Cross, O Jesus, having descended from heaven.* You came to death, O immortal Life,* as the true Light for those in darkness,* as the Resurrection and illumination of all who had fallen.* O our Savior — glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

We glorify Christ, who rose from the dead.* He, taking on a soul and body,* separated them from each other in His Passion.* His most pure soul descended to Hades and despoiled it;* and in the tomb, His holy body knew no corruption.* O Deliverer of our souls!
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.
With psalms and songs we glorify, O Savior,* Your Resurrection from the dead,* by which You delivered us from the torments of Hades* and as God granted eternal life and great mercy.
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.

O unapproachable Master of all,* Creator of heaven and earth!* You suffered on the Cross and granted me dispassion.* Accepting burial and rising in glory,* You raised Adam with Your almighty hand.* Glory to Your Resurrection on the third day,* by which You gave us eternal life and cleansing from sins,* as the only Lover of mankind!

Glory (Tone 2): Let us glorify John —* an angel on earth and a man of God in heaven,* a great adornment of the world,* the delight of the merciful, the praise of fasting virtues.* Called to the Church of God,* he flourished in righteousness and, like a cedar in the desert,* multiplied the flock of Christ's rational sheep* in godliness and truth.

Now and ever: O new wonder surpassing all ancient wonders!* Who has known a mother who gave birth without a man* and carried in her arms Him who holds all creation?* What was born is the good pleasure of God,* whom you, O Most Pure, carried as a child in your arms* and had a mother's boldness toward Him.* Therefore cease not to entreat Him for those who honor you,* that He may have mercy and save our souls.

troparia.x: Troparia
(Tone 8): From on high You descended, O Merciful One; You accepted the three-day burial to free us from our passions; O Lord, our Life and Resurrection, glory to You.
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Turn not Your face from Your servant, for I am in distress; make haste to hear me. Look upon my soul and deliver it.
Verse: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to the grave.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 8): You, the King and Master,* the angels unceasingly hymn.* But I come to You as the Publican and cry:* O God, cleanse and have mercy on me!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
Being immortal, O my soul,* do not drown in the waves of this life,* but arise and cry to your Benefactor:* O God, cleanse and save me!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

When I weigh in my mind the multitude of evil deeds I have committed* and think about the fearful examination to come,* I come trembling to You, the God who loves mankind!* Therefore, despise me not, I pray, O only Sinless One.* Before the end comes, grant my wretched soul heartfelt contrition* and save me.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Grant me tears, O God, as once to the sinful woman,* and deem me worthy to wash Your feet —* those feet that freed me from the path of deception —* and to bring to You, as fragrant myrrh,* a pure life spent in repentance,* that I too may hear Your longed-for voice:* Your faith has saved you — go in peace!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.
Let us show, O faithful, great labors during the fast,* that we may attain the glory to come,* delivered by the mercy of the great God from the fire of Gehenna.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

The midpoint of the fast is now passing.* Let us clearly show the beginning of the divine life* and zealously strive to attain the goal of a righteous life,* that we may receive the joy that never passes.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The midpoint of the sacred path of fasting has already passed.* Let us strive joyfully for what is better,* anointing our souls with the oil of charity,* that we also may be deemed worthy to venerate the divine Passion of Christ our God* and to reach His glorious and holy Resurrection.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

A hidden mystery,* incomprehensible even to the Angels,* the Archangel Gabriel confirms.* He now comes to you,* the only Untouched and Good Dove,* the Calling of our race,* and cries to you, O All-Holy: Rejoice!* Prepare through the word to receive God the Word* in your womb!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

A light-bearing Palace* has been prepared for You, O Master —* the pure womb of the God-receiving Maiden.* Come to it and descend;* have pity on Your creation,* which the Evil One has seized through envy* and holds in bondage,* and which has lost its primordial beauty* and awaits Your saving descent.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

The Archangel Gabriel* will come to You, O All-Immaculate, and plainly cry to You:* Rejoice, O Deliverance from the curse,* Restoration of the fallen!* Rejoice, O only One who acquired divine election!* Rejoice, living Cloud of the Sun —* receive the Bodiless One who desires to dwell in Your womb!

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion): Still the wandering of my mind* and the roaming of my senses.* Completely blot out the traces of soul-destroying passions* and calm the turmoil of my soul, O Virgin,* that I may magnify Your goodness,* for I have You as the Protectress of my whole life, O Maiden,* and Helper in my infirmities.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7

(Tone 7): The Lord is near — He who planted the vineyard and called the laborers.* Come, O athletes of the fast, to receive your reward,* for the Employer is rich and merciful.* Even those who worked but little will receive the soul's favor.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Adam fell into the schemes of the tempter:* his mind was deceived, his soul wounded,* and he lay naked and unprotected.* Neither the priest of the Old Covenant heard him* nor the Levite, keeping the Law, looked at him.* Only You, O God — who came not from Samaria but from the Theotokos —* O Lord, glory to You!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Your martyrs, O Lord, did not deny You* nor depart from Your commandments.* By their prayers, have mercy on us.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 2, Forefeast): The mystery hidden from eternity is now revealed.* Today the Son of God becomes the Son of Man,* that, assuming the lesser, He may give us the greater.* Adam was once deceived:* he desired to become God and did not.* Now God becomes man to make Adam divine.* Let creation rejoice! Let nature be glad,* for the Archangel stands with awe before the Virgin* and brings her the greeting that dispels sorrow.* O our God, who in Your mercy became man — glory to You!

troparia.x: Troparia
The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)
Now and ever: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of sins. (Prostration)
Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
Teach me Your way, O Lord, that I may walk in Your truth.
Verse: Incline Your ear, O Lord, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 3
Verse: Guard me from the snare that they have set for me* and from the traps of evildoers.

(Tone 3): O Word of God, You gave us the time of fasting* that we might turn and live and never perish.* Grant us all, O Christ, to serve You well* and offer You sincere repentance,* as the prudent harlot once did,* who for myrrh and the shedding of warm tears* received forgiveness of sins.
Verse: Let the wicked fall into their own nets together,* while I pass safely by.

Like the blind man, I cry from the heart:* O Son of God, illumine the eyes of my heart!* Like the faithful Canaanite woman, I cry:* Have mercy on me, O Merciful One, for the demon of sensuality torments my soul.* Free it from the darkness of the passions* and make the rest of my life pure,* that I may glorify Your great mercy.
Verse: With my voice I cry to the Lord;* with my voice I make supplication to the Lord.

Today, brighter than the sun, the God-illumined grace of abstinence has shone upon us.* It enlightens our souls,* driving away the clouds of sinful passions.* Therefore let us all hasten to embrace it gladly,* that we may joyfully complete this divine feat.* Receiving it with gladness, let us cry out to Christ:* Sanctify, O Good One, those who faithfully accomplish it.
(From here, at each sticheron a prostration is made)
Verse: I pour out my complaint before Him;* I tell my trouble before Him.

All my life* has been squandered with harlots and publicans.* Can I at least in old age repent* of all that I have sinned?* O Builder of all and Physician of the sick,* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: When my spirit is faint within me, You know my path.* On the way where I walk they have hidden a snare for me.

The burden of sloth holds me;* I wallow in the mire,* shot by Belial's arrow,* and I defile the image within me.* We are heedless — You call us back; we sin — You deliver us.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Look to the right hand and see:* there is no one who takes notice of me.

I have become a stumbling-block to others,* doing as an earthling — earthly things.* I bound myself in marriage by Your decree —* yet transgressed it, defiling my bed.* You who formed me from the earth — do not despise Your creation.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: There is no place left for me to flee;* no one cares for my soul.

I have become an executioner of my own soul,* pandering to my flesh,* a laughingstock of the demons —* a slave of pleasures and indecencies.* By Your compassion, spare me, O Banisher of demons!* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: I cry to You, O Lord, and say:* You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.

By my own will I have sinned above all others;* therefore I have been forsaken.* I have the carnal mind as the adversary of my soul,* and it darkens me.* O Illumination of those in darkness and Guide of the lost,* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Give heed to my cry,* for I am brought very low.

My soul shall live, O Lord, said the prophet, and it shall praise You.* Find me, the lost sheep,* and number me with Your flock.* Give me time for repentance* that, groaning, I may cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Deliver me from my persecutors,* for they are stronger than I.

I have sinned, I have sinned,* rejecting Your commandments, O Christ God.* Be merciful to me, O Benefactor,* that I may see with my inner eyes* and flee the darkness, and cry out with fear:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

Wild beasts surround me.* Snatch me from their claws, O Master,* for You desire that all be saved* and come to the knowledge of truth, as the Creator.* Save me together with all!* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

Be a remedy to me, O Benefactor, Deliverer, my Savior,* and do not cast me off.* Behold — I lie in lawlessness;* raise me up, as the Almighty,* that I too may confess my deeds and cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

Having buried the talent given me,* like a foolish servant I buried it in the earth.* And condemned as worthless,* I dare not ask You further.* As the Merciful One, have pity on me, that I too may cry:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

O You who dried the fount of suffering* of the woman with the issue of blood when she touched the hem of Your garment,* grant me remission of my transgressions —* to me who flees to You with undoubting faith.* Receive me as You received her, and heal my affliction.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

You will sit upon the throne,* O You who by Your word made heaven and earth.* We shall all stand before You,* confessing our sins to You.* Before that Day, accept me in repentance.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Look with a merciful eye* and be gracious to me, O only Savior.* Grant streams of healing* to my poor and wretched soul.* Wash me from the filth of my deeds, that I may sing:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Belial has readied swords,* hastening to ensnare my humble soul.* He has made me a stranger to the illumination* and knowledge of Your face, O Merciful One.* You who are mighty in strength, pull me from his nets.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

I have become wholly a slave of the passions,* abandoning the Law and the Divine Scriptures.* Heal me wholly, O Benefactor,* who for my sake became as I am, O Good One.* Convert me, O Merciful One, O Cleanser of the passions.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The harlot wets with her tears* Your most pure and venerable feet,* calling all to hasten* and receive release from their sins.* Give me her faith, O Savior, that I may cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Cleanse the defilement of my soul,* O You who for my sake became poor and were an infant in the flesh.* Send down a drop of Your mercy* upon me, weak and wretched, O Christ.* Wash me from filth, heal the sick one.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.

Strengthen, O Master, my soul* to flee to You always and to serve You,* for You are my Shelter and Guardian,* my help and defense.* Grant me, O God the Word, to cry with boldness:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.

Be an immovable wall for us, O Jesus our Savior,* and O merciful God,* for by deceitful habits and deeds we have fallen.* But raise up Your creation, O Benefactor,* and be reconciled, O Merciful One.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.

I have become a Prodigal Son, squandering my wealth.* Now I waste away with hunger.* Under Your protection I flee:* Receive me as You received him, O good Father,* and make me worthy of Your table, that I may cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.

Through envy the originator of evil* deprived the first-created one of Paradise.* By saying "Remember me!" on the Tree, the thief regained Paradise.* And I, with faith and fear,* cry "Remember me!" to You.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.

Stretch out Your hand to me as to Peter,* and raise me from the deep, O God.* Grant me grace and mercy* by the prayer of the All-Immaculate Mother who bore You without seed* and of all Your Saints.* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!
Verse: Glory to You, O our God, glory to You.
Accept me, Your daily singer,* O Lamb who takes away my sin.* I surrender my soul and body wholly* into Your hands.* Night and day I thirstily cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!

Glory: O inexpressible is Your compassion,* O All-Good, Blameless Lord!* O Sinless and Merciful One, do not cast me from Your presence!* — that I too, in thanksgiving,* rejoicing and singing, may cry to You:* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!

Now and ever: O inexpressible condescension!* O the marvel of this unheard-of Nativity!* How the Virgin carries You as a Child in her embrace —* the Creator and God!* You who deigned to be incarnate of her, O Benefactor —* O Lord, before I utterly perish — save me!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 7

(Tone 7): Like the one who fell among thieves and was wounded,* so I too have fallen, wounded by my many sins.* To whom shall I turn to be healed?* Only to You, O Physician of souls and bodies.* Pour out upon me, O God, Your great mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

(Tone 4): Through the first sin of transgression, from the joy and delight of Paradise* we passed to a dishonorable life.* Stripped of virtues and a praiseworthy life by our transgressions,* we fell, as it were, among thieves.* Having sinned against Your saving teachings,* we became half-dead.* But we pray to You, the dispassionate Master, born of Mary,* who drew near to our sufferings:* Bind up our wounds caused by sin* and pour out upon us, as Lover of mankind, Your boundless mercy,* surrounding us with Your healing care.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Accept the endurance of the holy martyrs* and accept from us also a song, O Lover of mankind.* By their prayers grant us great mercy.
Glory, now and ever: Deliver us from our necessities,* O Mother of Christ God, who gave birth to the Creator of all,* that we all may cry to you:* Rejoice, O only guide of our souls!

troparia.x: Troparia
The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)
Now and ever: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of sins. (Prostration)
Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 4)
You have been our dwelling place in all generations, O Lord.
Verse: Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever You had formed the earth and the world.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament lessons for this feast are proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 2
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 2): We extol Your life-giving Cross, O Lord,* and the sacred sufferings of Your body for our sake:* the lance, the piercing,* the mockery, the spitting, the blows and slaps,* the purple robe and the crown of thorns,* by which You saved all from the curse.* Therefore we pray:* Grant us the grace to complete the time of fasting in peace.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Revealing the pre-eternal counsel,* Gabriel stood before you, O Virgin,* and greeting you, announced:* Rejoice, O unsown earth!* Rejoice, O unburnt Bush!* Rejoice, O unfathomable depth!* Rejoice, O Bridge leading to heaven!* Rejoice, O lofty Ladder that Jacob saw!* Rejoice, O divine vessel of manna!* Rejoice, O deliverance from the curse!* Rejoice, O restoration of Adam,* for the Lord is with you!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Revealing the pre-eternal counsel,* Gabriel stood before you, O Virgin,* and greeting you, announced:* Rejoice, O unsown earth!* Rejoice, O unburnt Bush!* Rejoice, O unfathomable depth!* Rejoice, O Bridge leading to heaven!* Rejoice, O lofty Ladder that Jacob saw!* Rejoice, O divine vessel of manna!* Rejoice, O deliverance from the curse!* Rejoice, O restoration of Adam,* for the Lord is with you!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

You have appeared to me as a man,* says the Immaculate Virgin to the Chief Commander.* How then do you speak words beyond human understanding?* For you have said that God is with me and will dwell in my womb.* Tell me: How shall I become so spacious a dwelling* and a place of holiness surpassing the Cherubim?* Do not deceive me with trickery,* for I have not known pleasure, nor have I entered into wedlock.* How then shall I bear a child?
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

You have appeared to me as a man,* says the Immaculate Virgin to the Chief Commander.* How then do you speak words beyond human understanding?* For you have said that God is with me and will dwell in my womb.* Tell me: How shall I become so spacious a dwelling* and a place of holiness surpassing the Cherubim?* Do not deceive me with trickery,* for I have not known pleasure, nor have I entered into wedlock.* How then shall I bear a child?
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

When God wills, the order of nature is overcome,* says the Bodiless One,* and something beyond human understanding comes to pass.* Believe my truthful words, O All-Holy and Most Immaculate!* And she replied: Let it be to me according to your word,* and I shall bear the Bodiless One* who shall take flesh from me,* that by this union He, as the only Mighty One,* may lead mankind back to its original dignity.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): From heaven was sent the Archangel Gabriel* to announce to the Virgin the conception.* Coming to Nazareth,* he pondered within himself, marveling at the wonder:* How is the Incomprehensible One, dwelling on high, born of a Virgin?* He who has heaven for His throne and the earth for His footstool* is contained in the womb of a maiden?* He upon whom the six-winged and many-eyed ones cannot gaze* has, by a single word, consented to be incarnate of her.* It is God's Word who is here present!* Why then do I stand and not say to the Maiden:* Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with you!* Rejoice, O Pure Virgin!* Rejoice, O Bride Unwedded!* Rejoice, O Mother of Life —* blessed is the Fruit of your womb!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 5

(Tone 5): Through the passions, my wretched soul* left the right path of Yours and fell into a pit.* The Levite and the priest, seeing me, passed by.* But You, O Christ, had mercy on me.* By the weapon of Your Cross, You destroyed the bond of sin,* illumined me with dispassion, and seated me beside the Father.* Therefore I cry to You:* O unapproachable Lord — glory to You!

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

(Tone 8): As from Jerusalem, we departed, leaving the road of Your commandments* written for all nations,* and went to Jericho, surrendering ourselves to lusts,* like those inhabitants whom You condemned for their wickedness.* Through disobedience we fell into soul-destroying passions, as among thieves.* They wounded us and left us half-dead.* But You, O Lord, who for the sake of human sins* willingly allowed Your body to be pierced by nails and the lance,* and in Jerusalem accomplished salvation for all by the Cross — have mercy on us.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.

Your martyrs, O Lord, forgetting all that pertains to this life,* disregarded torments for the sake of the life to come.* Therefore they became its heirs and rejoice with the angels.* By their prayers, grant Your people great mercy.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 4): Today is the announcement of joy,* the feast of virginity.* The earthly is joined with the heavenly.* Adam is renewed,* Eve is freed from her primordial sorrow,* and the tabernacle of our nature,* by the deification of the assumed union,* has become a Temple of God.* O mystery!* The manner of His self-emptying is unknown,* the manner of His conception is invisible.* An Angel serves the wonder,* a Virgin womb receives the Son.* The Holy Spirit is sent down,* the Father on high gives His blessing,* and by their common will the reconciliation is accomplished.* In it and through it, having been saved,* let us cry with Gabriel to the Virgin:* Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with you!* From you comes salvation — Christ our God,* who assumed our nature and raised it to Himself.* Entreat Him for the salvation of our souls!

troparia.x: Troparia

(Tone 4): Today is the beginning of our salvation and the revelation of the eternal mystery: the Son of God becomes the Son of the Virgin, and Gabriel proclaims the good news of grace. Let us also cry out with him to the Theotokos: Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with you!
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
Priest:
May Christ our true God, through the prayers of His most pure Mother, whose holy Annunciation we celebrate, and of all the saints: have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loves mankind.
Amen.

prokeimenon.x: Prokeimenon (Tone 8)
O God, arise; judge the earth, for You shall inherit all the nations.
Verse: O God, do not keep silence; do not hold Your peace or be still, O God.

readings.x: The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.
The appointed Old Testament reading is proclaimed.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 8
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.

(Tone 8): By my thoughts I fell among thieves,* and I, the wretched one, was robbed of my reason and dreadfully beaten.* My whole soul is grievously wounded, and I, stripped of virtues, lie on the road of life.* The priest, seeing me suffering from incurable wounds, disdainfully passed by.* The Levite, too, glancing at my painful agony, walked on the other side.* But You, O my merciful Christ God,* who came not from Samaria but took flesh from Mary,* grant me healing from Your love of mankind,* pouring out upon me Your great mercy.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.

If there is any virtue and if there is any praise —* it belongs to the Saints!* For they bowed their necks beneath the sword* for You, who bowed the heavens and descended.* They shed their blood* for You, who emptied Yourself and took the form of a servant.* They humbled themselves even unto death,* imitating Your poverty.* By their prayers, according to the multitude of Your mercies,* O God, have mercy on us.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

(Tone 8, Podoben): O Lord, You showed Your sacred disciples* to be eloquent heavens.* By their sacred intercession,* deliver me from earthly evils,* ever raising my thought by the temperance of the mind* to Your Passion,* as the Merciful and Lover of mankind.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

All having this time of fasting* which cooperates with divine beneficence,* let us weep with all our hearts* and cry to the Savior:* For the sake of Your disciples, O Lord most merciful,* save those who with awe hymn* Your surpassing love for mankind.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 8): O all-praised Apostles, intercessors for the world,* healers of the sick, guardians of health,* protect us on all sides as we pass through the time of fasting,* in divine peace with one another,* keeping our minds undisturbed by the passions,* that we all may sing a hymn to the risen Christ* as to the Victor.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

In the sixth month the Archangel was sent to the Pure Virgin.* He spoke to her and told her to rejoice,* and announced that the Deliverer would come from her.* She, receiving the greeting, conceived You, the Pre-Eternal God,* who ineffably deigned to become man* for the salvation of our souls.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

The Theotokos heard a strange language* when the Archangel spoke the words of the Annunciation.* She conceived You, the Pre-Eternal God,* as soon as she accepted the greeting with faith.* Therefore we also, rejoicing, cry to You:* O God unchanged, who took flesh from her,* grant peace to the world and great mercy to our souls!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

This is today our Annunciation:* Beyond all understanding, God is united with man!* The voice of the Archangel drives away doubt,* for the Virgin receives joy;* the earthly becomes heavenly* and the world is freed from the ancient curse.* Therefore let creation rejoice, singing:* O our Creator and Savior, O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The great Gabriel, seer of God,* who brings light and salvation,* beholds the thrice-radiant splendor* and, together with the heavenly choirs, hymns the divine majesty!* He prays* to grant our souls peace* and great mercy.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

A great mystery, unknown before to the Angels* and guarded from the ages,* was entrusted to you alone, O Gabriel.* Coming to Nazareth, you delivered it to the only Pure One.* Therefore pray with her that we be granted peace* and great mercy.
Verse: You make Your angels spirits* and Your ministers a flaming fire.

You, ever full of light,* fulfilling the will and executing the commands of the Almighty,* O wondrous Archangel Gabriel!* Save those who lovingly honor you,* ever entreating* that our souls be granted peace* and great mercy.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 6): From heaven was sent the Archangel Gabriel* to announce to the Virgin the conception.* Coming to Nazareth, he pondered, marveling at the wonder:* How is the Incomprehensible One, dwelling on high, born of a Virgin?* He who has heaven for His throne and earth for His footstool —* how is He contained in a Virgin's womb?* He upon whom the six-winged and many-eyed cannot gaze —* by a single word He consented to take flesh from her!* It is God's Word who is here!* Why then do I stand and not say to the Maiden:* Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with You!* Rejoice, O Pure Virgin! Rejoice, O Bride Unwedded!* Rejoice, O Mother of Life — blessed is the Fruit of your womb!
