stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 1, Podoben: "O all-praised martyrs"): O Lord, nailed to the Cross!* As You destroyed Adam's bond by Your divine lance,* so destroy, O Word, my chains,* that with joy and faith I may offer You a sacrifice of praise* during this favorable time* which You have shown to all for salvation.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Moses, illumined of old by the light of fasting,* beheld the glory of God.* Imitate him, O my wretched soul,* and by your deeds, abstinence, and prayer, please Him* who out of love for you stretched out His hands upon the Cross,* that you may receive divine delight.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

(Tone 6): We venerate Your life-giving Cross,* on which You showed us, O Christ, Your ineffable and inexpressible goodness.* Having illumined our souls by it, we unceasingly hymn You and ask:* Grant us the grace to bravely and joyfully complete the time of the fasting struggle* and to reach Your Passion, O Lord,* by which You saved us.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

Against the contempt of your mistress* and her merciless frenzy,* you set a manly and wise resolve,* foreseeing, O God-wise one, the delight of future blessings* that endures forever,* unchanged through the ages.* You received it, having departed from earth* to the heavenly chambers* and to the choir whose song never ceases, O glorious one.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

The majesty and flourishing beauty* of the Kingdom of your Bridegroom* you were deemed worthy to behold,* adorned with the stigmata* of your steadfast suffering.* And you rightly drew near to the Fountain of good things,* from which you were richly enriched with the fruit of God-inspired gladness, O blessed one,* and with immortal good renown.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Neither the yoke of slavery* nor the weakness of womanhood,* neither hunger nor beatings* restrained you from the imitation of martyric endurance, O all-glorious one!* For out of the fervor of your soul* you endured the torments.* Therefore you received the heavenly bridal chamber* and were adorned with the crown of graces,* standing before your Creator.

Glory, now and ever (Cross-Theotokion): The sun and the moon beheld You, O Lover of mankind,* hung upon the tree, O Sun of Righteousness, O my Christ,* and hid their light.* The foundations of the earth trembled with fear before Your power,* and Your Mother, in anguish of heart, cried to You:* O sweetest Jesus — glory to Your goodness!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 8

(Tone 8): By my first transgression I willingly stripped myself of the beauty of virtues,* but through Your mercy toward me, O Word of God,* I clothed myself in it again.* For You did not despise me, though I fell into the passions* and was cruelly beaten by the robbers,* but You rescued me by Your all-powerful might* and deemed me worthy, O Most Merciful, of Your mercy.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

By my first transgression I willingly stripped myself of the beauty of virtues,* but through Your mercy toward me, O Word of God,* I clothed myself in it again.* For You did not despise me, though I fell into the passions* and was cruelly beaten by the robbers,* but You rescued me by Your all-powerful might* and deemed me worthy, O Most Merciful, of Your mercy.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
O martyrs of the Lord,* you sanctify every place* and heal every malady.* Now intercede* that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy, we pray.

Glory, now and ever: The spotless Ewe-Lamb,* beholding the Lamb willingly raised upon the tree,* cried out lamenting:* Woe is me, O my most beloved Son!* What has the lawless assembly done to You,* wishing to orphan me, O Dearest One?

The Lord is merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 6, Podoben: "Having placed all their hope"): Revealing the pre-eternal counsel,* Gabriel stood before you, O Virgin,* and greeting you, announced:* Rejoice, O unsown earth!* Rejoice, O unburnt Bush!* Rejoice, O unfathomable depth!* Rejoice, O Bridge leading to heaven!* Rejoice, O lofty Ladder that Jacob saw!* Rejoice, O divine vessel of manna!* Rejoice, O deliverance from the curse!* Rejoice, O restoration of Adam,* for the Lord is with you!
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Revealing the pre-eternal counsel,* Gabriel stood before you, O Virgin,* and greeting you, announced:* Rejoice, O unsown earth!* Rejoice, O unburnt Bush!* Rejoice, O unfathomable depth!* Rejoice, O Bridge leading to heaven!* Rejoice, O lofty Ladder that Jacob saw!* Rejoice, O divine vessel of manna!* Rejoice, O deliverance from the curse!* Rejoice, O restoration of Adam,* for the Lord is with you!
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

You have appeared to me as a man,* says the Immaculate Virgin to the Chief Commander.* How then do you speak words beyond human understanding?* You have said that God is with me and will dwell in my womb.* Tell me: How shall I become so spacious a dwelling* and a place of holiness surpassing the Cherubim?* Do not deceive me with trickery,* for I have not known pleasure, nor entered into wedlock.* How then shall I bear a child?
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

You have appeared to me as a man,* says the Immaculate Virgin to the Chief Commander.* How then do you speak words beyond human understanding?* You have said that God is with me and will dwell in my womb.* Tell me: How shall I become so spacious a dwelling* and a place of holiness surpassing the Cherubim?* Do not deceive me with trickery,* for I have not known pleasure, nor entered into wedlock.* How then shall I bear a child?
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

When God wills, the order of nature is surpassed,* says the Bodiless One,* and something beyond human understanding comes to pass.* Believe my truthful words, O All-Holy and Most Immaculate!* She replied: Let it be to me according to your word,* and I shall bear the Bodiless One* who shall take flesh from me,* that by this union He, as the only Mighty One,* may lead mankind back to its original dignity.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

When God wills, the order of nature is surpassed,* says the Bodiless One,* and something beyond human understanding comes to pass.* Believe my truthful words, O All-Holy and Most Immaculate!* She replied: Let it be to me according to your word,* and I shall bear the Bodiless One* who shall take flesh from me,* that by this union He, as the only Mighty One,* may lead mankind back to its original dignity.

Glory, now and ever (Tone 2): The mystery hidden from eternity is now revealed.* Today the Son of God becomes the Son of Man,* that, assuming the lesser, He may give us the greater.* Adam was once deceived: he desired to become God and did not.* Now God becomes man to make Adam divine.* Let creation rejoice! Let nature be glad,* for the Archangel stands with awe before the Virgin* and brings her the greeting that dispels sorrow.* O our God, who in Your mercy became man — glory to You!

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 6

(Tone 6): As from Jerusalem, my soul has departed* and come to the passionate Jericho,* lured by the false glory of life's cares.* Falling into the robbers' schemes,* they stripped from it the filial garment of grace.* Covered with wounds, it lies as though dead.* The priest passed by, saw it wounded, and paid no attention;* the Levite disdainfully turned away from it.* But You, O Lord, who ineffably took flesh from the Virgin,* willingly poured out blood and water from Your saving side,* like oil, upon the sores of my wounds.* With their balm bind my wounds, O Christ God,* and count me among the heavenly choir, as the Merciful One.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

As from Jerusalem, my soul has departed* and come to the passionate Jericho,* lured by the false glory of life's cares.* Falling into the robbers' schemes,* they stripped from it the filial garment of grace.* Covered with wounds, it lies as though dead.* The priest passed by, saw it wounded, and paid no attention;* the Levite disdainfully turned away from it.* But You, O Lord, who ineffably took flesh from the Virgin,* willingly poured out blood and water from Your saving side,* like oil, upon the sores of my wounds.* With their balm bind my wounds, O Christ God,* and count me among the heavenly choir, as the Merciful One.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Your martyrs, O Lord, did not deny You* nor depart from Your commandments.* By their prayers, have mercy on us.
Glory, now and ever: By the prayers, O Christ, of Your Mother,* of Your martyrs, apostles, prophets, and hierarchs,* and of the venerable and the righteous, grant rest to Your departed servants.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 1): Accept our evening prayers,* O holy Lord,* and grant us the forgiveness of sins,* for You alone* have shown the world the Resurrection.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
Encircle Zion, O peoples, and surround it,* and give glory in it to Him who rose from the dead,* for He is our God* who has delivered us from our transgressions.
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.
Come, O peoples, let us sing and worship Christ,* glorifying His Resurrection from the dead,* for He is our God* who has delivered the world from the deception of the enemy.
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.

Be glad, O heavens; let the foundations of the earth resound!* O mountains, sing with gladness!* For behold, Emmanuel has nailed our sins to the Cross.* The Giver of life has slain death* and raised Adam, as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

Let us hymn Him who was willingly crucified for us,* who suffered and was buried* and rose from the dead,* saying to Him:* O Christ, strengthen Your Church in Orthodoxy* and pacify our life,* for You are gracious and the Lover of mankind.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Standing before Your life-receiving tomb,* we, the unworthy, send up glory to Your ineffable compassion, O Christ our God.* For You, the Sinless One,* accepted suffering and death that, as the Lover of mankind,* You might grant the world the Resurrection.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Let us hymn with the Father His eternal and beginningless Word,* who ineffably came forth from the Virgin's womb,* and willingly accepted the Cross and death and rose in glory.* Let us say to Him: O Lord, Giver of life,* Savior of our souls — glory to You!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

The defilement of your former harlotry* barred you from beholding the holy place.* Yet your feeling and God-given reason,* and the weight on your conscience from all you had done,* brought you to conversion.* Looking upon the icon of the Blessed Virgin,* and acknowledging all your former falls, O all-praised one,* with boldness you venerated* the precious Tree.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

Having joyfully venerated* the holy places,* you received in them the saving provision of virtue* and boldly set out on the good path.* Crossing the stream of the Jordan,* you eagerly settled in the dwelling of the Baptist,* and by your way of life you bridled the fury of the passions,* exhausting the stirrings of the flesh by the austerity of your body,* O ever-memorable Mother.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

Having settled in the desert,* you piously destroyed the passionate images within yourself,* inscribing the image of the likeness of God* upon your soul with a variety of virtues.* And you shone so brightly* that you walked lightly upon the waters* and rose from the earth,* offering prayers to God.* Now, standing with boldness before Christ,* O all-glorious Mary,* pray for our souls.

Glory (Tone 4): The power of Your Cross, O Christ,* worked a miracle,* for even the former harlot, taking upon herself the struggle of fasting,* from that time cast off the weakness of nature* and bravely stood against the devil.* Therefore, having the honor of victory,* she now prays for our souls.

Now and ever (Dogmatikon, Tone 1): Let us praise Mary the Virgin,* glory of the whole world, who came forth from mankind* and gave birth to the Master,* the gate of heaven, the praise of the bodiless ones, and the adornment of the faithful.* For she was revealed as heaven and the temple of the Godhead.* She destroyed the middle wall of enmity,* established peace, and opened the Kingdom.* Therefore, holding her fast as the anchor of faith,* we have as our champion the Lord born of her.* Courage then, courage, O people of God!* For He will fight our foes, He the Almighty.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1
(Tone 1): By Your Passion, O Christ,* we have been freed from suffering,* and by Your Resurrection we have been delivered from corruption.* O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: The Lord reigns;* He is robed in majesty.

Let creation rejoice, let the heavens be glad,* and let the nations clap their hands with joy.* For Christ our Savior has nailed our sins to the Cross* and, having slain death, has given us life,* raising fallen Adam, as the Lover of mankind.
Verse: For He has established the world,* and it shall not be shaken.

Being the unapproachable King of heaven and earth,* out of Your love for mankind You were willingly crucified.* Hades, encountering You below, was embittered;* the souls of the righteous, receiving You, rejoiced.* Adam, seeing You, the Creator, in the netherworld, rose up.* O wonder! How has the Life of all tasted death?* Having willed to enlighten the world that cries:* O You who rose from the dead, O Lord — glory to You!
Verse: Holiness befits Your house, O Lord,* for length of days.

The myrrh-bearing women, carrying myrrh,* hastened with weeping to Your tomb.* Not finding Your most pure body,* they learned from the angel the new and most glorious wonder.* They told the Apostles:* The Lord is risen, granting the world great mercy.

Glory (Tone 2): You cut through the snares of the soul and the passions of the flesh* with the sword of abstinence,* and mortified the transgressions of the mind* by the silence of fasting.* With the streams of your tears you watered the whole desert* and brought forth for us the fruits of repentance.* Therefore, O Venerable One, we celebrate your memory.

Now and ever: O new wonder surpassing all ancient wonders!* Who has known a mother who gave birth without a man* and carried in her arms Him who holds all creation?* What was born is the good pleasure of God,* whom you, O Most Pure, carried as a child in your arms* and had a mother's boldness toward Him.* Therefore cease not to entreat Him for those who honor you,* that He may have mercy and save our souls.

(Tone 1): O Christ our God, when You raised Lazarus from the dead before Your Passion, You confirmed the resurrection of all. Wherefore, we, like the children bearing the symbols of victory, cry out to You, O Vanquisher of death: Hosanna in the highest! Blessed is He that comes in the name of the Lord.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 1
Verse: Lead my soul out of prison* that I may give thanks to Your name.
(Tone 1): Great is the depth of my transgressions, O Savior,* and I am drowning in my sins.* But give me Your hand, as to Peter,* and have mercy and save me, O God.
Verse: The righteous will surround me,* for You will deal bountifully with me.
By our wicked thoughts and deeds we have condemned ourselves, O Savior.* Give us, O God, the mind of conversion,* that we may cry to You: O good Benefactor,* save us and have mercy!
Verse: From the depths I cry to You, O Lord;* O Lord, hear my voice.

Another world awaits you, O my soul,* and a Judge will lay bare your hidden and foul deeds.* Therefore do not linger in temporal things,* but hasten before the Judge and say:* O God, cleanse me and save me!
Verse: Let Your ears be attentive* to the voice of my supplication.
Do not despise us, O Savior,* held captive by sinful sloth.* Raise our thoughts to repentance* and make us keen workers of Your vineyard.* Grant us the reward of the eleventh hour* and great mercy.
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

Being rich, O Christ, You became poor* to enrich mankind with immortality and radiance.* Therefore enrich us also, impoverished by the pleasures of life,* with virtues, and number us with poor Lazarus,* rescuing us from the torment of the rich man and from the Gehenna* that awaits us.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

We, O Lord, have grown exceedingly rich in malice,* loving indulgence* and experiencing the pleasures of life,* and we have become liable to the fire of Gehenna.* As to the despised Lazarus, be merciful, O Master,* to us also who hunger in mind* and are cast before the gates* of divine works.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Eagerly beginning the sixth week of the God-pleasing fast,* let us, O faithful, offer a pre-festal hymn of Palm Sunday to the Lord,* who comes in glory to Jerusalem* to put death to death by the power of His Divinity.* Therefore let us piously prepare for Him the signs of victory — the branches of virtue —* singing to the Creator of all: Hosanna!
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O venerable Father John,* you ever truly carried the praises of God in your heart,* learning the power of God-inspired words, O all-wise one.* Enriched by the grace flowing from them,* you became blessed,* overcoming all the evil designs of the impious.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O venerable and glorified Father John,* cleansing your soul with streams of tears* and appeasing God by all-night vigils,* you soared, O blessed one,* to the love and beauty of God.* In them you now worthily delight, ceaselessly rejoicing,* with your fellow ascetics,* O God-wise and venerable one.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O venerable and glorified Father John,* raising your mind in faith to God,* you disdained the inconstancy of worldly turmoil.* Taking up your cross,* you followed the All-Seeing One,* subjecting the unruly body to the mind by the labors of discipline* through the power of the Divine Spirit.

Now and ever (Theotokion): Come, O my soul, lamenting,* and bringing streams of tears from the heart,* cry out to the Virgin and Mother of our God:* By the multitude of Your mercies, O Pure One,* deliver me from dreadful torments* and settle me where there is rest* and everlasting joy and delight.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 1

(Tone 1): Wondrous is the Savior's merciful conduct toward us.* Knowing the future as the present,* He set before us the lives of Lazarus and the Rich Man.* Having pondered the death of both, let us flee the self-love and lack of humanity of the latter* and imitate the patience and magnanimity of the former,* that we too, with him in the bosom of Abraham, may cry:* O righteous Judge — O Lord, glory to You!

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Wondrous is the Savior's merciful conduct toward us.* Knowing the future as the present,* He set before us the lives of Lazarus and the Rich Man.* Having pondered the death of both, let us flee the self-love and lack of humanity of the latter* and imitate the patience and magnanimity of the former,* that we too, with him in the bosom of Abraham, may cry:* O righteous Judge — O Lord, glory to You!
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
By the prayers of all the Saints and the Theotokos,* grant us, O Lord, Your peace, and have mercy on us,* as the only Merciful One.
Glory, now and ever: O Joy of the heavenly choirs* and mighty helper of mankind on earth,* O Most Pure Virgin!* Save us who flee to you,* for in you, O Theotokos,* we have placed our hope after God.

Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good; His steadfast love endures forever.

stichera.x: Stichera on "Lord I Have Cried" — Tone 6
Verse: If You, O Lord, should mark iniquities, who could stand, O Lord?* But with You there is forgiveness.

(Tone 6, Podoben: "Having placed all their hope"): In my wretchedness I have weakened my soul* with many transgressions and the pleasures of life,* and now I lie constantly on the bed of sloth.* To You, O Master, I cry:* Mercifully come and visit me,* granting me health and mercy.* Do not abandon me, lest my soul fall into the sleep of death* and the enemy rejoice at my perdition,* for he, O Savior, always drags me into the abyss of Hades.
Verse: For Your name's sake I wait for You, O Lord;* my soul hopes in Your word; my soul hopes in the Lord.

Imitating, in my folly, the merciless Rich Man,* I feasted luxuriously,* indulging in sensuality and the passions.* Seeing my reason,* which, like Lazarus, constantly lay at the door of repentance,* I passed by it indifferently,* leaving it hungry,* sick, and covered with the sores of the passions.* Therefore I deserve condemnation to the fire of Gehenna.* But You, O Master, deliver me from it,* as the only Most Merciful.
Verse: From the morning watch until night,* from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.

Passing beyond the Jordan,* You, O Lord, foretold that the sickness of Lazarus would not be unto death,* but for the glory of You, our God.* Glory to the greatness of Your works and to Your almighty power,* for You, O Lover of mankind, by Your great mercy have conquered death.
Verse: For with the Lord there is mercy and with Him is plenteous redemption;* He will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

O God-wise Father Hypatius!* Well trained by abstinence,* you ascended to the glorious height of virtues,* clearly rising* to the all-mysterious vision of God,* beholding the beauty of Christ.* Enlightening your mind and thought with His radiance,* you illumined all* with the rays of miracles.
Verse: Praise the Lord, all nations!* Glorify Him, all peoples!

O Father of Fathers, Hypatius!* Illumined by the light of Orthodoxy,* you enlightened the thoughts of those who came to you with faith,* O venerable one.* Darkening the eyes of Arius,* you separated him from the Church of Christ.* Therefore, having acquired you as a luminary,* we all honor and bless your sacred memory.
Verse: For great is His mercy toward us,* and the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.

O sacred Father Hypatius!* Having Christ the King, whom you clearly proclaimed* as consubstantial with the Father,* in your heart,* you shone with the rays of miracles,* leading the world under the sun to the Light.* You slew the serpent* and by your prayers caused a stream of warm waters to flow* for the healing of the passions.

Glory, now and ever (Theotokion, Tone 8): O vessel of virginity* and pure, divine, and beautiful dwelling of God,* I pray to You, O Pure One:* Console my soul,* poisoned by the venom of the serpent* and put to death by sins,* that I may glorify You,* who have exalted all the faithful,* O Favored One of God.

aposticha.x: Stichera at the Aposticha — Tone 4

(Tone 4): Deliver us, O Savior, from the soul-destroying greed,* and with wretched Lazarus, number us among those in the bosom of Abraham.* Being rich in mercy,* You willingly became poor for our sake* and led us from corruption to incorruption,* for You are the merciful and man-loving God.

Verse: To You I lift up my eyes, to You who dwell in heaven. As the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, and the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress,* so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until He has mercy upon us.

Deliver us, O Savior, from the soul-destroying greed,* and with wretched Lazarus, number us among those in the bosom of Abraham.* Being rich in mercy,* You willingly became poor for our sake* and led us from corruption to incorruption,* for You are the merciful and man-loving God.
Verse: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we have had our fill of contempt.* Our soul has been filled with the scorn of the prosperous and the contempt of the proud.
Accept the endurance of the holy martyrs* and accept from us also a song, O Lover of mankind.* By their prayers grant us great mercy.
Glory, now and ever: Deliver us from our necessities,* O Mother of Christ God, who gave birth to the Creator of all,* that we all may cry to you:* Rejoice, O only guide of our souls!

troparia.x: Troparia
The troparia for Great Lent weekdays:

(Tone 1): Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, Mary full of grace, the Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb, for you have borne the Savior of our souls. (Prostration)
Glory: O Baptist of Christ, remember us all, that we may be delivered from our iniquities, for to you is given the grace to pray for us. (Prostration)
Now and ever: O holy Apostles, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls forgiveness of sins. (Prostration)
Beneath your compassion we take refuge, O Theotokos. Do not despise our prayers in our necessities, but deliver us from danger, O only pure and only blessed one. (Without prostration)
Lord, have mercy. (40x) Lord, bless.
Priest:
Blessed is Christ our God, always, now and ever, and forever.
Amen.
More honorable than the Cherubim and beyond compare more glorious than the Seraphim; without corruption you gave birth to God the Word; true Mother of God, we magnify you.
Glory to You, O Christ our God, our hope, glory to You.
Glory... Now and ever... Lord, have mercy. (3x) Father, bless.
